      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "distractors": [],
      "short_explanation": "White blood cells have many lysosomes to destroy pathogens."
    },
    {
      "id": "Cell Specialization Fun Facts 010",
      "distractors": [],
      "long_explanation": "These cells form a barrier and absorb nutrients efficiently due to finger-like extensions called microvilli.",
      "question": "Which type of cell forms the lining of the digestive tract and often has microvilli for absorption?",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "answers": [
        "Neurons",
        "Epithelial cells",
        "Cardiac cells",
        "Adipocytes"
      ],
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.460Z",
      "clinical_application": "Massage of abdominal regions may enhance parasympathetic activity, improving gut function and absorption.",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.115Z",
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_cells",
        "system_digestive",
        "digestive_absorption"
      ],
      "unit": "cell specialization fun facts",
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "keywords": [
        "anatomy_cells",
        "system_digestive",
        "digestive_absorption"
      ],
      "short_explanation": "Epithelial cells with microvilli increase nutrient absorption.",
      "correct": 1,
      "topic": "cell_theory"
    },
    {
      "id": "Cellular Anatomy 001",
      "distractors": [],
      "long_explanation": "The cell membrane is a semipermeable barrier that surrounds the cell, allowing selective transport of nutrients, ions, and waste. It also supports communication and recognition through membrane proteins and receptors.",
      "question": "What is the function of the cell membrane?",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "answers": [
        "Stores genetic information",
        "Produces energy",
        "Controls movement of substances in and out of the cell",
        "Synthesizes proteins"
      ],
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.457Z",
      "clinical_application": "Damage to the cell membrane, as seen in trauma or infection, can lead to loss of homeostasis and cell death. This is crucial in understanding inflammation and immune responses.",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.109Z",
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_membrane",
        "anatomy_cells",
        "anatomy_protein"
      ],
      "unit": "cellular anatomy",
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "keywords": [
        "anatomy_membrane",
        "anatomy_cells",
        "anatomy_protein"
      ],
      "short_explanation": "It controls the movement of substances into and out of the cell.",
      "correct": 2,
      "topic": "cell_theory"
    },
    {
      "id": "Cellular Anatomy 002",
      "clinical_application": "Mitochondrial dysfunction is linked to neurodegenerative diseases and metabolic syndromes, making it a focus in aging and chronic disease research.",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.457Z",
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "keywords": [
        "anatomy_nucleus",
        "anatomy_organelle",
        "anatomy_ribosomes"
      ],
      "correct": 2,
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.109Z",
      "unit": "cellular anatomy",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "distractors": [],
      "long_explanation": "Mitochondria convert glucose and oxygen into ATP through cellular respiration. They also regulate apoptosis (programmed cell death) and play roles in calcium signaling and metabolism.",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_nucleus",
        "anatomy_organelle",
        "anatomy_ribosomes"
      ],
      "question": "Which organelle is known as the 'powerhouse' of the cell?",
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "answers": [
        "Nucleus",
        "Ribosome",
        "Mitochondrion",
        "Golgi apparatus"
      ],
      "short_explanation": "The mitochondrion generates energy in the form of ATP."
    },
    {
      "id": "Cellular Anatomy 003",
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "long_explanation": "The nucleus is a membrane-bound structure that stores DNA and coordinates activities like growth, protein synthesis, and cell division. It contains the nucleolus and chromatin.",
      "clinical_application": "Mutations in nuclear DNA can cause genetic disorders and cancers. Nuclear changes are key diagnostic indicators in pathology.",
      "short_explanation": "The nucleus houses and protects the cell’s DNA.",
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "keywords": [
        "anatomy_nucleus",
        "anatomy_genetic_material"
      ],
      "correct": 2,
      "question": "Which structure contains the cell’s genetic material?",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "distractors": [],
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.109Z",
      "unit": "cellular anatomy",
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_nucleus",
        "anatomy_genetic_material"
      ],
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "answers": [
        "Endoplasmic reticulum",
        "Golgi body",
        "Nucleus",
        "Lysosome"
      ],
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.457Z"
    },
    {
      "id": "Cellular Anatomy 004",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "long_explanation": "The rough ER is studded with ribosomes and modifies newly synthesized proteins, while the smooth ER synthesizes lipids and detoxifies chemicals. Together, they act as the cell’s manufacturing and shipping center.",
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "clinical_application": "ER stress is implicated in diseases like diabetes, Alzheimer’s, and cancer due to its role in protein folding and cellular stress responses.",
      "short_explanation": "The ER processes and transports proteins and lipids.",
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "keywords": [
        "anatomy_protein",
        "anatomy_ribosomes"
      ],
      "correct": 2,
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "question": "What is the primary function of ribosomes?",
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "distractors": [],
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.109Z",
      "unit": "cellular anatomy",
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_protein",
        "anatomy_ribosomes"
      ],
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "answers": [
        "Break down waste",
        "Transport lipids",
        "Produce proteins",
        "Store water"
      ],
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.457Z"
    },
    {
      "id": "Cellular Anatomy 005",
      "unit": "cellular anatomy",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "clinical_application": "Antibiotics often target bacterial ribosomes. Ribosomal defects in humans can lead to disorders like Diamond-Blackfan anemia and some cancers.",
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_protein"
      ],
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "answers": [
        "Synthesizing proteins",
        "Breaking down cellular waste and debris",
        "Transporting RNA",
        "Generating energy"
      ],
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.109Z",
      "correct": 1,
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "keywords": [
        "anatomy_protein"
      ],
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.457Z",
      "long_explanation": "Ribosomes link amino acids into polypeptides by reading messenger RNA instructions. They may float freely or attach to the ER, supporting growth, repair, and cell signaling.",
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "question": "What is the role of lysosomes in a cell?",
      "distractors": [],
      "short_explanation": "Ribosomes synthesize proteins by translating mRNA."
    },
    {
      "id": "Cellular Anatomy 006",
      "clinical_application": "Disruption in Golgi function affects protein trafficking, contributing to diseases like congenital disorders of glycosylation (CDG).",
      "short_explanation": "The Golgi apparatus modifies, sorts, and ships cellular products.",
      "unit": "cellular anatomy",
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_protein",
        "anatomy_dna",
        "biochemistry_glucose"
      ],
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "answers": [
        "Stores calcium and lipids",
        "Produces glucose",
        "Processes and packages proteins and lipids",
        "Synthesizes DNA"
      ],
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.109Z",
      "long_explanation": "The Golgi receives vesicles from the ER, modifies proteins and lipids (e.g., glycosylation), and packages them into vesicles for transport within or outside the cell.",
      "correct": 2,
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "keywords": [
        "anatomy_protein",
        "anatomy_dna",
        "biochemistry_glucose"
      ],
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.457Z",
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "question": "What does the endoplasmic reticulum (ER) do?",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "distractors": []
    },
    {
      "id": "Cellular Anatomy 007",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "answers": [
        "Smooth ER",
        "Rough ER",
        "Golgi body",
        "Nucleolus"
      ],
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.457Z",
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "short_explanation": "Lysosomes break down waste and cellular debris.",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.109Z",
      "question": "Which type of ER has ribosomes attached and assists in protein synthesis?",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "distractors": [],
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_protein",
        "anatomy_ribosomes",
        "concept_assists",
        "concept_assisted"
      ],
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "long_explanation": "Lysosomes contain enzymes that digest macromolecules, damaged organelles, and pathogens. They help recycle cellular components and maintain homeostasis.",
      "unit": "cellular anatomy",
      "keywords": [
        "anatomy_protein",
        "anatomy_ribosomes",
        "concept_assists",
        "concept_assisted"
      ],
      "clinical_application": "Lysosomal storage diseases (e.g., Tay-Sachs) result from enzyme deficiencies, leading to toxic buildup and cell dysfunction.",
      "correct": 1
    },
    {
      "id": "Cellular Anatomy 008",
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_cells",
        "anatomy_protein"
      ],
      "clinical_application": "Defects in cytoskeletal proteins are linked to diseases like muscular dystrophy and certain neuropathies.",
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "question": "What is the function of the Golgi apparatus?",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.457Z",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "short_explanation": "The cytoskeleton gives structural support and aids movement.",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "unit": "cellular anatomy",
      "distractors": [],
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "long_explanation": "The cytoskeleton is a network of microfilaments, microtubules, and intermediate filaments that maintain shape, anchor organelles, and facilitate intracellular transport and cell division.",
      "correct": 2,
      "keywords": [
        "anatomy_cells",
        "anatomy_protein"
      ],
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.109Z",
      "answers": [
        "Powering the cell",
        "Producing enzymes",
        "Modifying, sorting, and packaging proteins",
        "Destroying toxins"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "Cellular Anatomy 009",
      "question": "What is cytoplasm?",
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "long_explanation": "The nuclear envelope surrounds the nucleus and contains nuclear pores that allow selective exchange of RNA, proteins, and signaling molecules, ensuring controlled communication between nucleus and cytoplasm.",
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_cells",
        "anatomy_cytoplasm",
        "anatomy_protein",
        "anatomy_organelle",
        "anatomy_dna"
      ],
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.109Z",
      "answers": [
        "A type of DNA",
        "A jelly-like substance that holds organelles",
        "A protein transporter",
        "A structure in the cell wall"
      ],
      "clinical_application": "Altered nuclear transport is involved in viral replication and neurodegenerative diseases like ALS and Huntington’s.",
      "keywords": [
        "anatomy_cells",
        "anatomy_cytoplasm",
        "anatomy_protein",
        "anatomy_organelle",
        "anatomy_dna"
      ],
      "distractors": [],
      "unit": "cellular anatomy",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "correct": 1,
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "short_explanation": "The nuclear envelope’s pores control molecular traffic.",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.457Z",
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "Cellular Anatomy 010",
      "keywords": [
        "anatomy_cells",
        "anatomy_ribosomes"
      ],
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.109Z",
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "answers": [
        "Produces ATP",
        "Produces ribosomes",
        "Breaks down sugars",
        "Directs cell division"
      ],
      "unit": "cellular anatomy",
      "question": "What is the function of the nucleolus?",
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_cells",
        "anatomy_ribosomes"
      ],
      "long_explanation": "The cytoplasm includes cytosol and suspended organelles. It’s where many metabolic reactions occur and facilitates molecular transport within the cell.",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "distractors": [],
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "clinical_application": "Cytoplasmic changes (e.g., inclusion bodies, vacuolization) are diagnostic clues in infection, cancer, and cell injury.",
      "short_explanation": "The cytoplasm provides a medium for chemical reactions and organelle suspension.",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.457Z",
      "correct": 1
    },
    {
      "id": "Characteristics Of Cells 001",
      "question": "Which of the following is a universal characteristic of all cells?",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.457Z",
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "distractors": [],
      "keywords": [
        "anatomy_membrane",
        "anatomy_cells",
        "anatomy_nucleus"
      ],
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "unit": "characteristics of cells",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.111Z",
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "answers": [
        "Presence of nucleus",
        "Ability to photosynthesize",
        "Enclosed by a cell membrane",
        "Multicellular structure"
      ],
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_membrane",
        "anatomy_cells",
        "anatomy_nucleus"
      ],
      "clinical_application": "Understanding the cell membrane helps massage therapists appreciate how cellular hydration, inflammation, and pressure sensitivity are affected by touch and tissue work.",
      "long_explanation": "The cell membrane is a flexible, semipermeable barrier that separates the interior of the cell from the external environment. It regulates what enters and exits the cell, providing protection and structure.",
      "short_explanation": "All cells are enclosed by a cell membrane that maintains their internal environment.",
      "correct": 2
    },
    {
      "id": "Characteristics Of Cells 002",
      "correct": 1,
      "short_explanation": "DNA contains instructions for cell function, growth, and reproduction.",
      "unit": "characteristics of cells",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.457Z",
      "answers": [
        "Produces energy",
        "Controls cell activities and stores genetic information",
        "Forms ribosomes",
        "Carries oxygen"
      ],
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_cells",
        "anatomy_ribosomes",
        "anatomy_dna"
      ],
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.111Z",
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "question": "What is the function of DNA in cells?",
      "distractors": [],
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "long_explanation": "DNA encodes the genetic blueprint of life. It directs the synthesis of proteins that regulate structure and function in the body. Each cell’s identity and purpose are defined by the genes it expresses.",
      "keywords": [
        "anatomy_cells",
        "anatomy_ribosomes",
        "anatomy_dna"
      ],
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "clinical_application": "Massage therapy cannot alter DNA, but stress reduction and healthy circulation may support gene expression related to repair and immunity."
    },
    {
      "id": "Characteristics Of Cells 003",
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.457Z",
      "correct": 2,
      "keywords": [
        "anatomy_cells",
        "anatomy_cytoplasm",
        "anatomy_organelle"
      ],
      "answers": [
        "The outer covering of the cell",
        "The site of energy production",
        "The jelly-like substance that holds organelles",
        "The storage site for waste"
      ],
      "long_explanation": "The cytoplasm includes the cytosol and embedded organelles. It facilitates nutrient diffusion, enzymatic reactions, and cellular movement. It's vital for homeostasis and energy transfer.",
      "short_explanation": "The cytoplasm suspends organelles and supports metabolic activity.",
      "distractors": [],
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_cells",
        "anatomy_cytoplasm",
        "anatomy_organelle"
      ],
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.111Z",
      "clinical_application": "Massage improves circulation, which enhances nutrient delivery that ultimately diffuses through the cytoplasm to maintain cell vitality.",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "unit": "characteristics of cells",
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "question": "Which of the following best describes the cytoplasm?"
    },
    {
      "id": "Characteristics Of Cells 004",
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "short_explanation": "Ribosomes create proteins by translating genetic instructions.",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "keywords": [
        "anatomy_cells",
        "anatomy_tissues",
        "anatomy_dna"
      ],
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_cells",
        "anatomy_tissues",
        "anatomy_dna"
      ],
      "answers": [
        "DNA molecules",
        "Spontaneous generation",
        "Other pre-existing cells",
        "Tissues"
      ],
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.457Z",
      "clinical_application": "Tissue regeneration depends on protein synthesis. Massage therapy may support protein production indirectly by improving circulation and reducing stress hormones that impair recovery.",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.111Z",
      "question": "All cells arise from:",
      "correct": 2,
      "long_explanation": "Ribosomes read messenger RNA (mRNA) to assemble amino acids into proteins, essential for cell repair, structure, and function. They can float freely or attach to the ER.",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "unit": "characteristics of cells",
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "distractors": []
    },
    {
      "id": "Characteristics Of Cells 005",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.457Z",
      "short_explanation": "Mitochondria generate cellular energy through respiration.",
      "question": "Which component do all cells use to synthesize proteins?",
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_cells",
        "anatomy_ribosomes",
        "anatomy_component"
      ],
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "unit": "characteristics of cells",
      "answers": [
        "Chloroplasts",
        "Ribosomes",
        "Mitochondria",
        "Golgi bodies"
      ],
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "clinical_application": "Massage therapy improves oxygen delivery, potentially supporting mitochondrial ATP production, enhancing tissue healing and energy availability.",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.111Z",
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "distractors": [],
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "correct": 1,
      "keywords": [
        "anatomy_cells",
        "anatomy_ribosomes",
        "anatomy_component"
      ],
      "long_explanation": "Mitochondria convert glucose and oxygen into ATP — the body’s usable energy form. They also regulate metabolism and cell death.",
      "difficulty": "intermediate"
    },
    {
      "id": "Characteristics Of Cells 006",
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_cells",
        "anatomy_nucleus",
        "anatomy_ribosomes",
        "biology_eukaryotic",
        "biology_prokaryotes"
      ],
      "short_explanation": "The cell membrane regulates entry and exit of materials.",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.111Z",
      "long_explanation": "The phospholipid bilayer of the cell membrane includes transport proteins and receptors that mediate the cell’s exchange of ions, nutrients, and waste with the extracellular environment.",
      "unit": "characteristics of cells",
      "answers": [
        "Nucleus",
        "Chloroplasts",
        "Ribosomes",
        "Endoplasmic reticulum"
      ],
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.457Z",
      "clinical_application": "Proper membrane function is essential for cell health. Massage may influence interstitial fluid exchange, indirectly benefiting nutrient flow to cell membranes.",
      "keywords": [
        "anatomy_cells",
        "anatomy_nucleus",
        "anatomy_ribosomes",
        "biology_eukaryotic",
        "biology_prokaryotes"
      ],
      "question": "What do both prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells have in common?",
      "correct": 2,
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "distractors": []
    },
    {
      "id": "Characteristics Of Cells 007",
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "unit": "characteristics of cells",
      "short_explanation": "The nucleus stores and protects DNA.",
      "clinical_application": "Though massage doesn’t directly influence nuclear function, reducing systemic inflammation may help create conditions that preserve cellular health.",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.457Z",
      "question": "Which structure regulates what enters and exits a cell?",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.111Z",
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_membrane",
        "anatomy_cells",
        "anatomy_nucleus",
        "anatomy_cytoplasm"
      ],
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "long_explanation": "As the control center of the cell, the nucleus houses chromosomes made of DNA. It regulates gene expression and directs cellular activity, including protein production and cell division.",
      "correct": 3,
      "answers": [
        "Cytoplasm",
        "Nucleus",
        "Mitochondria",
        "Cell membrane"
      ],
      "distractors": [],
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "keywords": [
        "anatomy_membrane",
        "anatomy_cells",
        "anatomy_nucleus",
        "anatomy_cytoplasm"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "Characteristics Of Cells 008",
      "keywords": [
        "anatomy_cells",
        "biology_eukaryotic",
        "biology_prokaryotes"
      ],
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.457Z",
      "question": "Which type of cell is typically larger and more complex?",
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_cells",
        "biology_eukaryotic",
        "biology_prokaryotes"
      ],
      "long_explanation": "The rough ER (with ribosomes) helps synthesize proteins, while the smooth ER produces lipids and detoxifies chemicals. The ER helps shape the internal cell structure.",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "clinical_application": "ER function is critical in tissue recovery and detoxification. Enhanced circulation from massage may support cellular repair that relies on ER pathways.",
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "short_explanation": "The ER builds and processes proteins and lipids.",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.111Z",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "answers": [
        "Prokaryotic",
        "Eukaryotic",
        "Viral",
        "Bacterial"
      ],
      "unit": "characteristics of cells",
      "distractors": [],
      "correct": 1
    },
    {
      "id": "Characteristics Of Cells 009",
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.457Z",
      "distractors": [],
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "clinical_application": "The Golgi is key in secretion and repair. Massage may support the demand for Golgi activity during recovery by promoting cell turnover and reducing stress.",
      "question": "Which cell function is most related to mitochondria?",
      "short_explanation": "The Golgi body processes and transports cellular products.",
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_cells",
        "anatomy_protein"
      ],
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "keywords": [
        "anatomy_cells",
        "anatomy_protein"
      ],
      "long_explanation": "The Golgi apparatus receives proteins and lipids from the ER, modifies them (e.g., adding sugars), and directs them to their destinations inside or outside the cell.",
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "answers": [
        "Protein production",
        "Cell division",
        "Energy production",
        "Transport of materials"
      ],
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.111Z",
      "unit": "characteristics of cells",
      "correct": 2,
      "category": "Cell Theory"
    },
    {
      "id": "Characteristics Of Cells 010",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_cells",
        "anatomy_nucleus",
        "anatomy_cytosol"
      ],
      "question": "The fluid portion of the cell outside the nucleus is called:",
      "keywords": [
        "anatomy_cells",
        "anatomy_nucleus",
        "anatomy_cytosol"
      ],
      "long_explanation": "Lysosomes contain digestive enzymes that break down damaged organelles, pathogens, and cellular debris. This prevents waste accumulation and supports immune function.",
      "distractors": [],
      "short_explanation": "Lysosomes digest and recycle cellular waste.",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.111Z",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.457Z",
      "clinical_application": "Tissue massage may aid in lymphatic movement and clearance of waste products, complementing lysosomal activity at the cellular level.",
      "unit": "characteristics of cells",
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "answers": [
        "Plasma",
        "Cytosol",
        "Nucleoplasm",
        "Periplasm"
      ],
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "correct": 1,
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "Cytology 001",
      "short_explanation": "Cytology is the study of cells and their structure and function.",
      "correct": 1,
      "question": "What is cytology the study of?",
      "distractors": [],
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.112Z",
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "tags": [
        "cytology",
        "cell biology",
        "cell structure"
      ],
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.458Z",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "unit": "cytology",
      "long_explanation": "Cytology examines the form, physiology, and pathology of individual cells. It’s essential for understanding how cells function, reproduce, and respond to disease.",
      "answers": [
        "Tissues",
        "Cells",
        "Organs",
        "Organ systems"
      ],
      "clinical_application": "Though massage doesn’t affect cells directly, cellular knowledge helps therapists appreciate tissue health and healing processes at the microscopic level.",
      "keywords": [
        "cell",
        "structure",
        "microscopic",
        "function",
        "pathology"
      ],
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0
    },
    {
      "id": "Cytology 002",
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "short_explanation": "Hematoxylin and eosin (H&E) stain cell nuclei dark blue or purple.",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.458Z",
      "keywords": [
        "hematoxylin",
        "eosin",
        "nucleus",
        "histology",
        "diagnosis"
      ],
      "unit": "cytology",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "tags": [
        "staining",
        "cytology",
        "H&E"
      ],
      "correct": 0,
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "answers": [
        "Hematoxylin and Eosin",
        "Gram stain",
        "Crystal violet",
        "Iodine stain"
      ],
      "distractors": [],
      "clinical_application": "Massage professionals may encounter cytology reports in patient records. Understanding H&E staining helps contextualize diagnostic findings related to inflammation or cellular injury.",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.113Z",
      "long_explanation": "Hematoxylin binds to nucleic acids in the nucleus, while eosin stains the cytoplasm pink. Together, they provide contrast to visualize cell structures in histological and cytological samples.",
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "question": "Which staining technique is commonly used in cytology to highlight cell nuclei?",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0
    },
    {
      "id": "Cytology 003",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.113Z",
      "long_explanation": "Because cells are microscopic, cytology relies on light and electron microscopes to reveal detailed cellular features such as organelles, nuclei, and abnormalities.",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.458Z",
      "keywords": [
        "microscope",
        "cell exam",
        "cytology tools"
      ],
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "short_explanation": "The microscope is essential for examining cells in cytology.",
      "clinical_application": "Appreciating the microscopic basis of tissue structure can help massage therapists understand the cellular effects of injury, inflammation, and recovery.",
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "unit": "cytology",
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "distractors": [],
      "tags": [
        "microscope",
        "cytology",
        "tools"
      ],
      "correct": 2,
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "answers": [
        "Scalpel",
        "Telescope",
        "Microscope",
        "Centrifuge"
      ],
      "question": "Which tool is essential in the study of cytology?"
    },
    {
      "id": "Cytology 004",
      "correct": 1,
      "keywords": [
        "malignancy",
        "cytology",
        "oncology"
      ],
      "unit": "cytology",
      "short_explanation": "Oncocytology is the study of cancerous cellular changes.",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.458Z",
      "clinical_application": "Massage therapists should be aware of cytological terms like oncocytology when clients disclose cancer histories, supporting appropriate referral and avoiding contraindicated work.",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "question": "What branch of cytology focuses on detecting cancer cells?",
      "long_explanation": "Oncocytology involves identifying abnormal cell morphology, such as irregular nuclei and mitotic figures, that indicate malignancy. It's essential for early cancer detection.",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.113Z",
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "tags": [
        "cytology",
        "oncocytology",
        "cancer"
      ],
      "answers": [
        "Histopathology",
        "Oncocytology",
        "Genomics",
        "Neurology"
      ],
      "distractors": []
    },
    {
      "id": "Cytology 005",
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "question": "Which cell component is analyzed for genetic mutations in cytological testing?",
      "long_explanation": "Cytological testing often focuses on the nucleus, where DNA mutations can signal conditions like cancer, infections, or inherited diseases.",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "answers": [
        "Ribosomes",
        "Cytoplasm",
        "Nucleus",
        "Golgi apparatus"
      ],
      "unit": "cytology",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "distractors": [],
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.458Z",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "tags": [
        "nucleus",
        "genetics",
        "cytology"
      ],
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.113Z",
      "short_explanation": "The nucleus is where genetic mutations are typically detected.",
      "correct": 2,
      "keywords": [
        "DNA",
        "mutation",
        "genetics",
        "cytopathology"
      ],
      "clinical_application": "While massage doesn’t influence nuclear DNA, understanding the role of the nucleus enhances awareness of how cellular changes affect systemic health."
    },
    {
      "id": "Cytology 006",
      "keywords": [
        "screening",
        "cytopathology",
        "women's health",
        "epithelial cells"
      ],
      "tags": [
        "Pap smear",
        "cytology",
        "cervical cancer"
      ],
      "short_explanation": "A Pap smear collects cervical cells for cytological analysis.",
      "answers": [
        "Urine",
        "Pap smear",
        "Saliva",
        "Blood plasma"
      ],
      "correct": 1,
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "distractors": [],
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.113Z",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "unit": "cytology",
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "clinical_application": "Massage therapists should be aware of Pap smear significance during client health histories and avoid abdominal work if recent abnormal findings are reported without clearance.",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "question": "What is a common cytological sample used in cervical cancer screening?",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.458Z",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "long_explanation": "Pap smears are used to detect abnormal epithelial cells in the cervix that could indicate precancerous or cancerous changes. The sample is examined under a microscope."
    },
    {
      "id": "Cytology 007",
      "clinical_application": "Massage therapists working with oncology or chronic illness clients may encounter exfoliative reports; understanding this type of cytology aids holistic awareness.",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "distractors": [],
      "keywords": [
        "shed cells",
        "screening",
        "diagnosis",
        "surface tissue"
      ],
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "long_explanation": "Exfoliative cytology analyzes cells naturally shed from surfaces like the mouth, cervix, or respiratory tract. It is used for routine screenings and non-invasive diagnosis.",
      "answers": [
        "Blood cells",
        "Muscle cells",
        "Shed epithelial cells",
        "Nerve cells"
      ],
      "unit": "cytology",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.113Z",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.458Z",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "question": "Which type of cells are often studied in exfoliative cytology?",
      "tags": [
        "cytology",
        "exfoliative",
        "epithelial cells"
      ],
      "correct": 2,
      "short_explanation": "Shed epithelial cells are common in exfoliative cytology."
    },
    {
      "id": "Cytology 008",
      "correct": 2,
      "long_explanation": "At 400x magnification, cytotechnologists can clearly identify nuclear and cytoplasmic details that indicate disease. It’s a standard in routine cytology exams.",
      "short_explanation": "They use high-powered microscopes, typically 400x magnification.",
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "keywords": [
        "400x",
        "slide analysis",
        "diagnostic detail"
      ],
      "question": "Cytotechnologists typically examine samples under what magnification?",
      "distractors": [],
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "tags": [
        "cytology",
        "microscope",
        "magnification"
      ],
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.458Z",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.113Z",
      "answers": [
        "2x",
        "10x",
        "400x",
        "1000x"
      ],
      "clinical_application": "While not directly used in massage, this underscores the precision of cellular evaluation — reminding therapists how subtle changes at the micro level can impact macro health.",
      "unit": "cytology"
    },
    {
      "id": "Cytology 009",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "tags": [
        "nucleus",
        "H&E staining",
        "cytology"
      ],
      "correct": 2,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.458Z",
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "distractors": [],
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "keywords": [
        "hematoxylin",
        "nuclear staining",
        "blue nucleus"
      ],
      "long_explanation": "In H&E staining, the nucleus takes up hematoxylin due to its acidic DNA content, aiding in the identification of cell health, division, and pathology.",
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "unit": "cytology",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.113Z",
      "answers": [
        "Golgi apparatus",
        "Cytoplasm",
        "Nucleus",
        "Lysosomes"
      ],
      "question": "Which part of a cell is typically stained dark blue/purple in H&E staining?",
      "short_explanation": "The nucleus stains dark blue/purple with hematoxylin.",
      "clinical_application": "Therapists may encounter cytological findings in medical documentation. Understanding staining patterns supports better communication with healthcare teams."
    },
    {
      "id": "Cytology 010",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.113Z",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "correct": 1,
      "short_explanation": "Cytology detects cellular abnormalities and diseases early.",
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "unit": "cytology",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "question": "Why is cytology important in diagnostics?",
      "answers": [
        "It identifies nutrient deficiencies",
        "It allows detection of cellular abnormalities and disease",
        "It helps determine heart rate",
        "It promotes exercise recovery"
      ],
      "clinical_application": "Massage therapists must recognize that cytological analysis informs many clinical decisions and that staying within scope means avoiding treatment claims for such cellular-level issues.",
      "tags": [
        "cytology",
        "diagnostics",
        "disease detection"
      ],
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "keywords": [
        "screening",
        "early detection",
        "cell changes",
        "precancer"
      ],
      "long_explanation": "Cytological exams identify changes in cell shape, size, and structure that indicate infections, precancerous conditions, or malignancy — often before symptoms appear.",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.458Z",
      "distractors": [],
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0
    },
    {
      "id": "Cytoplasm Cytosol 001",
      "short_explanation": "Cytoplasm is the jelly-like material inside the cell, excluding the nucleus.",
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_cytoplasm",
        "anatomy_cytosol",
        "cell_function"
      ],
      "answers": [
        "The outer covering of the nucleus",
        "The jelly-like material inside the cell, excluding the nucleus",
        "The site of DNA replication",
        "The rigid structure outside the plasma membrane"
      ],
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "long_explanation": "Cytoplasm consists of cytosol and suspended organelles. It supports intracellular transport, houses chemical reactions, and plays a key role in cell shape and function.",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.458Z",
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "unit": "cytoplasm cytosol",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.113Z",
      "question": "What is cytoplasm?",
      "keywords": [
        "cytoplasm",
        "intracellular",
        "cell interior",
        "fluid matrix"
      ],
      "clinical_application": "In massage therapy, understanding cytoplasm function supports appreciation for how improved circulation may enhance cellular nutrient exchange and waste removal.",
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "correct": 1,
      "distractors": [],
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0
    },
    {
      "id": "Cytoplasm Cytosol 002",
      "short_explanation": "The cytosol is the fluid portion of the cytoplasm, excluding organelles.",
      "keywords": [
        "cytosol",
        "fluid",
        "cell matrix",
        "diffusion"
      ],
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "question": "Cytosol refers specifically to:",
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_cytosol",
        "cell_fluid",
        "cell_environment"
      ],
      "answers": [
        "The entire cell content",
        "The fluid portion of the cytoplasm, not including organelles",
        "The nucleus only",
        "The membrane structure"
      ],
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.113Z",
      "long_explanation": "Cytosol is a water-based solution containing ions, proteins, and enzymes. It facilitates biochemical reactions and molecular diffusion.",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.458Z",
      "correct": 1,
      "distractors": [],
      "unit": "cytoplasm cytosol",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "clinical_application": "Massage therapy improves circulation, which may optimize delivery of nutrients that diffuse through cytosol to support tissue healing."
    },
    {
      "id": "Cytoplasm Cytosol 003",
      "tags": [
        "cytoplasm",
        "cellular_respiration",
        "glycolysis"
      ],
      "distractors": [],
      "answers": [
        "Photosynthesis",
        "Transcription",
        "Glycolysis",
        "DNA replication"
      ],
      "short_explanation": "Glycolysis occurs in the cytoplasm, producing ATP from glucose.",
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "unit": "cytoplasm cytosol",
      "correct": 2,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.458Z",
      "question": "Which of the following occurs in the cytoplasm?",
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "clinical_application": "Massage can enhance oxygen and glucose delivery, potentially improving cellular energy production where glycolysis occurs.",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.113Z",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "keywords": [
        "cytoplasm",
        "ATP",
        "glycolysis",
        "glucose"
      ],
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "long_explanation": "Glycolysis is the first step in cellular respiration. It takes place in the cytoplasm and breaks glucose into pyruvate, generating energy in the form of ATP."
    },
    {
      "id": "Cytoplasm Cytosol 004",
      "correct": 2,
      "tags": [
        "cytoplasm",
        "organelle",
        "intracellular"
      ],
      "short_explanation": "Cytoplasm includes both cytosol and the organelles it suspends.",
      "keywords": [
        "organelles",
        "cell structure",
        "cytosol",
        "cytoplasm"
      ],
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "answers": [
        "Only water and ions",
        "Only the nucleus",
        "Organelles and cytosol",
        "Only mitochondria"
      ],
      "question": "Cytoplasm includes which of the following?",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.458Z",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.113Z",
      "distractors": [],
      "long_explanation": "Cytoplasm is everything inside the cell membrane except the nucleus. It includes cytosol, organelles, and cytoskeletal elements.",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "clinical_application": "Massage may impact hydration and nutrient flow, indirectly influencing cellular performance supported by the cytoplasm.",
      "unit": "cytoplasm cytosol",
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "last_answered_at": null
    },
    {
      "id": "Cytoplasm Cytosol 005",
      "unit": "cytoplasm cytosol",
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "distractors": [],
      "clinical_application": "Metabolic efficiency is vital to tissue repair. Massage may promote nutrient delivery that supports these cytosolic processes.",
      "keywords": [
        "metabolism",
        "cell reactions",
        "enzyme activity",
        "cytosol"
      ],
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "correct": 2,
      "short_explanation": "The cytosol is the site of many metabolic reactions in the cell.",
      "tags": [
        "cytosol",
        "cell_metabolism",
        "cell_function"
      ],
      "answers": [
        "Protein packaging",
        "DNA storage",
        "Site of many metabolic reactions",
        "Lipid digestion"
      ],
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "long_explanation": "Many critical cellular processes—such as glycolysis, ion storage, and signal transduction—occur in the cytosol.",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.113Z",
      "question": "What is one of the main functions of the cytosol?",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.458Z"
    },
    {
      "id": "Cytoplasm Cytosol 006",
      "distractors": [],
      "long_explanation": "Cytosol is the medium in which organelles are suspended. It allows movement and communication between cellular structures.",
      "clinical_application": "Understanding cytosol structure helps massage therapists appreciate how fluid dynamics inside the cell mirror larger tissue processes.",
      "correct": 0,
      "short_explanation": "Mitochondria and other organelles are suspended in the cytosol.",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.458Z",
      "tags": [
        "cytosol",
        "organelle_suspension",
        "cell_structure"
      ],
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.113Z",
      "topic": "cell_theory",

      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "keywords": [
        "suspended organelles",
        "cell fluid",
        "cytoplasmic support"
      ],
      "answers": [
        "Mitochondria",
        "Plasma membrane",
        "Cell wall",
        "Nucleolus"
      ],
      "question": "Which of the following would be found suspended in the cytosol?",
      "last_answered_at": null
    },
    {
      "id": "Cytoplasm Cytosol 007",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.458Z",
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "clinical_application": "Massage therapy can support hydration—a key factor in cytosolic function—helping maintain tissue elasticity and repair.",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "short_explanation": "About 70% of the cytosol is composed of water.",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.113Z",
      "answers": [
        "10%",
        "30%",
        "70%",
        "100%"
      ],
      "unit": "cytoplasm cytosol",
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "long_explanation": "Cytosol is mostly water with dissolved ions, proteins, and small molecules. This high water content supports chemical reactions and transport.",
      "correct": 2,
      "distractors": [],
      "keywords": [
        "cytosol",
        "water content",
        "hydration",
        "cell environment"
      ],
      "question": "What percentage of the cytosol is typically water?",

    {
      "id": "Cytoplasm Cytosol 008",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.113Z",
      "long_explanation": "The cytoplasm encompasses all components inside the cell membrane, excluding the nucleus. This includes cytosol, organelles, and inclusions.",
      "unit": "cytoplasm cytosol",
      "clinical_application": "This distinction helps massage professionals interpret cellular responses to injury or recovery more accurately.",
      "question": "The cytoplasm does NOT include the:",
      "short_explanation": "The cytoplasm does not include the nucleus.",

    {

      "last_answered_at": null,
      "clinical_application": "Massage may influence oxygen availability, indirectly affecting how efficiently cells rely on cytosolic energy pathways.",
      "short_explanation": "Anaerobic respiration begins in the cytosol of animal cells.",
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "tags": [
        "cytosol",
        "anaerobic",
        "cell_energy"
      ],
      "unit": "cytoplasm cytosol",
      "stats": {

      },
      "question": "In animal cells, where does anaerobic respiration begin?",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "correct": 2,
      "distractors": [],
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.113Z",
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "long_explanation": "In the absence of oxygen, glycolysis begins in the cytosol to generate ATP. It precedes mitochondrial respiration or continues in anaerobic conditions.",
      "difficulty": "intermediate"
    },

    {
      "id": "Golgi Apparatus 001",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.113Z",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.458Z",
      "clinical_application": "Massage supports circulation, potentially enhancing delivery of nutrients that rely on proper packaging and secretion pathways managed by the Golgi.",
      "answers": [
        "Energy production",
        "Protein synthesis",
        "Modifying, sorting, and packaging proteins and lipids",
        "DNA replication"
      ],
      "correct": 2,
      "question": "What is the main function of the Golgi apparatus?",
      "short_explanation": "The Golgi apparatus modifies, sorts, and packages proteins and lipids.",
      "unit": "golgi apparatus",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "tags": [
        "golgi_function",
        "protein_processing"
      ],
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "long_explanation": "The Golgi apparatus receives materials from the ER, processes them (e.g., glycosylation), and then sorts and packages them into vesicles for delivery to specific destinations.",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "keywords": [
        "golgi",
        "protein modification",
        "cell packaging",
        "organelle"
      ],
      "distractors": []
    },
    {
      "id": "Golgi Apparatus 002",
      "clinical_application": "Efficient cellular repair—important in massage recovery—requires this ER-Golgi cooperation for protein distribution.",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.458Z",
      "tags": [
        "golgi_er",
        "protein_transport"
      ],
      "short_explanation": "The Golgi works closely with the rough ER to modify and package proteins.",
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "answers": [
        "Mitochondria",
        "Nucleolus",
        "Rough ER",
        "Centrioles"
      ],
      "question": "The Golgi apparatus works most closely with which organelle?",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.113Z",
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "distractors": [],
      "unit": "golgi apparatus",
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "long_explanation": "The rough ER synthesizes proteins, which are then transported to the Golgi apparatus for further processing and packaging into vesicles.",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "correct": 2,
      "keywords": [
        "rough ER",
        "golgi",
        "protein pathway"
      ]
    },
    {

      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "unit": "golgi apparatus",
      "correct": 2,
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0,

      },
      "clinical_application": "This structure helps organize how materials are processed and secreted—key to understanding tissue growth and repair after massage.",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.113Z",

      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.458Z",
      "distractors": [],
      "short_explanation": "Cisternae are the stacked membrane sacs of the Golgi apparatus.",
      "long_explanation": "The Golgi is composed of flattened membrane-bound sacs called cisternae, where different stages of protein and lipid modification occur.",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "question": "What structure describes the flattened sacs that make up the Golgi apparatus?",
      "keywords": [
        "cisternae",
        "golgi stack",
        "flattened sacs"
      ],
      "answers": [

    },
    {
      "id": "Golgi Apparatus 004",
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "short_explanation": "Proteins travel from the cis face to the trans face of the Golgi.",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.113Z",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "unit": "golgi apparatus",
      "answers": [
        "From trans face to cis face",
        "Randomly",
        "From nucleus directly to plasma membrane",
        "From cis face to trans face"
      ],
      "long_explanation": "The cis face of the Golgi receives vesicles from the ER, and proteins are processed as they move through the Golgi to the trans face for export.",
      "clinical_application": "This directional processing highlights how cellular systems prepare for extracellular communication—important in inflammatory and healing responses post-massage.",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.458Z",
      "question": "Which direction do proteins travel through the Golgi apparatus?",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "topic": "cell_theory",

      ],
      "keywords": [

      ],

      "correct": 3,
      "distractors": []
    },

      "short_explanation": "The lysosome receives digestive enzymes from the Golgi apparatus.",
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "question": "Which organelle often receives materials from the Golgi for digestion?",
      "long_explanation": "Lysosomes are vesicles filled with enzymes made in the ER and processed in the Golgi. These enzymes break down waste and cellular debris.",

    {
      "id": "Plasma Membrane Potential 001",
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_membrane_potential",
        "cell_electrophysiology"
      ],
      "short_explanation": "Membrane potential is the voltage difference across the plasma membrane.",
      "answers": [
        "The pressure inside the nucleus",
        "The voltage difference across the plasma membrane",
        "The size of the cell membrane",
        "The thickness of the lipid bilayer"
      ],
      "distractors": [],
      "unit": "plasma membrane potential",
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "correct": 1,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.459Z",
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "question": "What is membrane potential?",
      "clinical_application": "Massage may influence membrane potential indirectly by reducing sympathetic tone and supporting ionic balance critical for muscle relaxation.",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.114Z",
      "keywords": [
        "voltage",
        "ionic gradient",
        "nerve conduction",
        "membrane charge"
      ],
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "long_explanation": "It results from the unequal distribution of ions inside and outside the cell, creating a difference in electrical charge. This electrical gradient is crucial for nerve impulse conduction and muscle contraction."
    },
    {
      "id": "Plasma Membrane Potential 002",
      "keywords": [
        "Na+",
        "K+",
        "resting potential",
        "electrolytes",
        "ion balance"
      ],
      "correct": 2,
      "unit": "plasma membrane potential",
      "short_explanation": "Na+ and K+ are the primary ions involved.",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "clinical_application": "Understanding ion gradients helps massage therapists appreciate how electrolyte imbalances affect muscle function, tension, and cramping.",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.459Z",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "question": "What ions are most responsible for establishing resting membrane potential?",
      "long_explanation": "The sodium-potassium gradient, maintained by Na+/K+ pumps, creates the charge difference across the membrane that defines resting membrane potential.",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.114Z",
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_membrane_potential",
        "ions"
      ],
      "answers": [
        "Na+ and Cl-",
        "Ca2+ and Mg2+",
        "Na+ and K+",
        "H+ and OH-"
      ],
      "distractors": []
    },
    {
      "id": "Plasma Membrane Potential 003",
      "question": "What is the typical resting membrane potential of a neuron?",
      "keywords": [
        "-70 mV",
        "resting neuron",
        "nerve impulse",
        "cell polarity"
      ],
      "long_explanation": "At rest, neurons maintain a slightly negative internal charge. This polarity is essential for firing action potentials during nerve signaling.",
      "correct": 2,
      "unit": "plasma membrane potential",
      "distractors": [],
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.114Z",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "answers": [
        "+70 mV",
        "0 mV",
        "-70 mV",
        "-30 mV"
      ],
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.459Z",
      "short_explanation": "Around -70 mV",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "clinical_application": "Nerve communication underlies pain sensation and muscle tone. Massage may modulate this by promoting parasympathetic activity.",
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_membrane_potential",
        "neurophysiology"
      ],
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "difficulty": "intermediate"
    },
    {
      "id": "Plasma Membrane Potential 004",
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "unit": "plasma membrane potential",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "short_explanation": "The Na+/K+ ATPase pump maintains the ionic gradient.",
      "clinical_application": "Proper function of this pump supports neuromuscular responsiveness. Massage may enhance local oxygenation and ATP availability.",
      "question": "Which protein helps maintain resting membrane potential by pumping ions against their gradient?",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.459Z",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.114Z",
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_membrane_potential",
        "membrane_protein"
      ],
      "answers": [
        "Calcium channel",
        "Glucose transporter",
        "Na+/K+ ATPase pump",
        "Aquaporin"
      ],
      "correct": 2,
      "long_explanation": "This active transporter uses ATP to move 3 Na+ ions out and 2 K+ ions into the cell, preserving electrical charge and fluid balance.",
      "distractors": [],
      "keywords": [
        "sodium-potassium pump",
        "ATPase",
        "ion transport"
      ],
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0
    },
    {
      "id": "Plasma Membrane Potential 005",
      "short_explanation": "It moves 3 Na+ out and 2 K+ in using ATP.",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.459Z",
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "answers": [
        "Moves 2 K+ in and 3 Na+ out using ATP",
        "Moves 3 K+ in and 2 Na+ out using ATP",
        "Only moves sodium into the cell",
        "Transports glucose"
      ],
      "distractors": [],
      "question": "What does the Na+/K+ pump do?",
      "unit": "plasma membrane potential",
      "long_explanation": "This pump helps reset resting potential after nerve firing and maintains cellular ion gradients. It's vital for muscle and nerve cell recovery.",
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.114Z",
      "clinical_application": "Massage’s impact on circulation and parasympathetic tone may support the recovery of cells relying on this pump.",
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,

        "muscle function"
      ],

    {

      "last_answered_at": null,
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_membrane",
        "cell_polarization"
      ],
      "correct": 2,
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "short_explanation": "The inside of the cell is negative relative to the outside.",
      "question": "Which of the following is true of a polarized membrane?",
      "long_explanation": "At rest, the membrane maintains a polarized state due to ion separation, which is essential for muscle contraction and signal propagation.",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.114Z",
      "distractors": [],
      "keywords": [
        "polarized",
        "charge separation",
        "resting state",
        "bioelectricity"
      ],
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "clinical_application": "Techniques that reduce muscle tension may help reset polarization states and improve cellular responsiveness.",
      "unit": "plasma membrane potential",
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "answers": [
        "There is no difference in charge across the membrane",
        "The outside is negative relative to the inside",
        "The inside is negative relative to the outside",
        "It is not influenced by ions"
      ],
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.459Z"
    },

      "answers": [

        "Na+ channels open and Na+ rushes in",

      ],

    {
      "id": "Plasma Membrane Potential 008",
      "question": "During repolarization, the membrane potential is:",
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0,

      },
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,

      "keywords": [
        "repolarization",
        "K+ efflux",
        "membrane reset"
      ],
      "long_explanation": "Repolarization restores the resting state as K+ exits the cell. It's critical for repeated nerve signaling.",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.114Z",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.459Z",
      "unit": "plasma membrane potential",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_membrane_potential",
        "recovery_phase"
      ],
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "answers": [
        "Returning to its resting negative value",
        "Becoming more positive",
        "Maintained at 0 mV",
        "Dropping to -90 mV permanently"
      ],
      "correct": 0,
      "clinical_application": "Proper recovery between signals helps maintain healthy reflex and motor patterns. Massage may facilitate this process by reducing tension.",
      "short_explanation": "Returning to its negative resting value."
    },
    {
      "id": "Plasma Membrane Potential 009",
      "correct": 2,
      "long_explanation": "This occurs when excess K+ exits the cell or Cl- enters, temporarily inhibiting cell firing.",
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0,

      },
      "keywords": [

      "topic": "cell_theory",

      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "short_explanation": "The membrane becomes more negative than resting potential.",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "clinical_application": "Massage techniques that calm the nervous system may promote brief hyperpolarization and support muscle relaxation.",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "answers": [
        "The membrane becomes more positive",
        "The membrane returns to baseline",
        "The inside of the cell becomes more negative than resting potential",
        "The membrane breaks down"
      ],

      "correct": 2,
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.114Z",
      "long_explanation": "The cell nucleus acts as the control center, housing DNA that directs protein synthesis and cell function.",

      },
      "short_explanation": "The nucleus controls the cell and stores genetic material (DNA).",

    {
      "id": "Plasma Membrane 001",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.458Z",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "question": "What is the primary function of the plasma membrane?",
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_membrane",
        "anatomy_cells",
        "anatomy_plasma_membrane",
        "anatomy_genetic_material",
        "cell_transport",
        "massage_clinical"

      "correct": 2,
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "answers": [

        "To support the cell wall",
        "To regulate what enters and exits the cell",
        "To store genetic material"
      ],
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.114Z",
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "clinical_application": "Understanding this helps massage therapists recognize how cellular swelling or dehydration can occur from fluid imbalances during treatment.",
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "short_explanation": "The plasma membrane controls what enters and exits the cell.",
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "distractors": [],
      "long_explanation": "The plasma membrane is selectively permeable, acting as a dynamic barrier that manages the passage of substances into and out of the cell to maintain homeostasis.",

        "anatomy_cells",
        "anatomy_plasma_membrane",
        "anatomy_genetic_material",

        "massage_clinical"

    },

        "attempts": 0
      },
      "short_explanation": "The plasma membrane is primarily made of phospholipids, cholesterol, and proteins.",
      "question": "The plasma membrane is composed mainly of:",
      "long_explanation": "Its bilayer structure provides a flexible matrix and selective barrier, while proteins serve functional roles in transport and signaling.",

      "distractors": [],

      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.458Z",
      "answers": [
        "Proteins and RNA",
        "Lipids and cellulose",
        "Phospholipids bilayer, cholesterol, and proteins",
        "DNA and sugars"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "Plasma Membrane 003",
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "clinical_application": "This is crucial in understanding edema management and waste clearance during massage.",
      "long_explanation": "The plasma membrane's selective nature is vital for maintaining internal cellular conditions by controlling ions, nutrients, and waste.",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "distractors": [],
      "short_explanation": "Selective permeability means the membrane lets some substances pass while blocking others.",

      "unit": "plasma membrane",
      "topic": "cell_theory",

      ],

    },

      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "answers": [

        "Break down lipids",
        "Maintain fluidity and stability of the membrane",
        "Transport oxygen"

      "last_answered_at": null,
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.114Z",
      "question": "Cholesterol in the plasma membrane functions to:",
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "long_explanation": "Cholesterol embeds between phospholipids, preventing extremes in fluidity and preserving membrane integrity.",
      "clinical_application": "Knowledge of membrane fluidity informs how massage influences tissue pliability and health.",

    {
      "id": "Prokaryote Vs Eukaryote 001",
      "distractors": [],
      "short_explanation": "Prokaryotes lack a nucleus.",
      "question": "Which of the following is a defining characteristic of prokaryotic cells?",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "unit": "prokaryote vs eukaryote",
      "long_explanation": "Prokaryotic cells do not have a membrane-bound nucleus. Their DNA floats freely in the cytoplasm within a region called the nucleoid.",
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.114Z",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.459Z",
      "answers": [

        "Membrane-bound organelles",
        "Lack of a nucleus",
        "Multicellular structure"
      ],
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "correct": 2,

      ],
      "short_explanation": "Eukaryotic cells have linear DNA in a membrane-bound nucleus.",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.114Z",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "long_explanation": "Eukaryotes store their DNA as organized chromosomes within a nucleus, unlike prokaryotes which have circular DNA.",
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "distractors": [],
      "clinical_application": "This concept reinforces how genetic material behaves in tissue cells during recovery after massage.",

      "question": "Which type of cell has linear DNA stored in a nucleus?",

    {

      "last_answered_at": null,

      "distractors": [],
      "question": "Bacteria are examples of which type of cell?",
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "short_explanation": "Bacteria are prokaryotic cells.",
      "unit": "prokaryote vs eukaryote",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.459Z",
      "clinical_application": "Massage therapists must understand bacteria as prokaryotes to apply hygiene standards and reduce infection risk.",
      "long_explanation": "Bacteria lack a nucleus and organelles, fitting the definition of prokaryotes. They have circular DNA and reproduce asexually.",

        "Eukaryotic",
        "Animal",
        "Prokaryotic",
        "Plant"
      ],
      "correct": 2,
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0
    },
    {

      "short_explanation": "Only eukaryotic cells contain mitochondria.",

      "clinical_application": "Cellular energy production relates to muscle function and fatigue — critical for massage therapy outcomes.",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.114Z",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "distractors": [],
      "long_explanation": "Mitochondria are organelles responsible for cellular respiration and ATP production. These are absent in prokaryotic cells.",
