    {
      "id": "Cell Theory 001",
      "correct": 0,
      "distractors": [],
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.115Z",
      "tags": [
        "history_cell_discovery"
      ],
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.460Z",
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "long_explanation": "In 1665, Hooke described small box-like structures in cork as 'cells' because they reminded him of monastery cells.",
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "unit": "cell theory",
      "answers": [
        "Robert Hooke",
        "Anton van Leeuwenhoek",
        "Rudolf Virchow",
        "Theodor Schwann"
      ],
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "clinical_application": "Understanding cell discovery helps massage therapists appreciate how microscopic anatomy relates to hands-on practice.",
      "question": "Which scientist is credited with coining the term 'cell'?",
      "short_explanation": "Robert Hooke coined the term 'cell' after observing cork under a microscope.",
      "keywords": [
        "history_cell_discovery"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "Cell Theory 002",
      "long_explanation": "The classical cell theory includes: all living things are made of cells, cells are the basic unit of life, and all cells come from other cells. Nuclei are only present in eukaryotic cells.",
      "correct": 2,
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "short_explanation": "Not all cells contain a nucleus, such as prokaryotes.",
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_cells",
        "anatomy_nucleus",
        "classical_theory"
      ],
      "question": "Which of the following is NOT part of the classical Cell Theory?",
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "distractors": [],
      "answers": [
        "All living things are made of cells",
        "Cells arise from pre-existing cells",
        "All cells contain a nucleus",
        "The cell is the basic unit of life"
      ],
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.115Z",
      "unit": "cell theory",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.460Z",
      "clinical_application": "Differentiating cell types helps massage professionals understand tissue repair and immune response.",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "keywords": [
        "anatomy_cells",
        "anatomy_nucleus",
        "classical_theory"
      ],
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "Cell Theory 003",
      "question": "Which scientist stated that all animals are made of cells?",
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "distractors": [],
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "long_explanation": "In 1839, Schwann extended cell theory to animals, uniting plants and animals under one biological principle.",
      "short_explanation": "Theodor Schwann concluded that all animals are made of cells.",
      "answers": [
        "Matthias Schleiden",
        "Theodor Schwann",
        "Robert Hooke",
        "Louis Pasteur"
      ],
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "unit": "cell theory",
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.460Z",
      "correct": 1,
      "keywords": [
        "anatomy_cells",
        "animal_cell_theory"
      ],
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.115Z",
      "clinical_application": "Understanding tissue origins is essential for applying massage techniques that affect animal (human) cells.",
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_cells",
        "animal_cell_theory"
      ],
      "last_answered_at": null
    },
    {
      "id": "Cell Theory 004",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.460Z",
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "unit": "cell theory",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.115Z",
      "keywords": [
        "anatomy_cells",
        "plant_cell_theory"
      ],
      "clinical_application": "Relating to cellular makeup helps massage therapists understand the plant-based therapies used in complementary modalities.",
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_cells",
        "plant_cell_theory"
      ],
      "long_explanation": "Schleiden observed plant tissues under microscopes and proposed that cells are the basic units of plant life.",
      "distractors": [],
      "answers": [
        "Rudolf Virchow",
        "Anton van Leeuwenhoek",
        "Robert Brown",
        "Matthias Schleiden"
      ],
      "correct": 3,
      "question": "Which scientist concluded that all plants are composed of cells?",
      "short_explanation": "Matthias Schleiden stated that plants are made of cells.",
      "category": "Cell Theory"
    },
    {
      "id": "Cell Theory 005",
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "keywords": [
        "anatomy_cells",
        "anatomy_dna",
        "cell_reproduction"
      ],
      "unit": "cell theory",
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "short_explanation": "Virchow said cells come only from pre-existing cells.",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "question": "Rudolf Virchow contributed to Cell Theory by stating:",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.460Z",
      "correct": 1,
      "long_explanation": "Virchow expanded on cell theory by rejecting spontaneous generation and emphasizing biogenesis: 'Omnis cellula e cellula.'",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.115Z",
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_cells",
        "anatomy_dna",
        "cell_reproduction"
      ],
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "clinical_application": "Massage aids healing by supporting cellular processes rooted in this principle of cellular reproduction.",
      "answers": [
        "Cells can form spontaneously",
        "Cells are produced only from other cells",
        "Cells contain chloroplasts",
        "Cells develop from DNA only"
      ],
      "distractors": [],
      "difficulty": "intermediate"
    },
    {
      "id": "Cell Theory 006",
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.115Z",
      "long_explanation": "Cell theory emphasizes that each cell performs basic life processes like energy use, waste removal, and repair.",
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_cells",
        "cell_function"
      ],
      "clinical_application": "Massage therapy supports these life functions through improved circulation and nutrient delivery.",
      "correct": 2,
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "question": "What does Cell Theory suggest about the function of cells?",
      "short_explanation": "Cells carry out all life functions.",
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "distractors": [],
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.460Z",
      "keywords": [
        "anatomy_cells",
        "cell_function"
      ],
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "answers": [
        "Cells do not function independently",
        "Cells are only used for reproduction",
        "Cells carry out all vital life processes",
        "Cells only exist in animals and plants"
      ],
      "unit": "cell theory"
    },
    {
      "id": "Cell Theory 007",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.460Z",
      "short_explanation": "Microscopy enabled observation of cells.",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "unit": "cell theory",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "long_explanation": "Advances in microscope design allowed scientists like Hooke and Leeuwenhoek to see and describe cells for the first time.",
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.115Z",
      "distractors": [],
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_cells",
        "microscopy_development"
      ],
      "correct": 1,
      "clinical_application": "Microscopic insights enhance understanding of the tissues massage therapists work with.",
      "answers": [
        "Spectroscopy",
        "Microscopy",
        "PCR",
        "X-ray imaging"
      ],
      "question": "Which technology greatly contributed to the development of Cell Theory?",
      "keywords": [
        "anatomy_cells",
        "microscopy_development"
      ],
      "category": "Cell Theory"
    },
    {
      "id": "Cell Theory 008",
      "unit": "cell theory",
      "short_explanation": "All organisms begin as a single cell.",
      "long_explanation": "From fertilization to growth, all organisms start as one cell that divides and differentiates into many specialized cells.",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.460Z",
      "question": "Which of the following is consistent with Cell Theory?",
      "correct": 1,
      "answers": [
        "Viruses are living cells",
        "All organisms begin as a single cell",
        "Only animals have cells",
        "Cells can survive without energy"
      ],
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.115Z",
      "keywords": [
        "anatomy_cells",
        "anatomy_organs",
        "science_organization",
        "biology_organisms",
        "organism_development"
      ],
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "clinical_application": "This principle supports how massage may influence early cellular regeneration and healing in therapy.",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "distractors": [],
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_cells",
        "anatomy_organs",
        "science_organization",
        "biology_organisms",
        "organism_development"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "Cell Theory 009",
      "question": "Cell Theory applies to which of the following organisms?",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.460Z",
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "distractors": [],
      "short_explanation": "Cell theory applies to all life forms, including fungi.",
      "unit": "cell theory",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "keywords": [
        "anatomy_cells",
        "microbiology_fungi",
        "biology_organisms",
        "universal_cell_theory"
      ],
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.115Z",
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_cells",
        "microbiology_fungi",
        "biology_organisms",
        "universal_cell_theory"
      ],
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "correct": 3,
      "clinical_application": "Knowledge of cellular structure across organisms supports a holistic view of health and massage’s role.",
      "answers": [
        "Animals",
        "Plants",
        "Fungi",
        "All of the above"
      ],
      "long_explanation": "Every living organism—animal, plant, or fungus—is made up of cells, making the theory universal."
    },
    {
      "id": "Cell Theory 010",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.115Z",
      "question": "How did Anton van Leeuwenhoek contribute to cell studies?",
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_cells",
        "anatomy_dna",
        "live_cell_discovery"
      ],
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.460Z",
      "unit": "cell theory",
      "answers": [
        "He developed the Cell Theory",
        "He first observed live cells under a microscope",
        "He discovered DNA",
        "He proposed spontaneous generation"
      ],
      "keywords": [
        "anatomy_cells",
        "anatomy_dna",
        "live_cell_discovery"
      ],
      "correct": 1,
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "long_explanation": "Using handcrafted microscopes, Leeuwenhoek saw living microorganisms, paving the way for microbiology and cell biology.",
      "clinical_application": "This work underlies infection control, relevant for maintaining hygiene in massage practice.",
      "distractors": [],
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "short_explanation": "He was the first to observe live cells."
    },
    {
      "id": "Animal Vs Plant Cells 001",
      "unit": "animal vs plant cells",
      "short_explanation": "Plant cells have a rigid cell wall that is not present in animal cells.",
      "long_explanation": "The cell wall is a structural layer surrounding plant cells, composed mainly of cellulose. It provides strength, protection, and helps maintain cell shape. Animal cells lack this structure, which is one of the key distinctions between the two cell types.",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.456Z",
      "question": "Which structure is found in plant cells but not in animal cells?",
      "correct": 1,
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "answers": [
        "Mitochondria",
        "Cell wall",
        "Ribosomes",
        "Nucleus"
      ],
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.106Z",
      "keywords": [
        "anatomy_cells",
        "anatomy_nucleus",
        "anatomy_ribosomes",
        "anatomy_plant_cells",
        "anatomy_animal_cells"
      ],
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "distractors": [],
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_cells",
        "anatomy_nucleus",
        "anatomy_ribosomes",
        "anatomy_plant_cells",
        "anatomy_animal_cells"
      ],
      "difficulty": "intermediate"
    },
    {
      "id": "Animal Vs Plant Cells 002",
      "keywords": [
        "anatomy_cells",
        "anatomy_organelle",
        "biochemistry_glucose",
        "anatomy_plant_cells"
      ],
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "distractors": [],
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_cells",
        "anatomy_organelle",
        "biochemistry_glucose",
        "anatomy_plant_cells"
      ],
      "unit": "animal vs plant cells",
      "question": "What organelle is responsible for photosynthesis in plant cells?",
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "correct": 2,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.456Z",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "long_explanation": "Chloroplasts are organelles unique to plant cells that convert sunlight into chemical energy through photosynthesis. They contain the pigment chlorophyll, which captures light energy to synthesize glucose, providing energy for the plant.",
      "short_explanation": "The chloroplast carries out photosynthesis in plant cells.",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.106Z",
      "answers": [
        "Mitochondrion",
        "Golgi apparatus",
        "Chloroplast",
        "Lysosome"
      ],
      "category": "Cell Theory"
    },
    {
      "id": "Animal Vs Plant Cells 003",
      "answers": [
        "Chloroplasts",
        "Large central vacuoles",
        "Centrioles",
        "Cell walls"
      ],
      "short_explanation": "Animal cells have centrioles, which are rare in most plant cells.",
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_cells",
        "anatomy_organs",
        "science_organization",
        "biology_organisms",
        "anatomy_plant_cells",
        "anatomy_animal_cells"
      ],
      "unit": "animal vs plant cells",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.456Z",
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "distractors": [],
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.106Z",
      "question": "Animal cells typically have ______, which are usually absent in plant cells.",
      "keywords": [
        "anatomy_cells",
        "anatomy_organs",
        "science_organization",
        "biology_organisms",
        "anatomy_plant_cells",
        "anatomy_animal_cells"
      ],
      "correct": 2,
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "long_explanation": "Centrioles play a key role in organizing microtubules during cell division in animal cells. While plant cells can divide without them using other organizing centers, most animal cells rely on centrioles for proper spindle formation during mitosis.",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "topic": "cell_theory"
    },
    {
      "id": "Animal Vs Plant Cells 004",
      "unit": "animal vs plant cells",
      "distractors": [],
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.456Z",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "short_explanation": "Both plant and animal cells contain a nucleus.",
      "correct": 2,
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.106Z",
      "long_explanation": "The nucleus is the control center of the cell, housing genetic material (DNA). It is a common feature of all eukaryotic cells, including both plant and animal cells, and directs activities such as growth, metabolism, and reproduction.",
      "keywords": [
        "anatomy_cells",
        "anatomy_nucleus",
        "anatomy_dna",
        "anatomy_genetic_material",
        "biology_eukaryotic",
        "anatomy_animal_cells"
      ],
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "question": "Which of the following is found in both plant and animal cells?",
      "answers": [
        "Chloroplast",
        "Cell wall",
        "Nucleus",
        "Plasmodesmata"
      ],
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_cells",
        "anatomy_nucleus",
        "anatomy_dna",
        "anatomy_genetic_material",
        "biology_eukaryotic",
        "anatomy_animal_cells"
      ],
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "category": "Cell Theory"
    },
    {
      "id": "Animal Vs Plant Cells 005",
      "keywords": [
        "anatomy_cells",
        "anatomy_protein",
        "biochemistry_glucose",
        "anatomy_plant_cells",
        "anatomy_animal_cells"
      ],
      "correct": 2,
      "answers": [
        "Glycogen",
        "Cellulose",
        "Starch",
        "Protein"
      ],
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.106Z",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.456Z",
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_cells",
        "anatomy_protein",
        "biochemistry_glucose",
        "anatomy_plant_cells",
        "anatomy_animal_cells"
      ],
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "question": "Plant cells store energy primarily as:",
      "short_explanation": "Plant cells store energy in the form of starch.",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "unit": "animal vs plant cells",
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "long_explanation": "Starch is a polysaccharide that serves as the primary storage form of carbohydrates in plants. It is composed of glucose units and is stored in plastids. This contrasts with animal cells, which store energy as glycogen.",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "distractors": []
    },
    {
      "id": "Animal Vs Plant Cells 006",
      "long_explanation": "Turgor pressure is the internal pressure of the plant cell against the cell wall, which keeps the cell rigid. The large central vacuole fills with water and exerts pressure outward, supporting structural integrity and preventing wilting.",
      "tags": [
        "technique_pressure",
        "anatomy_membrane",
        "anatomy_cells",
        "anatomy_nucleus",
        "concept_integration",
        "anatomy_plant_cells"
      ],
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "correct": 3,
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.106Z",
      "answers": [
        "Nucleus",
        "Golgi body",
        "Cell membrane",
        "Large central vacuole"
      ],
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "distractors": [],
      "question": "Which structure helps maintain turgor pressure in plant cells?",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.456Z",
      "keywords": [
        "technique_pressure",
        "anatomy_membrane",
        "anatomy_cells",
        "anatomy_nucleus",
        "concept_integration",
        "anatomy_plant_cells"
      ],
      "short_explanation": "The large central vacuole maintains turgor pressure in plant cells.",
      "unit": "animal vs plant cells",
      "category": "Cell Theory"
    },
    {
      "id": "Animal Vs Plant Cells 007",
      "question": "Animal cells are typically more ______ in shape compared to plant cells.",
      "long_explanation": "Unlike plant cells, which are rigid and box-like due to their cell walls, animal cells lack this rigid support. This allows animal cells to adopt a wider variety of shapes and interact more flexibly with their environment.",
      "unit": "animal vs plant cells",
      "keywords": [
        "anatomy_cells",
        "anatomy_plant_cells",
        "anatomy_animal_cells"
      ],
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "correct": 2,
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "answers": [
        "Rigid",
        "Regular",
        "Round and flexible",
        "Box-like"
      ],
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "short_explanation": "Animal cells are generally rounder and more flexible in shape.",
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.106Z",
      "distractors": [],
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.456Z",
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_cells",
        "anatomy_plant_cells",
        "anatomy_animal_cells"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "Animal Vs Plant Cells 008",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.106Z",
      "question": "What provides structural support in plant cells?",
      "unit": "animal vs plant cells",
      "correct": 2,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.456Z",
      "keywords": [
        "technique_pressure",
        "anatomy_membrane",
        "anatomy_cells",
        "purpose_stress",
        "anatomy_plant_cells"
      ],
      "distractors": [],
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "short_explanation": "The cell wall provides structural support in plant cells.",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "tags": [
        "technique_pressure",
        "anatomy_membrane",
        "anatomy_cells",
        "purpose_stress",
        "anatomy_plant_cells"
      ],
      "long_explanation": "Made primarily of cellulose, the plant cell wall is a tough, fibrous layer that surrounds the cell membrane. It maintains the plant cell’s shape, resists osmotic pressure, and provides protection against mechanical stress.",
      "answers": [
        "Cell membrane",
        "Chloroplast",
        "Cell wall",
        "Mitochondria"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "Animal Vs Plant Cells 009",
      "long_explanation": "Mitochondria are the site of cellular respiration in eukaryotic cells. They convert glucose and oxygen into ATP, the usable energy currency of the cell. Both plant and animal cells rely on mitochondria to meet their energy needs.",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.106Z",
      "answers": [
        "Chloroplast",
        "Mitochondria",
        "Golgi complex",
        "Smooth ER"
      ],
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_cells",
        "biology_eukaryotic",
        "biochemistry_glucose",
        "anatomy_animal_cells"
      ],
      "keywords": [
        "anatomy_cells",
        "biology_eukaryotic",
        "biochemistry_glucose",
        "anatomy_animal_cells"
      ],
      "question": "Which structure is used for energy conversion in both plant and animal cells?",
      "distractors": [],
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.456Z",
      "short_explanation": "The mitochondria convert energy in both plant and animal cells.",
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "unit": "animal vs plant cells",
      "correct": 1
    },
    {
      "id": "Animal Vs Plant Cells 010",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.456Z",
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "correct": 2,
      "answers": [
        "Ribosome",
        "Mitochondrion",
        "Cell wall",
        "Lysosome"
      ],
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.106Z",
      "keywords": [
        "anatomy_membrane",
        "anatomy_cells",
        "microbiology_bacteria",
        "microbiology_fungi",
        "anatomy_ribosomes",
        "anatomy_plant_cells",
        "anatomy_animal_cells"
      ],
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "distractors": [],
      "short_explanation": "Animal cells do not have a cell wall.",
      "unit": "animal vs plant cells",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "question": "Which of the following structures is not present in most animal cells?",
      "long_explanation": "The cell wall is unique to plant cells and some fungi and bacteria. Animal cells have only a flexible cell membrane, which allows for a wide range of shapes and movement. This absence of a cell wall is one of the main differences between animal and plant cells.",
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_membrane",
        "anatomy_cells",
        "microbiology_bacteria",
        "microbiology_fungi",
        "anatomy_ribosomes",
        "anatomy_plant_cells",
        "anatomy_animal_cells"
      ],
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0
    },
    {
      "id": "Fungi Vs Bacteria 001",
      "question": "Which of the following is true about bacteria?",
      "answers": [
        "They are multicellular organisms",
        "They have membrane-bound organelles",
        "They are prokaryotic and lack a nucleus",
        "They perform photosynthesis only"
      ],
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_membrane",
        "anatomy_nucleus",
        "anatomy_cytoplasm",
        "anatomy_organelle",
        "anatomy_organs",
        "microbiology_bacteria",
        "science_organization",
        "anatomy_genetic_material",
        "biology_prokaryotes",
        "biology_organisms"
      ],
      "applied_example": "Massage therapists must maintain strict hygiene practices to avoid transmitting pathogenic bacteria that may reside on the skin or surfaces, especially when working near open wounds or cuts.",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.117Z",
      "correct": 2,
      "long_explanation": "Bacteria are unicellular prokaryotes, meaning they lack a true nucleus and organelles. Their genetic material is free-floating in the cytoplasm. They can be beneficial (gut flora) or harmful (pathogens). Their simple structure allows rapid reproduction and adaptation.",
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "keywords": [
        "anatomy_membrane",
        "anatomy_nucleus",
        "anatomy_cytoplasm",
        "anatomy_organelle",
        "anatomy_organs",
        "microbiology_bacteria",
        "science_organization",
        "anatomy_genetic_material",
        "biology_prokaryotes",
        "biology_organisms"
      ],
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.461Z",
      "unit": "fungi vs bacteria",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "distractors": [],
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "short_explanation": "Bacteria are prokaryotic organisms that lack a nucleus and membrane-bound organelles."
    },
    {
      "id": "Fungi Vs Bacteria 002",
      "long_explanation": "Fungi are eukaryotic organisms with membrane-bound nuclei and organelles. Some are unicellular like yeast; others are multicellular like molds and mushrooms. They absorb nutrients from their environment and play roles in decomposition, food production, and disease.",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "keywords": [
        "anatomy_membrane",
        "anatomy_cells",
        "anatomy_organs",
        "microbiology_fungi",
        "science_organization",
        "biology_eukaryotic",
        "biology_prokaryotes",
        "biology_organisms"
      ],
      "correct": 2,
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "question": "Which of the following best describes fungi?",
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_membrane",
        "anatomy_cells",
        "anatomy_organs",
        "microbiology_fungi",
        "science_organization",
        "biology_eukaryotic",
        "biology_prokaryotes",
        "biology_organisms"
      ],
      "applied_example": "In massage therapy, fungal infections like athlete's foot are local contraindications. Therapists should avoid direct contact and follow infection control protocols.",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.461Z",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.117Z",
      "distractors": [],
      "unit": "fungi vs bacteria",
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "answers": [
        "Unicellular and photosynthetic",
        "Prokaryotic and anaerobic",
        "Eukaryotic and can be unicellular or multicellular",
        "Viruses with cell walls"
      ],
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "short_explanation": "Fungi are eukaryotic and can be unicellular (yeast) or multicellular (mold)."
    },
    {
      "id": "Fungi Vs Bacteria 003",
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "answers": [
        "Protista",
        "Monera",
        "Plantae",
        "Fungi"
      ],
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.461Z",
      "long_explanation": "The kingdom Fungi includes a wide variety of organisms like yeasts, molds, and mushrooms. Fungi are heterotrophs that absorb nutrients from organic matter and reproduce via spores. Unlike plants, they do not perform photosynthesis and have chitin in their cell walls.",
      "unit": "fungi vs bacteria",
      "correct": 3,
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "distractors": [],
      "short_explanation": "Fungi belong to the Fungi kingdom, distinct from plants, animals, and bacteria.",
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_cells",
        "anatomy_organs",
        "microbiology_bacteria",
        "microbiology_fungi",
        "science_organization",
        "biology_organisms"
      ],
      "applied_example": "Recognizing that fungi can colonize skin and nails helps therapists refer clients with suspicious lesions to medical professionals before treatment.",
      "keywords": [
        "anatomy_cells",
        "anatomy_organs",
        "microbiology_bacteria",
        "microbiology_fungi",
        "science_organization",
        "biology_organisms"
      ],
      "question": "Which kingdom do fungi belong to?",
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.117Z"
    },
    {
      "id": "Fungi Vs Bacteria 004",
      "distractors": [],
      "long_explanation": "Binary fission is a rapid asexual reproduction method where bacteria copy their DNA and split into two daughter cells. This allows exponential growth in favorable conditions and contributes to antibiotic resistance when not managed properly.",
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "short_explanation": "Bacteria reproduce asexually via binary fission-dividing into two identical cells.",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.461Z",
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "keywords": [
        "anatomy_cells",
        "microbiology_bacteria",
        "anatomy_dna"
      ],
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.117Z",
      "correct": 0,
      "applied_example": "Rapid bacterial reproduction underscores the importance of cleaning equipment and linens between clients to prevent cross-contamination.",
      "answers": [
        "Binary fission",
        "Budding",
        "Spore formation",
        "Sexual reproduction"
      ],
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_cells",
        "microbiology_bacteria",
        "anatomy_dna"
      ],
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "unit": "fungi vs bacteria",
      "question": "Bacteria reproduce primarily through which process?"
    },
    {
      "id": "Fungi Vs Bacteria 005",
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "distractors": [],
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "keywords": [
        "anatomy_cells",
        "microbiology_bacteria",
        "microbiology_fungi",
        "anatomy_component"
      ],
      "short_explanation": "Fungi have cell walls made of chitin, while bacteria lack this component.",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.117Z",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "applied_example": "When skin feels thickened or flaky due to fungal infection, massage should be avoided on that area to prevent spread or discomfort.",
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "long_explanation": "Fungi possess rigid cell walls composed of chitin, a tough polysaccharide also found in insect exoskeletons. Bacteria have cell walls made of peptidoglycan instead. This structural difference is key to identifying infections and determining appropriate treatments.",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.461Z",
      "question": "What structure is commonly found in fungi but not in bacteria?",
      "unit": "fungi vs bacteria",
      "correct": 2,
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_cells",
        "microbiology_bacteria",
        "microbiology_fungi",
        "anatomy_component"
      ],
      "answers": [
        "Peptidoglycan",
        "Chlorophyll",
        "Chitin cell walls",
        "Capsules"
      ],
      "topic": "cell_theory"
    },
    {
      "id": "Fungi Vs Bacteria 006",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "question": "Which of the following is true about bacterial cell walls?",
      "applied_example": "Clients undergoing antibiotic treatment for bacterial infections may experience fatigue or compromised immunity. Massage therapists should adjust intensity and monitor for signs of systemic infection.",
      "unit": "fungi vs bacteria",
      "short_explanation": "Bacterial cell walls contain peptidoglycan, a polymer unique to prokaryotes.",
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "keywords": [
        "anatomy_cells",
        "microbiology_fungi",
        "biology_prokaryotes",
        "anatomy_animal_cells",
        "concept_targeted"
      ],
      "distractors": [],
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.461Z",
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "correct": 2,
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.117Z",
      "answers": [
        "They are made of cellulose",
        "They lack a cell wall",
        "They are made of peptidoglycan",
        "They are identical to animal cells"
      ],
      "long_explanation": "Peptidoglycan provides structure and protection to bacterial cells and is a major target of antibiotics like penicillin. This molecule is not present in fungi or human cells, making it useful for selective bacterial targeting in treatments.",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_cells",
        "microbiology_fungi",
        "biology_prokaryotes",
        "anatomy_animal_cells",
        "concept_targeted"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "Fungi Vs Bacteria 007",
      "correct": 2,
      "short_explanation": "Ringworm is a fungal skin infection caused by dermatophytes, not bacteria or viruses.",
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.117Z",
      "distractors": [],
      "long_explanation": "Despite its name, ringworm is not caused by a worm but by a fungus that infects the outer skin layer, often creating a circular rash. It is contagious via skin contact or contaminated surfaces like towels, mats, or clothing.",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "answers": [
        "Virus",
        "Bacterium",
        "Fungus",
        "Protozoan"
      ],
      "question": "Which of the following organisms can cause ringworm?",
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_organs",
        "microbiology_bacteria",
        "science_organization",
        "biology_organisms",
        "organ_skin"
      ],
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "unit": "fungi vs bacteria",
      "applied_example": "If a client presents with ringworm, the affected area is a local contraindication. Massage should avoid the lesion, and strict hygiene protocols must be followed.",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.461Z",
      "keywords": [
        "anatomy_organs",
        "microbiology_bacteria",
        "science_organization",
        "biology_organisms",
        "organ_skin"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "Fungi Vs Bacteria 008",
      "long_explanation": "Streptococcus is a genus of Gram-positive bacteria that can cause strep throat, skin infections, or systemic illness. Symptoms include sore throat, fever, and swollen lymph nodes. Bacterial infections require different treatment than fungal infections.",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "keywords": [
        "condition_pain",
        "microbiology_bacteria",
        "microbiology_fungi",
        "condition_soreness",
        "population_athlete",
        "organ_skin"
      ],
      "correct": 1,
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "question": "Which of the following diseases is caused by bacteria?",
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.461Z",
      "applied_example": "Clients with systemic bacterial infections like strep throat should postpone massage until symptoms resolve, as full-body work may exacerbate fatigue or spread infection.",
      "tags": [
        "condition_pain",
        "microbiology_bacteria",
        "microbiology_fungi",
        "condition_soreness",
        "population_athlete",
        "organ_skin"
      ],
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.117Z",
      "unit": "fungi vs bacteria",
      "distractors": [],
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "answers": [
        "Athlete’s foot",
        "Strep throat",
        "Candidiasis",
        "Aspergillosis"
      ],
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "short_explanation": "Strep throat is caused by Streptococcus bacteria, not fungi."
    },
    {
      "id": "Fungi Vs Bacteria 009",
      "correct": 2,
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_membrane",
        "anatomy_cells",
        "anatomy_nucleus",
        "anatomy_organs",
        "microbiology_bacteria",
        "microbiology_fungi",
        "science_organization",
        "biology_eukaryotic",
        "biology_prokaryotes",
        "biology_organisms"
      ],
      "long_explanation": "Fungi have membrane-bound organelles, including a nucleus, mitochondria, and vacuoles. Bacteria, as prokaryotes, have no true nucleus. This fundamental difference impacts how each organism grows, reproduces, and responds to treatment.",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "short_explanation": "Fungi are eukaryotic and have nuclei; bacteria are prokaryotic and lack nuclei.",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "question": "Which is a key structural difference between fungi and bacteria?",
      "answers": [
        "Fungi have no cell membrane",
        "Bacteria have mitochondria",
        "Fungi have nuclei, bacteria do not",
        "Bacteria are larger than fungi"
      ],
      "keywords": [
        "anatomy_membrane",
        "anatomy_cells",
        "anatomy_nucleus",
        "anatomy_organs",
        "microbiology_bacteria",
        "microbiology_fungi",
        "science_organization",
        "biology_eukaryotic",
        "biology_prokaryotes",
        "biology_organisms"
      ],
      "distractors": [],
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.461Z",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "unit": "fungi vs bacteria",
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "applied_example": "Understanding these differences helps massage therapists recognize conditions more likely to be fungal or bacterial in origin when observing skin lesions or infections.",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.117Z"
    },
    {
      "id": "Fungi Vs Bacteria 010",
      "distractors": [],
      "answers": [
        "Photosynthesis",
        "Consuming other animals",
        "Absorption of organic material",
        "Engulfing bacteria"
      ],
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.117Z",
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_organs",
        "microbiology_bacteria",
        "microbiology_fungi",
        "science_organization",
        "biology_organisms"
      ],
      "unit": "fungi vs bacteria",
      "applied_example": "Fungal overgrowth on skin (e.g., yeast infections) is linked to moisture and poor airflow. Therapists should observe hygiene and avoid occlusive lotions in such cases.",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "keywords": [
        "anatomy_organs",
        "microbiology_bacteria",
        "microbiology_fungi",
        "science_organization",
        "biology_organisms"
      ],
      "question": "How do fungi primarily obtain nutrients?",
      "category": "Cell Theory",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "short_explanation": "Fungi absorb nutrients from organic material through external digestion.",
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.461Z",
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "long_explanation": "Fungi release enzymes into their environment to break down organic matter, then absorb the resulting nutrients. This decomposer role is vital in ecosystems. Unlike animals, fungi do not ingest or photosynthesize their food.",
      "correct": 2
    },
    {
      "id": "Transport 001",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.460Z",
      "mblex_category": "Anatomy & Physiology",
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_membrane",
        "biochemistry_concentration",
        "organ_lungs"
      ],
      "distractors": [],
      "source_reference": "Guyton and Hall Textbook of Medical Physiology",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.116Z",
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "correct": 0,
      "answers": [
        "Movement against a concentration gradient using ATP",
        "Movement along a concentration gradient without energy input",
        "Bulk flow through membrane pores",
        "Water movement through aquaporins only"
      ],
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "difficulty": "easy",
      "question": "What is the primary mechanism of movement in simple diffusion?",
      "subcategory": "Passive Transport",
      "short_explanation": "Simple diffusion moves molecules from high to low concentration without energy.",
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "question_type": "multiple_choice",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/cell_theory/diffusion_osmosis_filtration",
      "clinical_application": "Oxygen exchange in the lungs occurs via simple diffusion from alveoli into capillaries.",
      "long_explanation": "Simple diffusion involves the passive movement of molecules like oxygen or carbon dioxide across membranes. It requires no energy and occurs due to the natural tendency of molecules to spread out.",
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "category": "Physiology",
      "keywords": [
        "anatomy_membrane",
        "biochemistry_concentration",
        "organ_lungs"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "Transport 002",
      "subcategory": "Osmosis",
      "short_explanation": "Osmosis is the passive movement of water across a semipermeable membrane.",
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_membrane",
        "anatomy_cells",
        "anatomy_protein",
        "client",
        "concept_balance",
        "biochemistry_glucose",
        "biochemistry_concentration"
      ],
      "source_reference": "Human Anatomy & Physiology, Marieb",
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.116Z",
      "mblex_category": "Anatomy & Physiology",
      "category": "Physiology",
      "distractors": [],
      "clinical_application": "IV fluids use osmotic principles to rehydrate patients without disrupting cell balance.",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.460Z",
      "long_explanation": "Osmosis is a special type of diffusion involving water molecules. It occurs when water moves across a semipermeable membrane from an area of low solute concentration to high solute concentration to equalize concentrations.",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/cell_theory/diffusion_osmosis_filtration",
      "question_type": "multiple_choice",
      "question": "Osmosis specifically refers to the movement of what substance?",
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "answers": [
        "Salt",
        "Glucose",
        "Proteins",
        "Water"
      ],
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "difficulty": "easy",
      "keywords": [
        "anatomy_membrane",
        "anatomy_cells",
        "anatomy_protein",
        "client",
        "concept_balance",
        "biochemistry_glucose",
        "biochemistry_concentration"
      ],
      "correct": 0
    },
    {
      "id": "Transport 003",
      "source_reference": "Medical Physiology, Guyton",
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_membrane",
        "anatomy_protein",
        "anatomy_tissues",
        "biochemistry_glucose"
      ],
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.116Z",
      "keywords": [
        "anatomy_membrane",
        "anatomy_protein",
        "anatomy_tissues",
        "biochemistry_glucose"
      ],
      "question": "Which of the following best describes facilitated diffusion?",
      "category": "Physiology",
      "answers": [
        "Movement of water through a semipermeable membrane",
        "Movement of molecules using protein carriers without ATP",
        "Transport of ions through pumps requiring energy",
        "Bulk flow of fluid from capillaries to tissues"
      ],
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.460Z",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "correct": 0,
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/cell_theory/diffusion_osmosis_filtration",
      "question_type": "multiple_choice",
      "distractors": [],
      "long_explanation": "Facilitated diffusion is a passive transport mechanism where specific molecules, such as glucose, pass through membrane proteins that aid their movement without requiring energy.",
      "subcategory": "Facilitated Diffusion",
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "difficulty": "medium",
      "short_explanation": "Facilitated diffusion helps transport molecules using carrier proteins without energy.",
      "clinical_application": "Insulin increases glucose uptake by promoting facilitated diffusion through GLUT-4 transporters.",
      "mblex_category": "Anatomy & Physiology"
    },
    {
      "id": "Transport 004",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/cell_theory/diffusion_osmosis_filtration",
      "distractors": [],
      "source_reference": "Essentials of Human Physiology",
      "difficulty": "medium",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "long_explanation": "Filtration occurs when hydrostatic pressure forces water and solutes through a membrane, such as in kidney glomeruli or capillary exchange in tissues.",
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "keywords": [
        "technique_pressure",
        "anatomy_membrane",
        "anatomy_tissues",
        "anatomy_kidneys",
        "organ_kidneys"
      ],
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "clinical_application": "Filtration underlies blood pressure–based capillary exchange and kidney ultrafiltration.",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.116Z",
      "question": "What type of transport is involved when blood pressure forces plasma through capillary walls?",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.460Z",
      "answers": [
        "Osmosis",
        "Facilitated diffusion",
        "Filtration",
        "Active transport"
      ],
      "subcategory": "Capillary Dynamics",
      "question_type": "multiple_choice",
      "category": "Physiology",
      "mblex_category": "Anatomy & Physiology",
      "short_explanation": "Filtration is driven by hydrostatic pressure, pushing fluids across membranes.",
      "tags": [
        "technique_pressure",
        "anatomy_membrane",
        "anatomy_tissues",
        "anatomy_kidneys",
        "organ_kidneys"
      ],
      "correct": 0
    },
    {
      "id": "Transport 005",
      "subcategory": "Osmotic Balance",
      "mblex_category": "Anatomy & Physiology",
      "question_type": "multiple_choice",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "answers": [
        "Hypotonic extracellular fluid",
        "Isotonic IV fluid administration",
        "Hypertonic extracellular fluid",
        "High intracellular glucose"
      ],
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/cell_theory/diffusion_osmosis_filtration",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "category": "Physiology",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.460Z",
      "long_explanation": "When the extracellular fluid is hypertonic, water exits the cell to balance osmotic pressure. This can cause cellular shrinkage and dehydration.",
      "question": "Which condition can cause water to move out of cells via osmosis?",
      "keywords": [
        "technique_pressure",
        "anatomy_cells",
        "concept_balance",
        "anatomy_brain",
        "biochemistry_glucose",
        "biochemistry_concentration",
        "organ_brain"
      ],
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.116Z",
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "correct": 0,
      "clinical_application": "Hypertonic IV solutions are used in cases like cerebral edema to draw fluid out of brain cells.",
      "short_explanation": "A hypertonic solution draws water out of cells due to higher solute concentration outside.",
      "tags": [
        "technique_pressure",
        "anatomy_cells",
        "concept_balance",
        "anatomy_brain",
        "biochemistry_glucose",
        "biochemistry_concentration",
        "organ_brain"
      ],

      "source_reference": "Clinical Medicine Review",
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "distractors": []
    },
    {
      "id": "Transport 006",
      "difficulty": "hard",
      "mblex_category": "Anatomy & Physiology",
      "correct": 0,
      "long_explanation": "Aquaporins are integral membrane proteins that facilitate rapid, passive movement of water across cell membranes, crucial in tissues like kidneys and lungs.",
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "category": "Cell Biology",
      "source_reference": "Molecular Biology of the Cell",
      "short_explanation": "Aquaporins are channels that allow water to pass through membranes passively.",
      "question_type": "multiple_choice",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.460Z",
      "answers": [
        "They prevent water from entering cells",
        "They actively transport water using ATP",
        "They allow passive water flow across membranes",
        "They bind solutes and change membrane charge"
      ],
      "distractors": [],
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/cell_theory/diffusion_osmosis_filtration",

        "anatomy_membrane",

        "anatomy_membrane",

        "anatomy_protein",
        "anatomy_tissues",
        "concept_integration",
        "anatomy_kidneys",
        "organ_lungs",
        "organ_kidneys"
      ],
      "question": "What is the role of aquaporins in osmosis?",
      "difficulty_rating": 4,
      "subcategory": "Membrane Channels",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.116Z",
      "clinical_application": "Aquaporin dysfunction can impair fluid regulation in diseases like nephrogenic diabetes insipidus."
    },
    {
      "id": "Transport 007",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.116Z",
      "question_type": "multiple_choice",
      "correct": 0,

      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0,

      },
      "source_reference": "Human Anatomy & Physiology, Marieb",
      "answers": [
        "Simple diffusion",
        "Facilitated diffusion",

        "Sodium-potassium pump"
      ],
      "keywords": [
        "concept_targeted",
        "organ_heart"
      ],
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.460Z",
      "subcategory": "Transport Types",
      "long_explanation": "Passive transport does not require energy and includes diffusion, osmosis, and facilitated diffusion. Active transport, such as the sodium-potassium pump, uses ATP to move ions against gradients.",
      "clinical_application": "Cardiac drugs like digoxin target the sodium-potassium pump to affect heart function.",
      "tags": [
        "concept_targeted",
        "organ_heart"
      ],
      "mblex_category": "Anatomy & Physiology",
      "distractors": [],
      "short_explanation": "The sodium-potassium pump requires ATP and is active, not passive.",
      "topic": "cell_theory",
      "question": "Which of the following is NOT a passive transport mechanism?",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/cell_theory/diffusion_osmosis_filtration",
      "difficulty": "medium"
    },
    {
      "id": "Transport 008",
      "question": "In the kidneys, what process filters blood plasma into the nephron?",
      "clinical_application": "Reduced filtration rate is a key marker in chronic kidney disease and is measured as GFR.",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.116Z",
