      "topic": "licensing",
      "question": "Which marketing approach aligns best with ethical massage business practices? [Variant 13]",
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "category": "Professional Practice",
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations",
      "clinical_application": "Describe techniques and expected benefits based on professional standards, not hype.",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/licensing/ethics/business_practices",
      "short_explanation": "Ethical marketing requires honesty and accuracy.",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "subcategory": "Marketing",
      "correct": 2,
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "purpose_healing",
        "purpose_benefit",
        "ethics",
        "business_massage",
        "business_marketing"
      ],
      "answers": [
        "Using testimonials that guarantee healing",
        "Offering exaggerated claims about massage cures",
        "Using clear, honest descriptions of services and credentials",
        "Selling unrelated wellness products during sessions"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "Business 014",
      "answers": [
        "Free community services",
        "Monthly specials",
        "Business license and liability insurance",
        "A client membership program"
      ],
      "long_explanation": "Massage businesses must meet state and local requirements including licensure, zoning, and liability insurance. Operating without them may result in fines or license suspension.",
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "client",
        "business_massage",
        "ethics_documentation",
        "concept_localized"
      ],
      "correct": 2,
      "subcategory": "Legal Compliance",
      "relevance_score": 4,
      "clinical_application": "Keep all documents up to date and posted as required in your workspace.",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "category": "Professional Practice",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.467Z",
      "topic": "licensing",
      "short_explanation": "Legal operations require a license and business insurance.",
      "source_reference": "FSMTB and State Licensing Boards",
      "question": "Which of the following is required to legally operate a massage business? [Variant 14]",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/licensing/ethics/business_practices",
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.121Z",

        "malpractice insurance"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "Business 015",
      "correct": 2,
      "keywords": [
        "ethics",
        "client",
        "business_billing"
      ],
      "difficulty": "easy",
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "variant": 15,
      "topic": "licensing",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "clinical_application": "List all fees and billing policies on your website or intake forms.",
      "tags": [
        "ethics",
        "client",
        "business_billing"
      ],
      "question": "Which of the following is considered an ethical billing practice? [Variant 15]",
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations",
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Ethics and Business Practices",
      "distractors": [],
      "synonyms": [
        "honest invoicing",
        "posted rates"
      ],
      "category": "Professional Practice",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/licensing/ethics/business_practices",
      "subcategory": "Financial Practices",
      "answers": [
        "Charging different rates without disclosing",
        "Padding session time to increase fees",
        "Providing clear invoices and posted rates",
        "Accepting barter instead of payment"
      ],
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "short_explanation": "Clients deserve transparent and fair billing.",
      "long_explanation": "Ethical billing includes publishing your rates, explaining fees clearly, and invoicing honestly. Discrepancies or surprises undermine professional credibility.",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.467Z",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.122Z",
      "relevance_score": 3
    },
    {
      "id": "Business 016",
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.467Z",
      "correct": 2,
      "distractors": [],
      "clinical_application": "Maintain a secure and consistent method of documenting and storing session notes.",
      "subcategory": "Client Records",
      "question": "How long should a massage therapist typically retain client records? [Variant 16]",
      "long_explanation": "Best practices recommend storing client records for a minimum of 5 years after the last session. Some states require longer. This supports ethical accountability and legal protection.",
      "difficulty": "easy",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/licensing/ethics/business_practices",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "topic": "licensing",
      "category": "Professional Practice",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "synonyms": [
        "client chart",
        "massage records"
      ],
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "role_massage_therapist",
        "ethics_documentation",
        "documentation_client_records"
      ],
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.122Z",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "role_massage_therapist",
        "ethics_documentation",
        "documentation_client_records"
      ],
      "answers": [
        "6 months",
        "1 year",
        "At least 5 years or as required by state law",
        "Until the client stops scheduling sessions"
      ],
      "variant": 16,
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Business Guidelines",
      "short_explanation": "Records should be kept at least 5 years or longer if required by law.",
      "relevance_score": 2
    },
    {
      "id": "Business 017",
      "answers": [
        "Ignore the behavior and rebook anyway",
        "Allow one free no-show per month",
        "Have a written cancellation policy clients agree to in advance",
        "Double-book clients to compensate for missed appointments"
      ],
      "long_explanation": "Clear policies set expectations and reduce misunderstandings. Requiring clients to acknowledge your policy in writing supports professionalism and accountability.",
      "tags": [
        "client"
      ],
      "correct": 2,
      "subcategory": "Scheduling",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "clinical_application": "Include your cancellation terms on intake forms and remind clients periodically.",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "category": "Professional Practice",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.467Z",
      "topic": "licensing",
      "short_explanation": "Written policies protect both the therapist and the client relationship.",
      "source_reference": "AMTA Practice Management Tips",

        "Double-book clients to compensate for missed appointments"
      ],
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.122Z",
      "topic": "licensing",
      "long_explanation": "Clear policies set expectations and reduce misunderstandings. Requiring clients to acknowledge your policy in writing supports professionalism and accountability.",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.467Z",
      "source_reference": "AMTA Practice Management Tips",
      "short_explanation": "Written policies protect both the therapist and the client relationship.",
      "tags": [
        "client"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "Business 028",
      "long_explanation": "Massage therapists must avoid misleading claims about outcomes. Ethical marketing builds trust and adheres to both legal and professional guidelines.",
      "relevance_score": 2,
      "short_explanation": "Ethical marketing requires honesty and accuracy.",
      "subcategory": "Marketing",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.122Z",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "purpose_healing",
        "purpose_benefit",
        "ethics",
        "business_massage",
        "business_marketing"
      ],
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "variant": 28,
      "correct": 2,
      "clinical_application": "Describe techniques and expected benefits based on professional standards, not hype.",
      "answers": [
        "Using testimonials that guarantee healing",
        "Offering exaggerated claims about massage cures",
        "Using clear, honest descriptions of services and credentials",
        "Selling unrelated wellness products during sessions"
      ],
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.467Z",
      "category": "Professional Practice",
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Business and Ethics Module",
      "tags": [

      ],

      },

      ],

      "topic": "licensing",
      "last_answered_at": null
    },
    {
      "id": "Business 029",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "synonyms": [
        "business license",
        "malpractice insurance"
      ],
      "short_explanation": "Legal operations require a license and business insurance.",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.122Z",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.467Z",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "distractors": [],
      "question": "Which of the following is required to legally operate a massage business? [Variant 29]",
      "correct": 2,
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "client",
        "business_massage",
        "ethics_documentation",
        "concept_localized"
      ],
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "answers": [
        "Free community services",
        "Monthly specials",
        "Business license and liability insurance",
        "A client membership program"
      ],
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "category": "Professional Practice",
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations",
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "clinical_application": "Keep all documents up to date and posted as required in your workspace.",
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "client",
        "business_massage",
        "ethics_documentation",
        "concept_localized"
      ],
      "topic": "licensing",
      "long_explanation": "Massage businesses must meet state and local requirements including licensure, zoning, and liability insurance. Operating without them may result in fines or license suspension.",
      "source_reference": "FSMTB and State Licensing Boards",
      "subcategory": "Legal Compliance",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/licensing/ethics/business_practices",
      "variant": 29
    },
    {
      "id": "Business 030",
      "subcategory": "Financial Practices",
      "tags": [
        "ethics",
        "client",
        "business_billing"
      ],
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "category": "Professional Practice",
      "keywords": [
        "ethics",
        "client",
        "business_billing"
      ],
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/licensing/ethics/business_practices",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.467Z",
      "variant": 30,
      "relevance_score": 4,
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "clinical_application": "List all fees and billing policies on your website or intake forms.",
      "long_explanation": "Ethical billing includes publishing your rates, explaining fees clearly, and invoicing honestly. Discrepancies or surprises undermine professional credibility.",
      "question": "Which of the following is considered an ethical billing practice? [Variant 30]",
      "correct": 2,
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Ethics and Business Practices",
      "answers": [
        "Charging different rates without disclosing",
        "Padding session time to increase fees",
        "Providing clear invoices and posted rates",
        "Accepting barter instead of payment"
      ],
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations",
      "synonyms": [
        "honest invoicing",
        "posted rates"
      ],
      "topic": "licensing",
      "distractors": [],
      "difficulty": "easy",
      "short_explanation": "Clients deserve transparent and fair billing.",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.122Z",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0
    },
    {
      "id": "Business 031",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "role_massage_therapist",
        "ethics_documentation",
        "documentation_client_records"
      ],
      "topic": "licensing",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "category": "Professional Practice",
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "role_massage_therapist",
        "ethics_documentation",
        "documentation_client_records"
      ],
      "subcategory": "Client Records",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "difficulty": "easy",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/licensing/ethics/business_practices",
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "answers": [
        "6 months",
        "1 year",
        "At least 5 years or as required by state law",
        "Until the client stops scheduling sessions"
      ],
      "relevance_score": 3,
      "clinical_application": "Maintain a secure and consistent method of documenting and storing session notes.",
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Business Guidelines",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.467Z",
      "variant": 31,
      "long_explanation": "Best practices recommend storing client records for a minimum of 5 years after the last session. Some states require longer. This supports ethical accountability and legal protection.",
      "correct": 2,
      "synonyms": [
        "client chart",
        "massage records"
      ],
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations",
      "short_explanation": "Records should be kept at least 5 years or longer if required by law.",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.122Z",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "question": "How long should a massage therapist typically retain client records? [Variant 31]",
      "distractors": []
    },

      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations",
      "subcategory": "Client Records",
      "long_explanation": "Best practices recommend storing client records for a minimum of 5 years after the last session. Some states require longer. This supports ethical accountability and legal protection."
    },
    {
      "id": "Business 042",
      "long_explanation": "Clear policies set expectations and reduce misunderstandings. Requiring clients to acknowledge your policy in writing supports professionalism and accountability.",
      "clinical_application": "Include your cancellation terms on intake forms and remind clients periodically.",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/licensing/ethics/business_practices",
      "tags": [
        "client"
      ],
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "subcategory": "Scheduling",
      "synonyms": [
        "rescheduling",
        "booking policy"
      ],
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations",
      "correct": 2,
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "topic": "licensing",
      "question": "What is a best practice for managing client no-shows or late cancellations? [Variant 42]",
      "variant": 42,
      "short_explanation": "Written policies protect both the therapist and the client relationship.",
      "answers": [
        "Ignore the behavior and rebook anyway",
        "Allow one free no-show per month",
        "Have a written cancellation policy clients agree to in advance",
        "Double-book clients to compensate for missed appointments"
      ],
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.467Z",
      "category": "Professional Practice",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.122Z",

      "short_explanation": "Legal operations require a license and business insurance.",
      "topic": "licensing",
      "stats": {

        "attempts": 0
      },
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "question": "Which of the following is required to legally operate a massage business? [Variant 44]",
      "clinical_application": "Keep all documents up to date and posted as required in your workspace.",
      "category": "Professional Practice",
      "distractors": [],
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "subcategory": "Legal Compliance",
      "relevance_score": 2,
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "correct": 2,
      "answers": [
        "Free community services",
        "Monthly specials",
        "Business license and liability insurance",
        "A client membership program"
      ],
      "source_reference": "FSMTB and State Licensing Boards",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.122Z",
      "long_explanation": "Massage businesses must meet state and local requirements including licensure, zoning, and liability insurance. Operating without them may result in fines or license suspension.",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.467Z",
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations",
      "variant": 44
    },

      },
      "answers": [

      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/licensing/ethics/business_practices",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.467Z",
      "question": "Which of the following is considered an ethical billing practice? [Variant 45]",
      "correct": 2,
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.122Z",
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Ethics and Business Practices",
      "long_explanation": "Ethical billing includes publishing your rates, explaining fees clearly, and invoicing honestly. Discrepancies or surprises undermine professional credibility.",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "short_explanation": "Clients deserve transparent and fair billing.",

    {
      "id": "Ethics 001",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.469Z",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "modality_swedish",
        "purpose_relaxation",
        "client",
        "role_massage_therapist",
        "ethics_scope_of_practice",
        "ethics_documentation",
        "practice_scope"
      ],
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations",
      "topic": "licensing",
      "synonyms": [
        "scope violation",
        "misdiagnosis"
      ],
      "answers": [
        "Referring a client to a physical therapist",
        "Diagnosing a musculoskeletal disorder",
        "Using Swedish massage for relaxation",
        "Educating clients about posture"
      ],
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/licensing/ethics/ethics",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.123Z",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "long_explanation": "Only licensed medical professionals may diagnose conditions. Massage therapists can observe and refer clients appropriately, but should not label or interpret pathology.",
      "subcategory": "Scope of Practice",
      "difficulty": "easy",
      "clinical_application": "Refer out when conditions appear outside your training. Document observations, not diagnoses.",
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Ethics and Scope Guidelines",
      "correct": 1,
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "modality_swedish",
        "purpose_relaxation",
        "client",
        "role_massage_therapist",
        "ethics_scope_of_practice",
        "ethics_documentation",
        "practice_scope"
      ],
      "distractors": [],
      "question": "Which of the following actions is outside the scope of practice for a massage therapist? [Variant 1]",
      "short_explanation": "Massage therapists are not permitted to diagnose medical conditions.",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "variant": 1,
      "category": "Professional Practice"
    },
    {
      "id": "Ethics 002",
      "short_explanation": "Stay within your scope and bring focus back to the massage.",
      "variant": 2,
      "synonyms": [
        "professional boundary",
        "therapist role"
      ],
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.123Z",
      "distractors": [],
      "relevance_score": 4,
      "answers": [
        "Offer counseling advice",
        "Redirect to the bodywork and stay present",
        "Encourage deeper emotional release",
        "Ask more personal questions"
      ],
      "clinical_application": "Have a referral list for mental health providers. Practice grounding techniques if the client becomes emotional.",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "ethics_boundary",
        "client",
        "concept_focus",
        "practice_scope"
      ],
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/licensing/ethics/ethics",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.469Z",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "subcategory": "Boundaries",
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "ethics_boundary",
        "client",
        "concept_focus",
        "practice_scope"
      ],
      "long_explanation": "Massage therapists must maintain professional boundaries. While compassionate listening is important, offering psychological advice or delving into trauma exceeds ethical scope and may harm the client.",
      "source_reference": "AMTA Code of Ethics; FSMTB Ethics Guide",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "correct": 1,
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "topic": "licensing",
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations",
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "question": "A client begins disclosing personal trauma during a session. What is the ethical response? [Variant 2]",
      "category": "Professional Practice"
    },
    {
      "id": "Ethics 003",
      "question": "What must occur before initiating any massage technique or session? [Variant 3]",
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "ethics_consent",
        "client",
        "ethics_informed_consent",
        "plan_plan"
      ],
      "distractors": [],
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Candidate Handbook",
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "synonyms": [
        "permission",
        "consent process"
      ],
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "short_explanation": "Clients must give informed consent before treatment begins.",
      "long_explanation": "Informed consent means the client understands the treatment plan, has had all questions answered, and agrees voluntarily. Consent must be ongoing and can be withdrawn at any time.",
      "answers": [
        "The client must remove all clothing",
        "Verbal agreement from the therapist",
        "Informed consent from the client",
        "A note from the client’s physician"
      ],
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/licensing/ethics/ethics",
      "correct": 2,
      "variant": 3,
      "relevance_score": 3,
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "topic": "licensing",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "ethics_consent",
        "client",
        "ethics_informed_consent",
        "plan_plan"
      ],
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "category": "Professional Practice",
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations",
      "subcategory": "Informed Consent",
      "clinical_application": "Review any changes in treatment or client condition before each session.",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.469Z",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.123Z"
    },
    {
      "id": "Ethics 004",
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "short_explanation": "Romantic or sexual relationships with clients violate ethical guidelines.",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "category": "Professional Practice",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "answers": [
        "Accepting a tip from a client",
        "Massaging a family member occasionally",
        "Providing massage to a romantic partner",
        "Offering a discount for seniors"
      ],
      "variant": 4,
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "source_reference": "AMTA Code of Ethics",
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "question": "Which situation may represent an ethical conflict of interest? [Variant 4]",
      "long_explanation": "Engaging in dual relationships where romantic or emotional boundaries are blurred can compromise objectivity, safety, and trust in the therapeutic relationship.",
      "subcategory": "Dual Relationships",
      "relevance_score": 2,
      "distractors": [],
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.123Z",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "correct": 2,
      "synonyms": [
        "relationship ethics",
        "professional boundary"
      ],
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/licensing/ethics/ethics",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.469Z",
      "topic": "licensing",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "ethics_boundary",
        "client"
      ],
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations",
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "ethics_boundary",
        "client"
      ],
      "clinical_application": "Refer out if emotional entanglements arise or cannot be avoided."
    },
    {
      "id": "Ethics 005",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/licensing/ethics/ethics",
      "topic": "licensing",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "ethics_consent",
        "ethics_professional",
        "documentation_client_records"
      ],
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.469Z",
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "question": "Under HIPAA and professional ethics, client records must be: [Variant 5]",
      "correct": 3,
      "source_reference": "HIPAA Compliance for Massage Professionals; FSMTB Ethics Guide",
      "variant": 5,
      "distractors": [],
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "ethics_consent",
        "ethics_professional",
        "documentation_client_records"
      ],
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "synonyms": [
        "data protection",
        "secure documentation"
      ],
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "subcategory": "Client Confidentiality",
      "clinical_application": "Have a lockable storage system and restrict access to client files.",
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations",
      "answers": [
        "Shared with other therapists for peer review",
        "Destroyed after each session",
        "Accessible to the client's family without permission",
        "Kept secure and confidential at all times"
      ],
      "category": "Professional Practice",
      "difficulty": "easy",
      "short_explanation": "Client information must remain private and protected.",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.123Z",
      "long_explanation": "Massage therapists must maintain client confidentiality and only share health information with written consent. Records must be stored securely to meet legal and ethical standards.",
      "question_type": "multiple choice"
    },
    {
      "id": "Ethics 006",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "variant": 6,
      "question": "Which of the following actions is outside the scope of practice for a massage therapist? [Variant 6]",
      "relevance_score": 4,
      "synonyms": [
        "scope violation",
        "misdiagnosis"
      ],
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Ethics and Scope Guidelines",
      "correct": 1,
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "long_explanation": "Only licensed medical professionals may diagnose conditions. Massage therapists can observe and refer clients appropriately, but should not label or interpret pathology.",
      "difficulty": "easy",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "modality_swedish",
        "purpose_relaxation",
        "client",
        "role_massage_therapist",
        "ethics_scope_of_practice",
        "ethics_documentation",
        "practice_scope"
      ],
      "short_explanation": "Massage therapists are not permitted to diagnose medical conditions.",
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "modality_swedish",
        "purpose_relaxation",
        "client",
        "role_massage_therapist",
        "ethics_scope_of_practice",
        "ethics_documentation",
        "practice_scope"
      ],
      "topic": "licensing",
      "clinical_application": "Refer out when conditions appear outside your training. Document observations, not diagnoses.",
      "distractors": [],
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.123Z",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/licensing/ethics/ethics",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "category": "Professional Practice",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.469Z",
      "answers": [
        "Referring a client to a physical therapist",
        "Diagnosing a musculoskeletal disorder",
        "Using Swedish massage for relaxation",
        "Educating clients about posture"
      ],
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations",
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "subcategory": "Scope of Practice"
    },
    {
      "id": "Ethics 007",
      "short_explanation": "Stay within your scope and bring focus back to the massage.",
      "synonyms": [
        "professional boundary",
        "therapist role"
      ],
      "topic": "licensing",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "clinical_application": "Have a referral list for mental health providers. Practice grounding techniques if the client becomes emotional.",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.469Z",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.123Z",
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "question": "A client begins disclosing personal trauma during a session. What is the ethical response? [Variant 7]",
      "subcategory": "Boundaries",
      "source_reference": "AMTA Code of Ethics; FSMTB Ethics Guide",
      "relevance_score": 3,
      "category": "Professional Practice",
      "correct": 1,
      "long_explanation": "Massage therapists must maintain professional boundaries. While compassionate listening is important, offering psychological advice or delving into trauma exceeds ethical scope and may harm the client.",
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "ethics_boundary",
        "client",
        "concept_focus",
        "practice_scope"
      ],
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "ethics_boundary",
        "client",
        "concept_focus",
        "practice_scope"
      ],
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/licensing/ethics/ethics",
      "answers": [
        "Offer counseling advice",
        "Redirect to the bodywork and stay present",
        "Encourage deeper emotional release",
        "Ask more personal questions"
      ],
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "variant": 7,
      "distractors": [],
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0
    },
    {
      "id": "Ethics 008",
      "question": "What must occur before initiating any massage technique or session? [Variant 8]",
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.123Z",
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "ethics_consent",
        "client",
        "ethics_informed_consent",
        "plan_plan"
      ],
      "category": "Professional Practice",
      "variant": 8,
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "topic": "licensing",
      "short_explanation": "Clients must give informed consent before treatment begins.",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/licensing/ethics/ethics",
      "relevance_score": 2,
      "answers": [
        "The client must remove all clothing",
        "Verbal agreement from the therapist",
        "Informed consent from the client",
        "A note from the client’s physician"
      ],
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Candidate Handbook",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "correct": 2,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.469Z",
      "long_explanation": "Informed consent means the client understands the treatment plan, has had all questions answered, and agrees voluntarily. Consent must be ongoing and can be withdrawn at any time.",
      "synonyms": [
        "permission",
        "consent process"
      ],
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "distractors": [],
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "subcategory": "Informed Consent",
      "clinical_application": "Review any changes in treatment or client condition before each session.",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "ethics_consent",
        "client",
        "ethics_informed_consent",
        "plan_plan"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "Ethics 009",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "correct": 2,
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/licensing/ethics/ethics",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "ethics_boundary",
        "client"
      ],
      "answers": [
        "Accepting a tip from a client",
        "Massaging a family member occasionally",
        "Providing massage to a romantic partner",
        "Offering a discount for seniors"
      ],
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "ethics_boundary",
        "client"
      ],
      "category": "Professional Practice",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.469Z",
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "variant": 9,
      "synonyms": [
        "relationship ethics",
        "professional boundary"
      ],
      "topic": "licensing",
      "short_explanation": "Romantic or sexual relationships with clients violate ethical guidelines.",
      "distractors": [],
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "source_reference": "AMTA Code of Ethics",
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "question": "Which situation may represent an ethical conflict of interest? [Variant 9]",
      "long_explanation": "Engaging in dual relationships where romantic or emotional boundaries are blurred can compromise objectivity, safety, and trust in the therapeutic relationship.",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "subcategory": "Dual Relationships",
      "clinical_application": "Refer out if emotional entanglements arise or cannot be avoided.",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.123Z"
    },
    {
      "id": "Ethics 010",
      "long_explanation": "Massage therapists must maintain client confidentiality and only share health information with written consent. Records must be stored securely to meet legal and ethical standards.",
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "ethics_consent",
        "ethics_professional",
        "documentation_client_records"
      ],
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations",
      "variant": 10,
      "answers": [
        "Shared with other therapists for peer review",
        "Destroyed after each session",
        "Accessible to the client's family without permission",
        "Kept secure and confidential at all times"
      ],
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/licensing/ethics/ethics",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "short_explanation": "Client information must remain private and protected.",
      "clinical_application": "Have a lockable storage system and restrict access to client files.",
      "distractors": [],

      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.123Z",
      "long_explanation": "Engaging in dual relationships where romantic or emotional boundaries are blurred can compromise objectivity, safety, and trust in the therapeutic relationship.",
      "distractors": [],
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/licensing/ethics/ethics",
      "source_reference": "AMTA Code of Ethics",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "correct": 2,
      "subcategory": "Dual Relationships",
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "ethics_boundary",
        "client"
      ],
      "category": "Professional Practice",
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "answers": [
        "Accepting a tip from a client",
        "Massaging a family member occasionally",
        "Providing massage to a romantic partner",
        "Offering a discount for seniors"
      ],
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "topic": "licensing",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.469Z"
    },
    {
      "id": "Ethics 020",
      "relevance_score": 2,
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "ethics_consent",
        "ethics_professional",
        "documentation_client_records"
      ],
      "correct": 3,
      "answers": [
        "Shared with other therapists for peer review",
        "Destroyed after each session",
        "Accessible to the client's family without permission",
        "Kept secure and confidential at all times"
      ],
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations",
      "difficulty": "easy",
      "question": "Under HIPAA and professional ethics, client records must be: [Variant 20]",
      "topic": "licensing",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.123Z",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/licensing/ethics/ethics",
      "distractors": [],
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "source_reference": "HIPAA Compliance for Massage Professionals; FSMTB Ethics Guide",
      "short_explanation": "Client information must remain private and protected.",
      "subcategory": "Client Confidentiality",
      "clinical_application": "Have a lockable storage system and restrict access to client files.",
      "variant": 20,
      "synonyms": [
        "data protection",
        "secure documentation"
      ],
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "long_explanation": "Massage therapists must maintain client confidentiality and only share health information with written consent. Records must be stored securely to meet legal and ethical standards.",
      "category": "Professional Practice",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "ethics_consent",
        "ethics_professional",
        "documentation_client_records"
      ],
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.469Z",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0
    },
    {
      "id": "Ethics 021",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/licensing/ethics/ethics",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "subcategory": "Scope of Practice",
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "variant": 21,
      "answers": [
        "Referring a client to a physical therapist",
        "Diagnosing a musculoskeletal disorder",
        "Using Swedish massage for relaxation",
        "Educating clients about posture"
      ],
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.123Z",
      "long_explanation": "Only licensed medical professionals may diagnose conditions. Massage therapists can observe and refer clients appropriately, but should not label or interpret pathology.",
      "distractors": [],
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "modality_swedish",
        "purpose_relaxation",
        "client",
        "role_massage_therapist",
        "ethics_scope_of_practice",
        "ethics_documentation",
        "practice_scope"
      ],
      "short_explanation": "Massage therapists are not permitted to diagnose medical conditions.",
      "question": "Which of the following actions is outside the scope of practice for a massage therapist? [Variant 21]",
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations",
      "category": "Professional Practice",
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "modality_swedish",
        "purpose_relaxation",
        "client",
        "role_massage_therapist",
        "ethics_scope_of_practice",
        "ethics_documentation",
        "practice_scope"
      ],
      "topic": "licensing",
      "difficulty": "easy",
      "clinical_application": "Refer out when conditions appear outside your training. Document observations, not diagnoses.",
      "synonyms": [
        "scope violation",
        "misdiagnosis"
      ],
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.469Z",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "correct": 1,
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Ethics and Scope Guidelines"
    },
    {
      "id": "Ethics 022",
      "answers": [
        "Offer counseling advice",
        "Redirect to the bodywork and stay present",
        "Encourage deeper emotional release",
        "Ask more personal questions"
      ],
      "topic": "licensing",
      "distractors": [],
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/licensing/ethics/ethics",
      "relevance_score": 4,
      "subcategory": "Boundaries",
      "question": "A client begins disclosing personal trauma during a session. What is the ethical response? [Variant 22]",
      "clinical_application": "Have a referral list for mental health providers. Practice grounding techniques if the client becomes emotional.",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.123Z",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "ethics_boundary",
        "client",
        "concept_focus",
        "practice_scope"
      ],
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "correct": 1,
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "ethics_boundary",
        "client",
        "concept_focus",
        "practice_scope"
      ],
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.469Z",
      "source_reference": "AMTA Code of Ethics; FSMTB Ethics Guide",
      "variant": 22,
      "long_explanation": "Massage therapists must maintain professional boundaries. While compassionate listening is important, offering psychological advice or delving into trauma exceeds ethical scope and may harm the client.",
      "category": "Professional Practice",
      "synonyms": [
        "professional boundary",
        "therapist role"
      ],
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "short_explanation": "Stay within your scope and bring focus back to the massage."
    },
    {
      "id": "Ethics 023",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "clinical_application": "Review any changes in treatment or client condition before each session.",
      "short_explanation": "Clients must give informed consent before treatment begins.",
      "category": "Professional Practice",
      "distractors": [],
      "answers": [
        "The client must remove all clothing",
        "Verbal agreement from the therapist",
        "Informed consent from the client",
        "A note from the client’s physician"
      ],
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.123Z",
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations",
      "subcategory": "Informed Consent",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "ethics_consent",
        "client",
        "ethics_informed_consent",
        "plan_plan"
      ],
      "topic": "licensing",
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "ethics_consent",
        "client",
        "ethics_informed_consent",
        "plan_plan"
      ],
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "question": "What must occur before initiating any massage technique or session? [Variant 23]",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/licensing/ethics/ethics",
      "correct": 2,
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "long_explanation": "Informed consent means the client understands the treatment plan, has had all questions answered, and agrees voluntarily. Consent must be ongoing and can be withdrawn at any time.",
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Candidate Handbook",
      "variant": 23,
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "relevance_score": 3,
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "synonyms": [
        "permission",
        "consent process"
      ],
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.469Z"
    },

        "concept_focus",
        "practice_scope"
      ],
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "ethics_boundary",
        "client",
        "concept_focus",
        "practice_scope"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "Ethics 033",
      "question": "What must occur before initiating any massage technique or session? [Variant 33]",
      "distractors": [],
      "correct": 2,
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/licensing/ethics/ethics",
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "ethics_consent",
        "client",
        "ethics_informed_consent",
        "plan_plan"
      ],
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.469Z",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "category": "Professional Practice",
      "topic": "licensing",
      "variant": 33,
      "clinical_application": "Review any changes in treatment or client condition before each session.",
      "short_explanation": "Clients must give informed consent before treatment begins.",
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "subcategory": "Informed Consent",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "long_explanation": "Informed consent means the client understands the treatment plan, has had all questions answered, and agrees voluntarily. Consent must be ongoing and can be withdrawn at any time.",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Candidate Handbook",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "ethics_consent",
        "client",
        "ethics_informed_consent",
        "plan_plan"
      ],
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "answers": [
        "The client must remove all clothing",
        "Verbal agreement from the therapist",
        "Informed consent from the client",
        "A note from the client’s physician"
      ],
      "synonyms": [
        "permission",
        "consent process"
      ],
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.123Z",
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations"
    },
    {
      "id": "Ethics 034",
      "variant": 34,
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "clinical_application": "Refer out if emotional entanglements arise or cannot be avoided.",
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "ethics_boundary",
        "client"
      ],
      "category": "Professional Practice",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.123Z",
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "ethics_boundary",
        "client"
      ],
      "answers": [
        "Accepting a tip from a client",
        "Massaging a family member occasionally",
        "Providing massage to a romantic partner",
        "Offering a discount for seniors"
      ],
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "short_explanation": "Romantic or sexual relationships with clients violate ethical guidelines.",
      "distractors": [],
      "correct": 2,
      "question": "Which situation may represent an ethical conflict of interest? [Variant 34]",
      "topic": "licensing",
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "subcategory": "Dual Relationships",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/licensing/ethics/ethics",
      "source_reference": "AMTA Code of Ethics",
      "long_explanation": "Engaging in dual relationships where romantic or emotional boundaries are blurred can compromise objectivity, safety, and trust in the therapeutic relationship.",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.469Z",
      "relevance_score": 4,
      "synonyms": [
        "relationship ethics",
        "professional boundary"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "Ethics 035",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/licensing/ethics/ethics",
      "distractors": [],
      "subcategory": "Client Confidentiality",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "ethics_consent",
        "ethics_professional",
        "documentation_client_records"
      ],
      "clinical_application": "Have a lockable storage system and restrict access to client files.",
      "correct": 3,
      "relevance_score": 3,
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "difficulty": "easy",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "variant": 35,
      "source_reference": "HIPAA Compliance for Massage Professionals; FSMTB Ethics Guide",
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "short_explanation": "Client information must remain private and protected.",
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations",
      "answers": [
        "Shared with other therapists for peer review",
        "Destroyed after each session",
        "Accessible to the client's family without permission",
        "Kept secure and confidential at all times"
      ],
      "category": "Professional Practice",
      "synonyms": [
        "data protection",
        "secure documentation"
      ],
      "question": "Under HIPAA and professional ethics, client records must be: [Variant 35]",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.469Z",
      "topic": "licensing",
      "long_explanation": "Massage therapists must maintain client confidentiality and only share health information with written consent. Records must be stored securely to meet legal and ethical standards.",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.123Z",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "ethics_consent",
        "ethics_professional",
        "documentation_client_records"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "Ethics 036",
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Ethics and Scope Guidelines",
      "answers": [
        "Referring a client to a physical therapist",
        "Diagnosing a musculoskeletal disorder",
        "Using Swedish massage for relaxation",
        "Educating clients about posture"
      ],
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations",
      "category": "Professional Practice",
      "difficulty": "easy",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "short_explanation": "Massage therapists are not permitted to diagnose medical conditions.",
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "topic": "licensing",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "subcategory": "Scope of Practice",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/licensing/ethics/ethics",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.469Z",
      "relevance_score": 2,
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.123Z",
      "clinical_application": "Refer out when conditions appear outside your training. Document observations, not diagnoses.",
      "correct": 1,
      "variant": 36,
      "question": "Which of the following actions is outside the scope of practice for a massage therapist? [Variant 36]",
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "synonyms": [
        "scope violation",
        "misdiagnosis"
      ],
      "distractors": [],
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "modality_swedish",
        "purpose_relaxation",
        "client",
        "role_massage_therapist",
        "ethics_scope_of_practice",
        "ethics_documentation",
        "practice_scope"
      ],
      "long_explanation": "Only licensed medical professionals may diagnose conditions. Massage therapists can observe and refer clients appropriately, but should not label or interpret pathology.",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "modality_swedish",
        "purpose_relaxation",
        "client",
        "role_massage_therapist",
        "ethics_scope_of_practice",
        "ethics_documentation",
        "practice_scope"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "Ethics 037",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.123Z",
      "variant": 37,
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "ethics_boundary",
        "client",
        "concept_focus",
        "practice_scope"
      ],
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "topic": "licensing",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.469Z",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "ethics_boundary",
        "client",
        "concept_focus",
        "practice_scope"
      ],
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "source_reference": "AMTA Code of Ethics; FSMTB Ethics Guide",
      "subcategory": "Boundaries",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/licensing/ethics/ethics",
      "short_explanation": "Stay within your scope and bring focus back to the massage.",
      "long_explanation": "Massage therapists must maintain professional boundaries. While compassionate listening is important, offering psychological advice or delving into trauma exceeds ethical scope and may harm the client.",
      "distractors": [],
      "correct": 1,
      "synonyms": [
        "professional boundary",
        "therapist role"
      ],
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "category": "Professional Practice",
      "answers": [
        "Offer counseling advice",
        "Redirect to the bodywork and stay present",
        "Encourage deeper emotional release",
        "Ask more personal questions"
      ],
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "question": "A client begins disclosing personal trauma during a session. What is the ethical response? [Variant 37]",
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations",
      "clinical_application": "Have a referral list for mental health providers. Practice grounding techniques if the client becomes emotional."
    },

      "question": "Which of the following actions is outside the scope of practice for a massage therapist? [Variant 46]",
      "answers": [
        "Referring a client to a physical therapist",
        "Diagnosing a musculoskeletal disorder",
        "Using Swedish massage for relaxation",
        "Educating clients about posture"
      ],
      "variant": 46,
      "correct": 1,
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "modality_swedish",
        "purpose_relaxation",
        "client",
        "role_massage_therapist",
        "ethics_scope_of_practice",
        "ethics_documentation",
        "practice_scope"
      ],
      "short_explanation": "Massage therapists are not permitted to diagnose medical conditions.",
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Ethics and Scope Guidelines",
      "clinical_application": "Refer out when conditions appear outside your training. Document observations, not diagnoses.",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/licensing/ethics/ethics",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.469Z",
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "long_explanation": "Only licensed medical professionals may diagnose conditions. Massage therapists can observe and refer clients appropriately, but should not label or interpret pathology."
    },
    {
      "id": "Ethics 047",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "clinical_application": "Have a referral list for mental health providers. Practice grounding techniques if the client becomes emotional.",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.469Z",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "ethics_boundary",
        "client",
        "concept_focus",
        "practice_scope"
      ],
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/licensing/ethics/ethics",
      "topic": "licensing",
      "synonyms": [
        "professional boundary",
        "therapist role"
      ],
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "ethics_boundary",
        "client",
        "concept_focus",
        "practice_scope"
      ],
      "subcategory": "Boundaries",
      "source_reference": "AMTA Code of Ethics; FSMTB Ethics Guide",
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.123Z",
      "short_explanation": "Stay within your scope and bring focus back to the massage.",
      "relevance_score": 3,
      "answers": [
        "Offer counseling advice",
        "Redirect to the bodywork and stay present",
        "Encourage deeper emotional release",
        "Ask more personal questions"
      ],
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "correct": 1,
      "category": "Professional Practice",
      "question": "A client begins disclosing personal trauma during a session. What is the ethical response? [Variant 47]",
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "variant": 47,
      "distractors": [],
      "long_explanation": "Massage therapists must maintain professional boundaries. While compassionate listening is important, offering psychological advice or delving into trauma exceeds ethical scope and may harm the client.",
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations"
    },
    {
      "id": "Ethics 048",
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "variant": 48,
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "ethics_consent",
        "client",
        "ethics_informed_consent",
        "plan_plan"
      ],
      "subcategory": "Informed Consent",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/licensing/ethics/ethics",
      "question": "What must occur before initiating any massage technique or session? [Variant 48]",
      "category": "Professional Practice",
      "clinical_application": "Review any changes in treatment or client condition before each session.",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.123Z",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "ethics_consent",
        "client",
        "ethics_informed_consent",
        "plan_plan"
      ],
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.469Z",
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Candidate Handbook",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations",
      "short_explanation": "Clients must give informed consent before treatment begins.",
      "answers": [
        "The client must remove all clothing",
        "Verbal agreement from the therapist",
        "Informed consent from the client",
        "A note from the client’s physician"
      ],
      "relevance_score": 2,
      "topic": "licensing",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "correct": 2,
      "synonyms": [
        "permission",
        "consent process"
      ],
      "long_explanation": "Informed consent means the client understands the treatment plan, has had all questions answered, and agrees voluntarily. Consent must be ongoing and can be withdrawn at any time.",
      "distractors": [],
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "Ethics 049",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "short_explanation": "Romantic or sexual relationships with clients violate ethical guidelines.",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.123Z",
      "clinical_application": "Refer out if emotional entanglements arise or cannot be avoided.",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/licensing/ethics/ethics",
      "synonyms": [
        "relationship ethics",
        "professional boundary"
      ],
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "long_explanation": "Engaging in dual relationships where romantic or emotional boundaries are blurred can compromise objectivity, safety, and trust in the therapeutic relationship.",
      "distractors": [],
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "ethics_boundary",
        "client"
      ],
      "variant": 49,
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations",
      "subcategory": "Dual Relationships",
      "question": "Which situation may represent an ethical conflict of interest? [Variant 49]",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "source_reference": "AMTA Code of Ethics",
      "category": "Professional Practice",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "ethics_boundary",
        "client"
      ],
      "correct": 2,
      "answers": [
        "Accepting a tip from a client",
        "Massaging a family member occasionally",
        "Providing massage to a romantic partner",
        "Offering a discount for seniors"
      ],
      "topic": "licensing",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.469Z"
    },
    {
      "id": "Ethics 050",
      "subcategory": "Client Confidentiality",
      "variant": 50,
      "relevance_score": 4,
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "synonyms": [
        "data protection",
        "secure documentation"
      ],
      "topic": "licensing",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "category": "Professional Practice",
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "ethics_consent",
        "ethics_professional",
        "documentation_client_records"
      ],
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "short_explanation": "Client information must remain private and protected.",
      "clinical_application": "Have a lockable storage system and restrict access to client files.",
      "source_reference": "HIPAA Compliance for Massage Professionals; FSMTB Ethics Guide",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.123Z",
      "difficulty": "easy",
      "long_explanation": "Massage therapists must maintain client confidentiality and only share health information with written consent. Records must be stored securely to meet legal and ethical standards.",
      "distractors": [],
      "answers": [
        "Shared with other therapists for peer review",
        "Destroyed after each session",
        "Accessible to the client's family without permission",
        "Kept secure and confidential at all times"
      ],
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.469Z",
      "correct": 3,
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "ethics_consent",
        "ethics_professional",
        "documentation_client_records"
      ],
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/licensing/ethics/ethics",
      "question": "Under HIPAA and professional ethics, client records must be: [Variant 50]"
    },
    {
      "id": "Law 001",
      "source_reference": "FSMTB State Licensing Overview",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "client",
        "role_massage_therapist"
      ],
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.125Z",
      "distractors": [],
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations",
      "long_explanation": "Licensure ensures that therapists meet minimum educational, ethical, and safety standards. Practicing without a license can lead to fines or legal penalties.",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "category": "Professional Practice",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.471Z",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "client",
        "role_massage_therapist"
      ],
      "subcategory": "Licensure",
      "answers": [
        "A personal liability waiver",
        "A verbal contract with the client",
        "A state-issued massage license",
        "An employer recommendation letter"
      ],
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "variant": 1,
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "synonyms": [
        "practice license",
        "state credential"
      ],
      "question": "What is required before a massage therapist can legally begin practicing in most states? [Variant 1]",
      "clinical_application": "Keep your license active, visible, and compliant with all renewal and CEU requirements.",
      "topic": "licensing",
      "difficulty": "easy",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/licensing/ethics/laws_regulations",
      "short_explanation": "Massage therapists must be licensed to practice legally in most U.S. states.",
      "correct": 2
    },
    {
      "id": "Law 002",
      "subcategory": "Scope of Practice",
      "answers": [
        "Assessing muscular tension",
        "Providing nutritional counseling",
        "Applying Swedish massage techniques",
        "Documenting client posture observations"
      ],
      "keywords": [
        "modality_swedish",
        "purpose_stress",
        "exam_assessment",
        "client",
        "role_massage_therapist",
        "modality_massage_techniques",
        "ethics_scope_of_practice",
        "ethics_legal_scope",
        "ethics_documentation",
        "concept_tension",
        "practice_scope"
      ],
      "tags": [
        "modality_swedish",
        "purpose_stress",
        "exam_assessment",
        "client",
        "role_massage_therapist",
        "modality_massage_techniques",
        "ethics_scope_of_practice",
        "ethics_legal_scope",
        "ethics_documentation",
        "concept_tension",
        "practice_scope"
      ],
      "variant": 2,
      "short_explanation": "Nutrition advice is outside the legal scope for most massage therapists.",
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.471Z",
      "synonyms": [
        "scope of practice",
        "legal boundaries"
      ],
      "topic": "licensing",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "relevance_score": 4,
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/licensing/ethics/laws_regulations",
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Scope of Practice Standards",
      "correct": 1,
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations",
      "long_explanation": "Massage therapists must avoid activities that require a separate license, like prescribing supplements or diets. They should refer clients to registered dietitians for nutrition-related concerns.",
      "distractors": [],
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.125Z",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "question": "Which of the following actions is outside the legal scope of practice for a massage therapist? [Variant 2]",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "clinical_application": "When clients ask about supplements or diets, refer them to a qualified professional.",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "category": "Professional Practice"
    },
    {
      "id": "Law 003",
      "variant": 3,
      "relevance_score": 3,
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/licensing/ethics/laws_regulations",
      "subcategory": "Inspection & Compliance",
      "correct": 2,
      "long_explanation": "State massage boards are authorized to conduct inspections to ensure compliance with health, hygiene, and recordkeeping laws. Obstruction may lead to penalties or suspension.",
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "role_massage_therapist",
        "ethics_documentation"
      ],
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "short_explanation": "Massage therapists are legally obligated to comply with board inspections.",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "role_massage_therapist",
        "ethics_documentation"
      ],
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "topic": "licensing",
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Board Compliance Handbook",
      "category": "Professional Practice",
      "question": "What should a massage therapist do during an official inspection by a state board? [Variant 3]",
      "distractors": [],
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations",
      "synonyms": [
        "compliance audit",
        "regulatory visit"
      ],
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "clinical_application": "Keep documentation, hygiene, and license visibility up to date at all times.",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.125Z",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "answers": [
        "Refuse access until the next business day",
        "Only allow access with a subpoena",
        "Cooperate fully and provide requested documentation",
        "Immediately contact a lawyer before speaking"
      ],
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.471Z"
    },
    {
      "id": "Law 004",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "clinical_application": "Know your state’s mandatory reporting laws and have a referral protocol in place.",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.471Z",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "role_massage_therapist",
        "ethics_documentation"
      ],
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/licensing/ethics/laws_regulations",
      "topic": "licensing",
      "synonyms": [
        "legal reporting",
        "duty to report"
      ],
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "role_massage_therapist",
        "ethics_documentation"
      ],
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "subcategory": "Mandatory Reporting",
      "source_reference": "State Mandated Reporting Laws; FSMTB Ethics Code",
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "short_explanation": "Massage therapists are mandated reporters in many states.",
      "relevance_score": 2,
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.125Z",
      "answers": [
        "Document it quietly but take no action",
        "Confront the guardian immediately",
        "Report it to appropriate authorities as required by law",
        "Ignore it to avoid liability"
      ],
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "correct": 2,
      "category": "Professional Practice",
      "question": "What should a massage therapist do if a minor discloses abuse during a session? [Variant 4]",
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "variant": 4,
      "distractors": [],
      "long_explanation": "Laws vary by state, but therapists often have a duty to report suspected abuse of minors or vulnerable adults to child protective services or law enforcement.",
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations"
    },
    {
      "id": "Law 005",
      "short_explanation": "Accurate and confidential documentation protects both client and therapist.",
      "category": "Professional Practice",
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "difficulty": "easy",
      "tags": [
        "assessment_soap",
        "ethics_consent",
        "client",
        "ethics_documentation",
        "documentation_soap_notes"
      ],
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.125Z",
      "synonyms": [
        "secure records",
        "professional charting"
      ],
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/licensing/ethics/laws_regulations",
      "variant": 5,
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Documentation and Ethics Handbook",
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations",
      "keywords": [
        "assessment_soap",
        "ethics_consent",
        "client",
        "ethics_documentation",
        "documentation_soap_notes"
      ],
      "answers": [
        "Sharing SOAP notes with colleagues without permission",
        "Keeping clear, dated, and private client session notes",
        "Using client nicknames to protect identity",
        "Documenting only if the session was complex"
      ],
      "distractors": [],
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.471Z",
      "correct": 1,
      "clinical_application": "Use a consistent SOAP note format and follow HIPAA or equivalent privacy regulations.",
      "subcategory": "Documentation",
      "long_explanation": "Notes should include date, session summary, observations, and follow-up plans. All documentation must be stored securely and never shared without written consent.",
      "topic": "licensing",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "question": "Which of the following best reflects legally sound documentation practices? [Variant 5]",
      "difficulty_rating": 2
    },
    {

      "answers": [

        "An employer recommendation letter"
