      "question": "What should a massage therapist do if a minor discloses abuse during a session? [Variant 9]",
      "source_reference": "State Mandated Reporting Laws; FSMTB Ethics Code",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "clinical_application": "Know your state’s mandatory reporting laws and have a referral protocol in place.",
      "short_explanation": "Massage therapists are mandated reporters in many states.",
      "subcategory": "Mandatory Reporting"
    },
    {
      "id": "Law 010",
      "clinical_application": "Use a consistent SOAP note format and follow HIPAA or equivalent privacy regulations.",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.471Z",
      "question": "Which of the following best reflects legally sound documentation practices? [Variant 10]",
      "topic": "licensing",
      "variant": 10,
      "answers": [
        "Sharing SOAP notes with colleagues without permission",
        "Keeping clear, dated, and private client session notes",
        "Using client nicknames to protect identity",
        "Documenting only if the session was complex"
      ],
      "difficulty": "easy",
      "synonyms": [
        "secure records",
        "professional charting"
      ],
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "correct": 1,
      "subcategory": "Documentation",
      "tags": [
        "assessment_soap",
        "ethics_consent",
        "client",
        "ethics_documentation",
        "documentation_soap_notes"
      ],
      "long_explanation": "Notes should include date, session summary, observations, and follow-up plans. All documentation must be stored securely and never shared without written consent.",
      "category": "Professional Practice",
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "short_explanation": "Accurate and confidential documentation protects both client and therapist.",
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "distractors": [],
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/licensing/ethics/laws_regulations",
      "relevance_score": 4,
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.125Z",
      "keywords": [
        "assessment_soap",
        "ethics_consent",
        "client",
        "ethics_documentation",
        "documentation_soap_notes"
      ],
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Documentation and Ethics Handbook"
    },
    {
      "id": "Law 011",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.125Z",
      "short_explanation": "Massage therapists must be licensed to practice legally in most U.S. states.",
      "source_reference": "FSMTB State Licensing Overview",
      "answers": [
        "A personal liability waiver",
        "A verbal contract with the client",
        "A state-issued massage license",
        "An employer recommendation letter"
      ],
      "distractors": [],
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "client",
        "role_massage_therapist"
      ],
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "difficulty": "easy",
      "variant": 11,
      "long_explanation": "Licensure ensures that therapists meet minimum educational, ethical, and safety standards. Practicing without a license can lead to fines or legal penalties.",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "clinical_application": "Keep your license active, visible, and compliant with all renewal and CEU requirements.",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "relevance_score": 3,
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "client",
        "role_massage_therapist"
      ],
      "synonyms": [
        "practice license",
        "state credential"
      ],
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/licensing/ethics/laws_regulations",
      "question": "What is required before a massage therapist can legally begin practicing in most states? [Variant 11]",
      "subcategory": "Licensure",
      "topic": "licensing",
      "correct": 2,
      "category": "Professional Practice",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.471Z"
    },
    {
      "id": "Law 012",
      "long_explanation": "Massage therapists must avoid activities that require a separate license, like prescribing supplements or diets. They should refer clients to registered dietitians for nutrition-related concerns.",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "answers": [
        "Assessing muscular tension",
        "Providing nutritional counseling",
        "Applying Swedish massage techniques",
        "Documenting client posture observations"
      ],
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations",
      "relevance_score": 2,
      "distractors": [],
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.125Z",
      "correct": 1,
      "short_explanation": "Nutrition advice is outside the legal scope for most massage therapists.",
      "synonyms": [
        "scope of practice",
        "legal boundaries"
      ],
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.471Z",
      "subcategory": "Scope of Practice",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Scope of Practice Standards",
      "clinical_application": "When clients ask about supplements or diets, refer them to a qualified professional.",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/licensing/ethics/laws_regulations",
      "category": "Professional Practice",
      "variant": 12,
      "tags": [
        "modality_swedish",
        "purpose_stress",
        "exam_assessment",
        "client",
        "role_massage_therapist",
        "modality_massage_techniques",
        "ethics_scope_of_practice",
        "ethics_legal_scope",
        "ethics_documentation",
        "concept_tension",
        "practice_scope"
      ],
      "question": "Which of the following actions is outside the legal scope of practice for a massage therapist? [Variant 12]",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "topic": "licensing",
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "keywords": [
        "modality_swedish",
        "purpose_stress",
        "exam_assessment",
        "client",

        "modality_massage_techniques",
        "ethics_scope_of_practice",
        "ethics_legal_scope",
        "ethics_documentation",
        "concept_tension",
        "practice_scope"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "Law 013",
      "topic": "licensing",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.125Z",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.471Z",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "question": "What should a massage therapist do during an official inspection by a state board? [Variant 13]",
      "long_explanation": "State massage boards are authorized to conduct inspections to ensure compliance with health, hygiene, and recordkeeping laws. Obstruction may lead to penalties or suspension.",
      "category": "Professional Practice",
      "variant": 13,
      "distractors": [],
      "clinical_application": "Keep documentation, hygiene, and license visibility up to date at all times.",
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "role_massage_therapist",
        "ethics_documentation"
      ],
      "answers": [
        "Refuse access until the next business day",
        "Only allow access with a subpoena",
        "Cooperate fully and provide requested documentation",
        "Immediately contact a lawyer before speaking"
      ],
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/licensing/ethics/laws_regulations",
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "role_massage_therapist",
        "ethics_documentation"
      ],
      "short_explanation": "Massage therapists are legally obligated to comply with board inspections.",
      "correct": 2,
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Board Compliance Handbook",
      "subcategory": "Inspection & Compliance",
      "synonyms": [
        "compliance audit",
        "regulatory visit"
      ],
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "difficulty": "moderate"
    },
    {
      "id": "Law 014",
      "relevance_score": 4,
      "topic": "licensing",
      "source_reference": "State Mandated Reporting Laws; FSMTB Ethics Code",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.471Z",
      "long_explanation": "Laws vary by state, but therapists often have a duty to report suspected abuse of minors or vulnerable adults to child protective services or law enforcement.",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations",
      "question": "What should a massage therapist do if a minor discloses abuse during a session? [Variant 14]",

      "correct": 2,
      "variant": 14,
      "answers": [

        "Confront the guardian immediately",
        "Report it to appropriate authorities as required by law",
        "Ignore it to avoid liability"
      ],

    {
      "id": "Law 023",
      "clinical_application": "Keep documentation, hygiene, and license visibility up to date at all times.",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.471Z",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.125Z",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/licensing/ethics/laws_regulations",
      "category": "Professional Practice",
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "topic": "licensing",
      "synonyms": [
        "compliance audit",
        "regulatory visit"
      ],
      "relevance_score": 3,
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "role_massage_therapist",
        "ethics_documentation"
      ],
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Board Compliance Handbook",
      "long_explanation": "State massage boards are authorized to conduct inspections to ensure compliance with health, hygiene, and recordkeeping laws. Obstruction may lead to penalties or suspension.",
      "subcategory": "Inspection & Compliance",
      "answers": [
        "Refuse access until the next business day",
        "Only allow access with a subpoena",
        "Cooperate fully and provide requested documentation",
        "Immediately contact a lawyer before speaking"
      ],
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "variant": 23,
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "correct": 2,
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "distractors": [],
      "short_explanation": "Massage therapists are legally obligated to comply with board inspections.",
      "question": "What should a massage therapist do during an official inspection by a state board? [Variant 23]",
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "role_massage_therapist",
        "ethics_documentation"
      ],
      "difficulty": "moderate"
    },
    {
      "id": "Law 024",
      "subcategory": "Mandatory Reporting",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.125Z",
      "question": "What should a massage therapist do if a minor discloses abuse during a session? [Variant 24]",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "role_massage_therapist",
        "ethics_documentation"
      ],
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/licensing/ethics/laws_regulations",
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations",
      "answers": [
        "Document it quietly but take no action",
        "Confront the guardian immediately",
        "Report it to appropriate authorities as required by law",
        "Ignore it to avoid liability"
      ],
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "variant": 24,
      "category": "Professional Practice",
      "correct": 2,
      "source_reference": "State Mandated Reporting Laws; FSMTB Ethics Code",
      "short_explanation": "Massage therapists are mandated reporters in many states.",
      "relevance_score": 2,
      "synonyms": [
        "legal reporting",
        "duty to report"
      ],
      "distractors": [],
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "long_explanation": "Laws vary by state, but therapists often have a duty to report suspected abuse of minors or vulnerable adults to child protective services or law enforcement.",
      "topic": "licensing",
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "role_massage_therapist",
        "ethics_documentation"
      ],
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.471Z",
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "clinical_application": "Know your state’s mandatory reporting laws and have a referral protocol in place."
    },
    {
      "id": "Law 025",
      "tags": [
        "assessment_soap",
        "ethics_consent",
        "client",
        "ethics_documentation",
        "documentation_soap_notes"
      ],
      "variant": 25,
      "clinical_application": "Use a consistent SOAP note format and follow HIPAA or equivalent privacy regulations.",
      "short_explanation": "Accurate and confidential documentation protects both client and therapist.",
      "difficulty": "easy",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.471Z",
      "synonyms": [
        "secure records",
        "professional charting"
      ],
      "distractors": [],
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.125Z",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/licensing/ethics/laws_regulations",
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "question": "Which of the following best reflects legally sound documentation practices? [Variant 25]",
      "subcategory": "Documentation",
      "long_explanation": "Notes should include date, session summary, observations, and follow-up plans. All documentation must be stored securely and never shared without written consent.",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "keywords": [
        "assessment_soap",
        "ethics_consent",
        "client",
        "ethics_documentation",
        "documentation_soap_notes"
      ],
      "topic": "licensing",
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Documentation and Ethics Handbook",
      "category": "Professional Practice",
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "correct": 1,
      "answers": [
        "Sharing SOAP notes with colleagues without permission",
        "Keeping clear, dated, and private client session notes",
        "Using client nicknames to protect identity",
        "Documenting only if the session was complex"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "Law 026",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.471Z",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.125Z",
      "answers": [
        "A personal liability waiver",
        "A verbal contract with the client",
        "A state-issued massage license",
        "An employer recommendation letter"
      ],
      "question": "What is required before a massage therapist can legally begin practicing in most states? [Variant 26]",
      "subcategory": "Licensure",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "distractors": [],
      "category": "Professional Practice",
      "short_explanation": "Massage therapists must be licensed to practice legally in most U.S. states.",
      "correct": 2,
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "client",
        "role_massage_therapist"
      ],
      "variant": 26,
      "relevance_score": 4,
      "topic": "licensing",
      "clinical_application": "Keep your license active, visible, and compliant with all renewal and CEU requirements.",
      "synonyms": [
        "practice license",
        "state credential"
      ],
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "source_reference": "FSMTB State Licensing Overview",
      "difficulty": "easy",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/licensing/ethics/laws_regulations",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "client",
        "role_massage_therapist"
      ],
      "long_explanation": "Licensure ensures that therapists meet minimum educational, ethical, and safety standards. Practicing without a license can lead to fines or legal penalties.",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "Law 027",
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "clinical_application": "When clients ask about supplements or diets, refer them to a qualified professional.",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_swedish",
        "purpose_stress",
        "exam_assessment",
        "client",
        "role_massage_therapist",
        "modality_massage_techniques",
        "ethics_scope_of_practice",
        "ethics_legal_scope",
        "ethics_documentation",
        "concept_tension",
        "practice_scope"
      ],
      "category": "Professional Practice",
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.471Z",
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations",
      "question": "Which of the following actions is outside the legal scope of practice for a massage therapist? [Variant 27]",
      "tags": [
        "modality_swedish",
        "purpose_stress",
        "exam_assessment",
        "client",
        "role_massage_therapist",
        "modality_massage_techniques",
        "ethics_scope_of_practice",
        "ethics_legal_scope",
        "ethics_documentation",
        "concept_tension",
        "practice_scope"
      ],
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "subcategory": "Scope of Practice",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.125Z",
      "distractors": [],
      "topic": "licensing",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "short_explanation": "Nutrition advice is outside the legal scope for most massage therapists.",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "synonyms": [
        "scope of practice",
        "legal boundaries"
      ],
      "long_explanation": "Massage therapists must avoid activities that require a separate license, like prescribing supplements or diets. They should refer clients to registered dietitians for nutrition-related concerns.",
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Scope of Practice Standards",
      "correct": 1,
      "relevance_score": 3,
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/licensing/ethics/laws_regulations",
      "variant": 27,
      "answers": [
        "Assessing muscular tension",
        "Providing nutritional counseling",
        "Applying Swedish massage techniques",
        "Documenting client posture observations"
      ]
    },

        "practice license",
        "state credential"
      ],
      "distractors": [],
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "client",
        "role_massage_therapist"
      ],
      "topic": "licensing",
      "category": "Professional Practice",
      "subcategory": "Licensure",
      "difficulty": "easy",
      "answers": [
        "A personal liability waiver",
        "A verbal contract with the client",
        "A state-issued massage license",
        "An employer recommendation letter"
      ],
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.471Z",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.125Z",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/licensing/ethics/laws_regulations",
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "client",
        "role_massage_therapist"
      ],
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "correct": 2,
      "short_explanation": "Massage therapists must be licensed to practice legally in most U.S. states."
    },
    {
      "id": "Law 037",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/licensing/ethics/laws_regulations",
      "long_explanation": "Massage therapists must avoid activities that require a separate license, like prescribing supplements or diets. They should refer clients to registered dietitians for nutrition-related concerns.",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "distractors": [],
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.471Z",
      "correct": 1,
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Scope of Practice Standards",
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "clinical_application": "When clients ask about supplements or diets, refer them to a qualified professional.",
      "category": "Professional Practice",
      "synonyms": [
        "scope of practice",
        "legal boundaries"
      ],
      "subcategory": "Scope of Practice",
      "answers": [
        "Assessing muscular tension",
        "Providing nutritional counseling",
        "Applying Swedish massage techniques",
        "Documenting client posture observations"
      ],
      "topic": "licensing",
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations",
      "question": "Which of the following actions is outside the legal scope of practice for a massage therapist? [Variant 37]",
      "tags": [
        "modality_swedish",
        "purpose_stress",
        "exam_assessment",
        "client",
        "role_massage_therapist",
        "modality_massage_techniques",
        "ethics_scope_of_practice",
        "ethics_legal_scope",
        "ethics_documentation",
        "concept_tension",
        "practice_scope"
      ],
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "variant": 37,
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_swedish",
        "purpose_stress",
        "exam_assessment",
        "client",
        "role_massage_therapist",
        "modality_massage_techniques",
        "ethics_scope_of_practice",
        "ethics_legal_scope",
        "ethics_documentation",
        "concept_tension",
        "practice_scope"
      ],
      "short_explanation": "Nutrition advice is outside the legal scope for most massage therapists.",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.125Z"
    },
    {
      "id": "Law 038",
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Board Compliance Handbook",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "role_massage_therapist",
        "ethics_documentation"
      ],
      "clinical_application": "Keep documentation, hygiene, and license visibility up to date at all times.",
      "answers": [
        "Refuse access until the next business day",
        "Only allow access with a subpoena",
        "Cooperate fully and provide requested documentation",
        "Immediately contact a lawyer before speaking"
      ],
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/licensing/ethics/laws_regulations",
      "variant": 38,
      "short_explanation": "Massage therapists are legally obligated to comply with board inspections.",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.125Z",
      "relevance_score": 4,
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "subcategory": "Inspection & Compliance",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "role_massage_therapist",
        "ethics_documentation"
      ],
      "long_explanation": "State massage boards are authorized to conduct inspections to ensure compliance with health, hygiene, and recordkeeping laws. Obstruction may lead to penalties or suspension.",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.471Z",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "question": "What should a massage therapist do during an official inspection by a state board? [Variant 38]",
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "correct": 2,
      "synonyms": [
        "compliance audit",
        "regulatory visit"
      ],
      "distractors": [],
      "topic": "licensing",
      "category": "Professional Practice"
    },
    {
      "id": "Law 039",
      "clinical_application": "Know your state’s mandatory reporting laws and have a referral protocol in place.",
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations",
      "source_reference": "State Mandated Reporting Laws; FSMTB Ethics Code",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.125Z",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "topic": "licensing",
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "long_explanation": "Laws vary by state, but therapists often have a duty to report suspected abuse of minors or vulnerable adults to child protective services or law enforcement.",
      "answers": [
        "Document it quietly but take no action",
        "Confront the guardian immediately",
        "Report it to appropriate authorities as required by law",
        "Ignore it to avoid liability"
      ],
      "synonyms": [
        "legal reporting",
        "duty to report"
      ],
      "distractors": [],
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "correct": 2,
      "short_explanation": "Massage therapists are mandated reporters in many states.",
      "subcategory": "Mandatory Reporting",
      "relevance_score": 3,
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/licensing/ethics/laws_regulations",
      "variant": 39,
      "question": "What should a massage therapist do if a minor discloses abuse during a session? [Variant 39]",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.471Z",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "role_massage_therapist",
        "ethics_documentation"
      ],
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "role_massage_therapist",
        "ethics_documentation"
      ],
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "category": "Professional Practice"
    },
    {
      "id": "Law 040",
      "synonyms": [
        "secure records",
        "professional charting"
      ],
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "distractors": [],
      "subcategory": "Documentation",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.471Z",
      "answers": [
        "Sharing SOAP notes with colleagues without permission",
        "Keeping clear, dated, and private client session notes",
        "Using client nicknames to protect identity",
        "Documenting only if the session was complex"
      ],
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/licensing/ethics/laws_regulations",
      "tags": [
        "assessment_soap",
        "ethics_consent",
        "client",
        "ethics_documentation",
        "documentation_soap_notes"
      ],
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "variant": 40,
      "short_explanation": "Accurate and confidential documentation protects both client and therapist.",
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.125Z",
      "question": "Which of the following best reflects legally sound documentation practices? [Variant 40]",
      "keywords": [
        "assessment_soap",
        "ethics_consent",
        "client",
        "ethics_documentation",
        "documentation_soap_notes"
      ],
      "long_explanation": "Notes should include date, session summary, observations, and follow-up plans. All documentation must be stored securely and never shared without written consent.",
      "category": "Professional Practice",
      "topic": "licensing",
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Documentation and Ethics Handbook",
      "difficulty": "easy",
      "relevance_score": 2,
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "clinical_application": "Use a consistent SOAP note format and follow HIPAA or equivalent privacy regulations.",
      "correct": 1,
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations"
    },
    {
      "id": "Law 041",
      "clinical_application": "Keep your license active, visible, and compliant with all renewal and CEU requirements.",
      "subcategory": "Licensure",

      "question": "What is required before a massage therapist can legally begin practicing in most states? [Variant 41]",

    {
      "id": "Law 050",
      "distractors": [],
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Documentation and Ethics Handbook",
      "topic": "licensing",
      "variant": 50,
      "correct": 1,
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "answers": [
        "Sharing SOAP notes with colleagues without permission",
        "Keeping clear, dated, and private client session notes",
        "Using client nicknames to protect identity",
        "Documenting only if the session was complex"
      ],
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "relevance_score": 4,
      "tags": [
        "assessment_soap",
        "ethics_consent",
        "client",
        "ethics_documentation",
        "documentation_soap_notes"
      ],
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.471Z",
      "clinical_application": "Use a consistent SOAP note format and follow HIPAA or equivalent privacy regulations.",
      "keywords": [
        "assessment_soap",
        "ethics_consent",
        "client",
        "ethics_documentation",
        "documentation_soap_notes"
      ],
      "difficulty": "easy",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.125Z",
      "synonyms": [
        "secure records",
        "professional charting"
      ],
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "question": "Which of the following best reflects legally sound documentation practices? [Variant 50]",
      "short_explanation": "Accurate and confidential documentation protects both client and therapist.",
      "category": "Professional Practice",
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations",
      "subcategory": "Documentation",
      "long_explanation": "Notes should include date, session summary, observations, and follow-up plans. All documentation must be stored securely and never shared without written consent.",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/licensing/ethics/laws_regulations"
    },
    {
      "id": "Professional Ethics 001",
      "category": "Licensing",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage_therapy",
        "ethics_professional",
        "ethics_confidentiality"
      ],
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "correct": 1,
      "answers": [
        "Flexibility",
        "Confidentiality",
        "Appearance",
        "Friendship"
      ],
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "topic": "licensing",
      "short_explanation": "Confidentiality ensures client privacy and trust.",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.472Z",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.126Z",
      "clinical_application": "A therapist who maintains confidentiality builds client confidence and ensures compliance with legal and ethical standards.",
      "unit": "professional ethics",
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage_therapy",
        "ethics_professional",
        "ethics_confidentiality"
      ],
      "question": "What is the primary principle of professional ethics in massage therapy?",
      "distractors": [],
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "long_explanation": "Professional ethics in massage therapy emphasize maintaining client confidentiality to protect sensitive health information and uphold therapeutic integrity."
    },
    {
      "id": "Professional Ethics 002",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "category": "Licensing",
      "long_explanation": "Scope of practice outlines the boundaries of the therapist’s role, helping prevent overstepping into diagnosis or treatment beyond licensure.",
      "answers": [
        "Types of oils a therapist can use",
        "Legal activities a massage therapist is allowed to perform",
        "Client preferences for technique",
        "Optional extra services"
      ],
      "correct": 1,
      "unit": "professional ethics",
      "keywords": [

        "client",

      "stats": {

      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.472Z",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.126Z",
      "question": "What does 'scope of practice' refer to?",
      "tags": [

      "short_explanation": "Defines what a therapist is legally allowed to do.",

      "topic": "licensing",

      "clinical_application": "Respecting scope of practice maintains client safety and prevents legal or disciplinary issues."
    },
    {
      "id": "Professional Ethics 003",
      "tags": [
        "technique_fulling",
        "ethics_consent",
        "client",
        "modality_massage_therapy",
        "ethics_informed_consent",
        "ethics_informed_consent"
      ],
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "clinical_application": "Informed consent supports ethical transparency and builds trust in client-therapist relationships.",
      "unit": "professional ethics",
      "category": "Licensing",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "keywords": [
        "technique_fulling",
        "ethics_consent",
        "client",
        "modality_massage_therapy",
        "ethics_informed_consent",
        "ethics_informed_consent"
      ],
      "question": "What does 'informed consent' mean in massage therapy?",
      "answers": [
        "Consent given automatically when a session starts",
        "Permission after full disclosure of procedures and risks",
        "Therapist's approval to treat a client",
        "A waiver signed by the therapist"
      ],
      "correct": 1,
      "short_explanation": "Client agrees after being informed about the session.",
      "distractors": [],
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.126Z",
      "topic": "licensing",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.472Z",
      "long_explanation": "Informed consent ensures that clients understand what the massage entails, including benefits, risks, and boundaries, before giving permission."
    },
    {
      "id": "Professional Ethics 004",
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "distractors": [],
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.472Z",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "correct": 2,
      "long_explanation": "This principle obligates massage therapists to avoid causing physical, emotional, or psychological harm through negligence or inappropriate actions.",
      "answers": [
        "Client's right to know the truth",
        "Avoiding dual relationships",
        "The duty to do no harm",
        "Obtaining liability insurance"
      ],
      "short_explanation": "Non-maleficence means 'do no harm.'",
      "tags": [
        "ethics",
        "client",
        "ethics_nonmaleficence"
      ],
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.126Z",
      "clinical_application": "Practicing safe techniques and respecting client feedback helps ensure non-maleficence.",
      "topic": "licensing",
      "keywords": [
        "ethics",
        "client",
        "ethics_nonmaleficence"
      ],
      "question": "What is 'non-maleficence' in ethical practice?",
      "category": "Licensing",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "unit": "professional ethics"
    },
    {
      "id": "Professional Ethics 005",
      "correct": 1,
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.126Z",
      "keywords": [
        "client",
        "modality_massage_therapy",
        "ethics_dual_relationship"
      ],
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "unit": "professional ethics",
      "category": "Licensing",
      "short_explanation": "Occurs when personal and professional roles overlap.",
      "long_explanation": "Dual relationships can compromise objectivity, exploit trust, or confuse boundaries in therapeutic care.",
      "tags": [
        "client",
        "modality_massage_therapy",
        "ethics_dual_relationship"
      ],
      "question": "What is a dual relationship in massage therapy?",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.473Z",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "clinical_application": "Avoiding dual relationships protects the integrity of the therapeutic relationship.",
      "topic": "licensing",
      "answers": [
        "Treating more than one area of the body",
        "Having both personal and professional relationships with a client",
        "Working in two clinics",
        "Providing two services in one session"
      ],
      "distractors": [],
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0
    },
    {
      "id": "Professional Ethics 006",
      "category": "Licensing",
      "topic": "licensing",
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "unit": "professional ethics",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.126Z",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "tags": [
        "client",
        "business_marketing",
        "ethics_reporting"
      ],
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.473Z",
      "correct": 2,
      "long_explanation": "Confidentiality can be ethically breached if disclosure is necessary to protect the client or others from harm, such as abuse or danger.",
      "question": "When is it appropriate to breach client confidentiality?",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "clinical_application": "Therapists must understand legal obligations to report certain conditions while respecting confidentiality.",
      "distractors": [],
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "answers": [
        "When the client is famous",
        "If the client misses appointments",
        "If legally required to protect the client or others",
        "To improve clinic marketing"
      ],
      "keywords": [
        "client",
        "business_marketing",
        "ethics_reporting"
      ],
      "short_explanation": "Only when legally mandated for safety."
    },
    {
      "id": "Professional Ethics 007",
      "unit": "professional ethics",
      "long_explanation": "Veracity is the commitment to provide accurate, complete, and honest information to clients.",
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "topic": "licensing",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "distractors": [],
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.126Z",
      "category": "Licensing",
      "short_explanation": "Means honesty and truthfulness in professional behavior.",
      "clinical_application": "Being truthful helps clients make informed decisions and fosters therapeutic trust.",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "keywords": [
        "purpose_healing",
        "ethics_boundary",
        "modality_massage_therapy",
        "ethics_veracity"
      ],
      "correct": 0,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.473Z",
      "tags": [
        "purpose_healing",
        "ethics_boundary",
        "modality_massage_therapy",
        "ethics_veracity"
      ],
      "question": "What is veracity in massage therapy ethics?",
      "answers": [
        "Right to objective truth and honesty",
        "Physical boundaries between techniques",
        "Avoiding personal expression",
        "Emphasizing spiritual healing"
      ],
      "difficulty": "intermediate"
    },
    {
      "id": "Professional Ethics 008",

      "short_explanation": "HIPAA safeguards personal health data.",

      "long_explanation": "HIPAA is a U.S. law that requires practitioners to maintain the privacy and security of client health records.",

      ],
      "question": "What organization protects health information under federal law?",

    {
      "id": "Professional Ethics 009",
      "long_explanation": "Boundaries protect both the client and therapist by clearly defining what is appropriate within a professional relationship.",
      "keywords": [
        "ethics_boundary",
        "condition_injury",
        "condition_injuries",
        "organ_skin",
        "ethics_boundaries"
      ],
      "question": "What is a professional boundary?",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.126Z",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "distractors": [],
      "correct": 1,
      "category": "Licensing",
      "clinical_application": "Maintaining professional boundaries prevents misunderstandings and ethical violations.",
      "tags": [
        "ethics_boundary",
        "condition_injury",
        "condition_injuries",
        "organ_skin",
        "ethics_boundaries"
      ],
      "answers": [
        "A technique used near the skin surface",
        "Predetermined ethical and behavioral limits in therapeutic settings",
        "A law restricting clinic hours",
        "A physical limitation from injury"
      ],
      "topic": "licensing",
      "unit": "professional ethics",
      "short_explanation": "An ethical limit on therapist-client interactions.",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.473Z",
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "last_answered_at": null
    },
    {
      "id": "Professional Ethics 010",
      "correct": 1,
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "topic": "licensing",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.126Z",
      "clinical_application": "Therapists should reaffirm consent and autonomy to equalize power in the therapeutic dynamic.",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.473Z",
      "category": "Licensing",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "short_explanation": "When the therapist holds more influence than the client.",
      "tags": [
        "ethics_consent",
        "ethics_boundary",
        "client",
        "ethics_informed_consent",
        "ethics_power_balance"
      ],
      "long_explanation": "Power differentials can arise from the therapist's knowledge, authority, or control, potentially leading to imbalanced interactions if not addressed.",
      "distractors": [],
      "answers": [
        "Ignore it",
        "Ensure informed consent and reinforce professional boundaries",
        "End the session immediately",
        "Refer the client elsewhere without explanation"
      ],
      "keywords": [
        "ethics_consent",
        "ethics_boundary",
        "client",
        "ethics_informed_consent",
        "ethics_power_balance"
      ],
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "unit": "professional ethics",
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "question": "What should a therapist do if they suspect a power differential is affecting the session?"
    },
    {
      "id": "Transference 001",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "topic": "ethics",
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "synonyms": [
        "emotional projection",
        "therapist response"
      ],
      "created_at": "2025-05-26T00:18:06.508413Z",
      "variant": 1,
      "category": "Professional Ethics",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "long_explanation": "Transference is a psychological occurrence where a client projects emotions, often from early life relationships, onto the therapist. These reactions are often subconscious.",
      "subcategory": "Therapeutic Boundaries",
      "tags": [
        "ethics",
        "transference",
        "countertransference",
        "emotional_dynamics",
        "therapeutic_boundaries"
      ],
      "clinical_application": "Recognizing transference helps the therapist maintain boundaries and avoid taking client behaviors personally.",
      "answers": [
        "When a client projects feelings from past relationships onto the therapist.",
        "When a therapist shares personal information with a client.",
        "When a client requests deeper pressure during a session.",
        "When a client cancels multiple appointments in a row."
      ],
      "keywords": [
        "transference",
        "countertransference",
        "client_emotion",
        "therapist_response",
        "ethics"
      ],
      "question": "What best describes transference in a massage therapy session?",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "short_explanation": "Transference is when a client unconsciously redirects feelings onto the therapist.",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T00:18:06.508413Z",
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations",
      "distractors": [],
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Ethics and Boundaries Module",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/licensing/ethics/transference_and_countertransference",
      "correct": 0,
      "last_answered_at": null
    },
    {
      "id": "Transference 002",
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Ethics and Boundaries Module",
      "question": "How should a therapist respond to signs of countertransference?",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations",
      "distractors": [],
      "long_explanation": "Countertransference involves the therapist projecting their own feelings onto the client. Recognizing and addressing it through supervision ensures ethical practice.",
      "keywords": [
        "transference",
        "countertransference",
        "client_emotion",
        "therapist_response",
        "ethics"
      ],
      "subcategory": "Therapeutic Boundaries",
      "tags": [
        "ethics",
        "transference",
        "countertransference",
        "emotional_dynamics",
        "therapeutic_boundaries"
      ],
      "answers": [
        "Seek supervision or consultation while maintaining professional boundaries.",
        "Immediately disclose all feelings to the client.",
        "Ignore the feelings and continue the session.",
        "Refer the client out without explanation."
      ],
      "topic": "ethics",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "short_explanation": "Professional support helps manage countertransference.",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/licensing/ethics/transference_and_countertransference",
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "correct": 0,
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "variant": 2,
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "clinical_application": "Therapists benefit from regular reflection and peer support to stay emotionally neutral during treatments.",
      "synonyms": [
        "emotional projection",
        "therapist response"
      ],
      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T00:18:06.508413Z",
      "category": "Professional Ethics",
      "created_at": "2025-05-26T00:18:06.508413Z"
    },
    {
      "id": "Transference 003",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/licensing/ethics/transference_and_countertransference",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "clinical_application": "Recognizing early signs of emotional boundary blurring protects therapeutic integrity.",
      "synonyms": [
        "emotional projection",
        "therapist response"
      ],
      "distractors": [],
      "correct": 0,
      "long_explanation": "Transference can create dependency or emotional attachment, which must be addressed with firm boundaries and professional clarity.",
      "subcategory": "Therapeutic Boundaries",
      "tags": [
        "ethics",
        "transference",
        "countertransference",
        "emotional_dynamics",
        "therapeutic_boundaries"
      ],
      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T00:18:06.508413Z",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "short_explanation": "Gifts and personal attention may reflect emotional transfer.",
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Ethics and Boundaries Module",
      "question": "Which situation may indicate a boundary issue related to transference?",
      "created_at": "2025-05-26T00:18:06.508413Z",
      "variant": 3,
      "category": "Professional Ethics",
      "answers": [
        "The client begins bringing gifts and asking personal questions about the therapist.",
        "The client reschedules for a different time.",
        "The client requests a longer session due to injury.",
        "The client asks about pressure adjustments during massage."
      ],
      "keywords": [
        "transference",
        "countertransference",
        "client_emotion",
        "therapist_response",
        "ethics"
      ],
      "topic": "ethics"
    },
    {
      "id": "Transference 004",
      "subcategory": "Therapeutic Boundaries",
      "distractors": [],
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "short_explanation": "It can compromise therapist objectivity.",
      "correct": 0,
      "category": "Professional Ethics",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T00:18:06.508413Z",
      "tags": [
        "ethics",
        "transference",
        "countertransference",
        "emotional_dynamics",
        "therapeutic_boundaries"
      ],
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations",
      "created_at": "2025-05-26T00:18:06.508413Z",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "topic": "ethics",
      "question": "Countertransference may result in which risk during treatment?",
      "synonyms": [
        "emotional projection",
        "therapist response"
      ],
      "variant": 4,
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "clinical_application": "Staying self-aware and emotionally grounded is crucial for therapeutic effectiveness.",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/licensing/ethics/transference_and_countertransference",
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "long_explanation": "Countertransference may cause therapists to overidentify with a client or react emotionally, which can interfere with professional boundaries.",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "answers": [
        "Loss of objectivity and impaired therapeutic judgment.",
        "Improved massage technique and body mechanics.",
        "Better emotional connection with the client.",
        "Decreased risk of professional burnout."
      ],
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Ethics and Boundaries Module",
      "keywords": [
        "transference",
        "countertransference",
        "client_emotion",
        "therapist_response",
        "ethics"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "Transference 005",
      "short_explanation": "Self-care and supervision protect therapist neutrality.",
      "distractors": [],
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "answers": [
        "Engaging in regular self-care and supervision.",
        "Allowing clients to process their emotions through the therapist.",
        "Mirroring the client’s emotional tone.",
        "Permitting friendships to form outside the session."
      ],
      "clinical_application": "Support structures help therapists remain grounded, especially when working with emotional or trauma-prone clients.",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "category": "Professional Ethics",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T00:18:06.508413Z",
      "created_at": "2025-05-26T00:18:06.508413Z",
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,

      },

      "subcategory": "Therapeutic Boundaries",
      "topic": "ethics",
      "variant": 5,
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/licensing/ethics/transference_and_countertransference",
      "question": "Which of the following helps prevent countertransference in massage therapy?",
      "synonyms": [
        "emotional projection",
        "therapist response"
      ],
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Ethics and Boundaries Module",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "tags": [
        "ethics",

      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "keywords": [
        "transference",
        "countertransference",
        "client_emotion",
        "therapist_response",
        "ethics"
      ],
      "long_explanation": "Proactive reflection, mentoring, and self-awareness are essential for preventing emotional entanglement.",
      "correct": 0
    },
    {
      "id": "Transference 006",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/licensing/ethics/transference_and_countertransference",
      "variant": 6,
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Ethics and Boundaries Module",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "clinical_application": "Recognizing this bias ensures the therapist maintains neutrality and delivers care without emotional entanglement.",
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations",
      "question": "A client reminds a therapist of a close family member. This is an example of:",
      "long_explanation": "This describes countertransference, where the therapist transfers unresolved personal emotions onto the client.",
      "subcategory": "Therapeutic Boundaries",
      "category": "Professional Ethics",
      "synonyms": [
        "emotional projection",
        "therapist response"
      ],
      "tags": [
        "ethics",
        "transference",
        "countertransference",
        "emotional_dynamics",
        "therapeutic_boundaries"
      ],
      "answers": [
        "Countertransference",
        "Dual relationship",
        "Burnout",
        "Active listening"
      ],
      "correct": 0,
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "created_at": "2025-05-26T00:18:06.508413Z",
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T00:18:06.508413Z",
      "short_explanation": "Therapist’s projection is countertransference.",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "topic": "ethics",
      "distractors": [],
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0

    {
      "id": "Transference 007",
      "long_explanation": "Unacknowledged transference can lead clients to misinterpret the therapeutic relationship, risking dependency or boundary confusion.",
      "keywords": [
        "transference",
        "countertransference",
        "client_emotion",
        "therapist_response",
        "ethics"
      ],
      "last_answered_at": null,

      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "question": "Transference may lead to ethical concerns when:",
      "short_explanation": "Emotional attachment can cloud therapeutic boundaries.",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "answers": [
        "The client develops an emotional attachment to the therapist.",
        "The therapist performs a deep tissue massage.",
        "The client asks about the next appointment date.",
        "The therapist explains hydrotherapy benefits."

      "stats": {

        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "clinical_application": "Setting and reinforcing clear therapeutic limits protects both the client and therapist.",

      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T00:18:06.508413Z",

        "End the session early without explanation."

      "source_reference": "FSMTB Ethics and Boundaries Module",
      "long_explanation": "Therapist overreaction is often rooted in personal emotional triggers. Recognizing and addressing it professionally is crucial for ethical care.",

      "short_explanation": "Beneficence means acting in the client’s best interest.",

    {
      "id": "Licensing Standards 001",
      "long_explanation": "The MBLEx (Massage & Bodywork Licensing Examination) is used by most U.S. states to determine a candidate's readiness to practice massage therapy. It covers a wide range of topics, from anatomy to ethics.",
      "answers": [
        "NCBTMB",
        "MBLEx",
        "CPR",
        "USMLE"
      ],
      "difficulty": "intermediate",

      "tags": [

        "exam_mblex"
      ],
      "clinical_application": "Passing the MBLEx is typically a legal requirement to practice massage therapy in regulated states.",
      "category": "Licensing",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "question": "Which national exam is required for licensure in many U.S. states for massage therapists?",
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T14:23:05.018828Z",
      "short_explanation": "The MBLEx is the most widely accepted national exam for massage therapists.",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "exam_mblex"
      ],
      "distractors": [],
      "unit": "licensing standards",
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Licensure & MBLEx Guidelines",
      "correct": 1,

      "last_answered_at": null
    },

      "short_explanation": "FSMTB administers the MBLEx exam.",

      "correct": 2,
      "long_explanation": "The Federation of State Massage Therapy Boards (FSMTB) is the official body that develops and oversees the MBLEx. It works with state boards to ensure licensure standards are upheld.",

    },

      "short_explanation": "Most states require at least 500 hours of massage training.",

    {
      "id": "Licensing Standards 007",
      "short_explanation": "Violating scope of practice or ethics can lead to suspension.",
      "unit": "licensing standards",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "category": "Licensing",
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "keywords": [
        "modality_oils",
        "ethics_scope_of_practice",
        "practice_scope",
        "modality_modality"
      ],
      "topic": "licensing",
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.126Z",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "tags": [
        "modality_oils",
        "ethics_scope_of_practice",
        "practice_scope",
        "modality_modality"
      ],
      "clinical_application": "Therapists must always adhere to ethical and legal standards to protect their license.",
      "answers": [
        "Changing business location",
        "Using non-gluten oil",
        "Violating scope of practice or committing ethics violations",
        "Switching modalities too often"
      ],
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Licensure & MBLEx Guidelines",
      "correct": 2,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T14:23:05.018828Z",
      "long_explanation": "Common reasons for suspension include practicing outside one's legal scope (e.g., diagnosing illness), misconduct, or violating client boundaries.",
      "question": "Which of the following is a typical reason for license suspension?",
      "distractors": [],
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0
    },
    {
      "id": "Licensing Standards 008",

      },

      "answers": [

        "Referral from chiropractor"

      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations",
      "distractors": [],
      "long_explanation": "Licensing applications typically require evidence of education, professional liability insurance, and a clear background to ensure client safety and ethical standards.",

    {
      "id": "Licensing Standards 009",
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "question": "Which credential is issued upon passing the MBLEx?",
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations",
      "short_explanation": "Passing the MBLEx grants the LMT credential.",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "clinical_application": "Displaying this credential shows professionalism and compliance with state regulations.",
      "category": "Licensing",
      "answers": [

        "RMT (Registered Massage Therapist)",
        "CMT (Certified Massage Trainer)",
        "PT (Physical Therapist)"
      ],
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Licensure & MBLEx Guidelines",

        "modality_massage",
        "exam_mblex",
        "role_massage_therapist"
      ],
      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T14:23:05.018828Z",
      "correct": 0,
      "long_explanation": "LMT stands for Licensed Massage Therapist, a protected title used by those who’ve met all state-specific education, exam, and ethics requirements.",

      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,

    {
      "id": "Licensing Standards 010",
      "short_explanation": "Therapists must apply for reciprocity or meet new state rules.",
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations",
      "unit": "licensing standards",
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "keywords": [
        "licensure",
        "massage",
        "MBLEx",
        "scope",
        "FSMTB"
      ],
      "category": "Licensing",
      "clinical_application": "Therapists moving states should prepare documentation in advance to avoid delays in practice.",
      "tags": [
        "licensure_massage",
        "massage_legal"
      ],
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "long_explanation": "Licensure isn’t automatically transferred between states. Some states offer reciprocity agreements, but others may require re-examination or additional CEUs.",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.126Z",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T14:23:05.018828Z",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "distractors": [],
      "answers": [
        "Apply for reciprocity or meet the new state's licensing requirements",
        "Keep practicing under their old license",
        "Reapply for school training",
        "Cancel all existing certifications"
      ],
      "correct": 0,
      "topic": "licensing",
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Licensure & MBLEx Guidelines",
      "question": "What must a therapist do if they move to a different state?",
      "last_answered_at": null
    },

      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "category": "Massage Therapy Organizations",
      "topic": "massage_licensing",
      "short_explanation": "AMTA is a leading professional organization for massage therapists.",
      "answers": [
        "American Massage Therapy Association",
        "American Manual Therapy Alliance",
        "Associated Massage Therapists of America",
        "Allied Massage Trade Association"
      ],
      "variant": 1,
      "question": "What does AMTA stand for? [Variant 1]",
      "created_at": "2025-05-26T14:32:39.532888Z",
      "long_explanation": "The American Massage Therapy Association offers membership, continuing education, advocacy, and research funding. It's one of the most established massage organizations in the U.S.",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "clinical_application": "Therapists use AMTA for liability insurance, CE tracking, and professional development.",
      "synonyms": [],
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "distractors": [],

    },
    {
      "id": "Organizations 002",

      "question": "What is the role of the FSMTB in massage therapy? [Variant 2]",

      "variant": 2,
      "created_at": "2025-05-26T14:32:39.532888Z",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "long_explanation": "FSMTB (Federation of State Massage Therapy Boards) oversees the Massage & Bodywork Licensing Examination, used by most U.S. states for licensure.",
      "question_type": "multiple choice"
    },

    {

    {

    {
      "id": "ScopeOfPractice 001",
      "short_explanation": "Massage therapists treat musculoskeletal tension, not diagnose conditions.",
      "category": "Legal and Ethical Practice",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "ethics_scope_of_practice",
        "practice_scope"
      ],
      "created_at": "2025-05-26T14:38:48.224919Z",
      "synonyms": [],
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/licensing/scope_of_practice",
      "variant": 1,
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Code of Ethics",
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations",
      "keywords": [
        "muscle",
        "pain",
        "massage",
        "scope",
        "ethics"
      ],
      "answers": [
        "Providing therapeutic massage for musculoskeletal discomfort",
        "Prescribing antibiotics",
        "Diagnosing fibromyalgia",
        "Ordering blood tests"
      ],
      "distractors": [],
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T14:38:48.224919Z",
      "correct": 0,
      "clinical_application": "Therapists often treat back pain or postural discomfort by focusing on soft tissue relief.",
      "subcategory": "Scope of Practice",
      "long_explanation": "Licensed massage therapists are permitted to address muscular pain or stress through therapeutic techniques. However, they must not diagnose or prescribe, which are outside their legal scope.",
      "topic": "massage_scope",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "question": "Which of the following is typically within the scope of practice for a licensed massage therapist? [Variant 1]",
      "difficulty_rating": 3
    },
    {
      "id": "ScopeOfPractice 002",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T14:38:48.224919Z",
      "keywords": [
        "prescription",
        "legal limits",
        "massage"
      ],
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "correct": 0,
      "category": "Legal and Ethical Practice",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/licensing/scope_of_practice",
      "answers": [
        "Prescribe medications",
        "Stretch a client’s hamstrings",
        "Use essential oils during sessions",
        "Document muscle tension areas"
      ],
      "created_at": "2025-05-26T14:38:48.224919Z",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "long_explanation": "Only licensed medical providers (e.g., physicians, nurse practitioners) can legally prescribe medications. Massage therapists focus on non-invasive, holistic interventions.",
      "topic": "massage_scope",
      "clinical_application": "Clients asking about meds should be referred to medical professionals.",
      "short_explanation": "Prescribing drugs is outside massage therapy's legal boundaries.",
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations",
      "synonyms": [],
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "distractors": [],
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "question": "Massage therapists are generally NOT allowed to: [Variant 2]",
      "tags": [
        "ethics_scope_of_practice",
        "pharmacology",
        "practice_scope"
      ],
      "source_reference": "State Medical Board Guidelines",
      "subcategory": "Scope of Practice",
      "variant": 2,
      "question_type": "multiple choice"
    },
    {
      "id": "ScopeOfPractice 003",
      "variant": 3,
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "question": "What action must a massage therapist take if a client presents with a medical issue outside their scope? [Variant 3]",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "answers": [
        "Refer the client to a qualified healthcare professional",
        "Ignore the issue and continue",
        "Attempt to treat the condition anyway",
        "Prescribe an over-the-counter remedy"
      ],
      "category": "Legal and Ethical Practice",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Ethics Advisory",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "created_at": "2025-05-26T14:38:48.224919Z",
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations",
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "synonyms": [],
      "long_explanation": "When clients present with unfamiliar symptoms, therapists are ethically and legally obligated to refer rather than treat or guess.",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T14:38:48.224919Z",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "topic": "massage_scope",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/licensing/scope_of_practice",
      "tags": [
        "referral",
        "scope_of_practice",
        "client_safety"
      ],
      "short_explanation": "Therapists must refer when symptoms are outside their scope.",
      "subcategory": "Scope of Practice",
      "clinical_application": "Refer clients with suspected fractures, infections, or systemic conditions.",
      "distractors": [],
      "keywords": [
        "referral",
        "out of scope",
        "ethical response"
      ],
      "correct": 0
    },
    {
      "id": "ScopeOfPractice 004",
      "tags": [
        "legal_scope",
        "diagnosis_restriction",
        "ethics_communication"
      ],
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "distractors": [],
      "subcategory": "Scope of Practice",
      "created_at": "2025-05-26T14:38:48.224919Z",
      "variant": 4,
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/licensing/scope_of_practice",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "long_explanation": "Terms like 'tendinitis' or 'sciatica' are protected under medical licensing laws. Massage therapists may describe observations, not diagnose.",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "clinical_application": "Use descriptive terms like 'tension in the lower back' instead.",
      "question": "Which of the following is considered outside the legal scope of massage therapy? [Variant 4]",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T14:38:48.224919Z",
      "short_explanation": "Massage therapists cannot use clinical diagnostic labels.",
      "category": "Legal and Ethical Practice",
      "synonyms": [],
      "source_reference": "State Scope of Practice Guidelines",
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations",
      "topic": "massage_scope",
      "keywords": [
        "diagnosis",
        "label",
        "legality",
        "massage"
      ],
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "answers": [
        "Using protected diagnostic terminology",
        "Manipulating soft tissue",
        "Hydrotherapy",
        "Stretching techniques"
      ],
      "correct": 0
    },
    {
      "id": "ScopeOfPractice 005",
      "tags": [
        "ethics_informed_consent",
        "legal_practice",
        "client_rights"
      ],
      "answers": [
        "No, informed consent is always required",
        "Yes, if the condition is minor",
        "Yes, during emergencies",
        "Only if insurance covers the session"
      ],
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "keywords": [
        "consent",
        "documentation",
        "informed decision"
      ],
      "short_explanation": "Clients must understand and agree before treatment begins.",
      "distractors": [],
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations",
      "category": "Legal and Ethical Practice",
      "long_explanation": "Informed consent involves explaining the session, answering questions, and respecting boundaries. Lack of consent can lead to legal consequences.",
      "topic": "massage_scope",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/licensing/scope_of_practice",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "subcategory": "Scope of Practice",
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "correct": 0,
      "synonyms": [],
      "source_reference": "MBLEx Candidate Handbook",
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "created_at": "2025-05-26T14:38:48.224919Z",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "question": "Can a massage therapist treat a condition without client consent? [Variant 5]",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T14:38:48.224919Z",
      "clinical_application": "Always get verbal and written consent, even for regular clients.",
      "variant": 5
    },
    {
      "id": "ScopeOfPractice 006",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "variant": 6,
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T14:38:48.224919Z",
      "long_explanation": "Interprofessional collaboration is encouraged, but therapists cannot override other providers' plans. Staying within scope builds trust and safety.",
      "question": "Which statement is true about the massage therapist's role? [Variant 6]",
      "topic": "massage_scope",
      "keywords": [
        "professional boundaries",
        "interdisciplinary",
        "scope"
      ],
      "correct": 0,
      "distractors": [],
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Interprofessional Practice Manual",
      "created_at": "2025-05-26T14:38:48.224919Z",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "tags": [
        "collaboration",
        "ethics_scope",
        "team_care"
      ],
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "synonyms": [],
      "clinical_application": "Coordinate with PTs, chiropractors, or physicians without altering medical directives.",
      "category": "Legal and Ethical Practice",
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations",
      "subcategory": "Scope of Practice",
      "answers": [
        "They collaborate with other professionals within their defined scope",
        "They may override doctor recommendations",
        "They treat infectious disease directly",
        "They provide emergency trauma care"
      ],
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/licensing/scope_of_practice",
      "short_explanation": "Massage therapists work as part of a health team within their boundaries.",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "last_answered_at": null
    },
    {
      "id": "ScopeOfPractice 007",
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Approved Modalities List",
      "short_explanation": "These are approved within scope in most states.",
      "long_explanation": "State massage boards define allowed techniques. Most include muscle manipulation, light hydrotherapy, and basic self-care education.",
      "clinical_application": "Teaching clients posture tips can prevent future injury.",
      "subcategory": "Scope of Practice",
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "variant": 7,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T14:38:48.224919Z",

      ],
      "last_answered_at": null,

      "question": "Massage therapists are allowed to perform which of the following? [Variant 7]",

      "answers": [

        "Prescribing herbal supplements",
        "Vaccination procedures"

      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "long_explanation": "Therapists may note tension or mobility issues but cannot assign clinical terms or conditions.",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "variant": 8,
      "correct": 0,
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "clinical_application": "Say 'tightness in the trapezius' instead of 'you have a strain.'",
      "short_explanation": "Only licensed medical providers can diagnose.",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T14:38:48.224919Z",

    {
      "id": "ScopeOfPractice 009",
      "long_explanation": "Each licensed profession has a defined scope, and working outside it can lead to fines or loss of license.",

    {
      "id": "ScopeOfPractice 010",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T14:38:48.224919Z",
      "short_explanation": "Medication advice is outside the massage therapist’s training and licensure.",
      "source_reference": "Massage Law & Ethics Textbook, 4th Ed.",
      "question": "Can a massage therapist recommend over-the-counter medications to a client? [Variant 10]",
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "created_at": "2025-05-26T14:38:48.224919Z",
      "subcategory": "Scope of Practice",
      "synonyms": [],
      "long_explanation": "Recommending any medication—even nonprescription—may be considered practicing medicine without a license.",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "clinical_application": "Redirect questions about medications to a pharmacist or physician.",
      "correct": 0,
      "mblex_category": "Ethics, Boundaries, Laws, Regulations",
      "keywords": [
        "OTC",
        "recommendation",
        "unlicensed practice"
      ],
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "distractors": [],
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/licensing/scope_of_practice",
      "tags": [
