        "concept_tension"
      ],
      "short_explanation": "Myofascial massage targets the fascia.",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.140Z",
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "category": "Modalities",
      "long_explanation": "Fascia is a web of connective tissue that envelops muscles, organs, and bones. Restrictions or adhesions in this tissue can affect mobility and pain levels.",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.482Z",
      "question": "Which connective tissue is primarily targeted in myofascial massage?",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/modalities/myofascial/myofascial",
      "correct": 2,
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "modality_myofascial",
        "anatomy_fascia",
        "anatomy_tissues",
        "anatomy_muscles",
        "anatomy_bones",
        "anatomy_organs",
        "anatomy_connective_tissue",
        "condition_pain",
        "purpose_stress",
        "biochemistry_levels",
        "concept_targeted",
        "concept_tension"
      ],
      "answers": [
        "Tendon",
        "Cartilage",
        "Fascia",
        "Ligament"
      ],
      "topic": "modalities",
      "clinical_application": "Releasing fascial adhesions may improve posture and reduce chronic tension."
    },
    {
      "id": "Myofascial 004",
      "topic": "modalities",
      "category": "Modalities",
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "short_explanation": "Restricted fascia often causes pain and stiffness.",
      "clinical_application": "Massage therapists use myofascial work to restore mobility and reduce compensatory strain.",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.140Z",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/modalities/myofascial/myofascial",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.482Z",
      "distractors": [],
      "question": "What is a common result of restricted fascia?",
      "long_explanation": "When fascia tightens or develops adhesions, it can pull on muscles and joints, reducing movement and causing discomfort or dysfunction.",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "correct": 2,
      "answers": [
        "Improved coordination",
        "Reduced blood pressure",
        "Pain and limited range of motion",
        "Increased reflex speed"
      ],
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "modality_myofascial",
        "anatomy_fascia",
        "technique_pressure",
        "anatomy_muscles",
        "condition_pain",
        "purpose_healing",
        "ethics_boundary",
        "condition_discomfort",
        "concept_restoration"
      ],
      "keywords": [

        "anatomy_muscles",

        "concept_restoration"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "Myofascial 005",
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "short_explanation": "Chronic musculoskeletal pain often responds well to myofascial release.",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/modalities/myofascial/myofascial",
      "distractors": [],
      "long_explanation": "Clients with ongoing pain, especially those with postural strain or old injuries, may benefit from the fascial decompression and realignment that myofascial techniques offer.",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.482Z",
      "answers": [
        "Acute fractures",
        "Influenza",
        "Chronic neck and back pain",
        "Vertigo"
      ],
      "correct": 2,
      "category": "Modalities",
      "tags": [
        "condition_pain",
        "condition_fibromyalgia",
        "condition_scoliosis",
        "purpose_benefit",
        "client",
        "modality_myofascial_release",
        "condition_injuries"
      ],
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.140Z",
      "keywords": [
        "condition_pain",
        "condition_fibromyalgia",
        "condition_scoliosis",
        "purpose_benefit",
        "client",
        "modality_myofascial_release",
        "condition_injuries"
      ],
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "question": "Which condition is most likely to benefit from myofascial therapy?",
      "topic": "modalities",
      "clinical_application": "Frequently used for clients with fibromyalgia, scoliosis, or repetitive strain injuries."
    },
    {
      "id": "Myofascial 006",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.140Z",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "keywords": [

        "concept_tension"
      ],
      "clinical_application": "Used for clients with trauma, chronic holding patterns, or emotional tension.",
      "long_explanation": "Myofascial unwinding allows the client’s body to move instinctively as fascial restrictions are released. This technique is passive and often intuitive, guided by tissue response.",
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.482Z",
      "answers": [
        "Guided breathing",
        "Repetitive muscle contraction",
        "Spontaneous body movement guided by internal tension release",
        "Fast-paced stretching"
      ],
      "question": "What is a key principle behind myofascial unwinding?",
      "category": "Modalities",
      "short_explanation": "Myofascial unwinding involves spontaneous, guided motion.",
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "correct": 2,
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/modalities/myofascial/myofascial",
      "tags": [

        "anatomy_muscle_contraction",
        "purpose_stress",
        "client",
        "concept_tension"
      ],
      "distractors": [],
      "topic": "modalities"
    },
    {
      "id": "Myofascial 007",
      "long_explanation": "Postural re-education teaches clients to move and hold their bodies in ways that reduce stress on the fascia and support release outcomes.",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/modalities/myofascial/myofascial",
      "short_explanation": "Postural awareness supports long-term fascial balance.",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.482Z",
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "tags": [

      "topic": "modalities",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.140Z",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "correct": 2,
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "clinical_application": "Therapists may offer stretches or ergonomic tips post-session to maintain fascial mobility.",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "category": "Modalities",
      "keywords": [

    {
      "id": "Myofascial 008",
      "category": "Modalities",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/modalities/myofascial/myofascial",
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_fascia",
        "anatomy_nerve",
        "technique_pressure",
        "anatomy_tissues",
        "anatomy_muscles",
        "ethics_boundary"
      ],
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "clinical_application": "Therapists must use sustained pressure and time to engage fascial release safely.",
      "distractors": [],
      "topic": "modalities",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.140Z",
      "long_explanation": "Unlike muscle, fascia is a dense, fibrous tissue that doesn’t contract on command and has limited vascular supply. It plays a structural, rather than motor, role in the body.",
      "short_explanation": "Fascia lacks its own blood supply and does not contract voluntarily.",
      "answers": [
        "Fascia is vascular and contracts like muscle",
        "Fascia is avascular and does not contract voluntarily",
        "Fascia is a type of nerve",
        "Fascia only exists in the legs"
      ],
      "keywords": [
        "anatomy_fascia",
        "anatomy_nerve",
        "technique_pressure",
        "anatomy_tissues",
        "anatomy_muscles",
        "ethics_boundary"
      ],
      "question": "What distinguishes fascia from muscle tissue?",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.482Z",
      "correct": 1,
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "last_answered_at": null
    },
    {
      "id": "Myofascial 009",
      "topic": "modalities",
      "keywords": [

      "stats": {

        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "correct": 2,
      "short_explanation": "Adhesions are sticky points in fascia that limit tissue movement.",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "clinical_application": "Myofascial release techniques are designed to melt or break apart these adhesions gently.",
      "category": "Modalities",
      "answers": [
        "A scar in the bone marrow",
        "A growth on a tendon",
        "A sticky or fibrous area limiting fascial glide",
        "A nerve entrapment in muscle"
      ],
      "long_explanation": "Adhesions are areas where fascia sticks to adjacent layers, reducing its ability to slide. This can pull on muscles and contribute to pain or restriction.",
      "question": "Which of the following best describes a fascial adhesion?",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.140Z",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/modalities/myofascial/myofascial",
      "distractors": []
    },
    {
      "id": "Myofascial 010",
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "answers": [
        "The eye",
        "The abdomen",
        "The ear",
        "The tongue"
      ],

      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,

      "question": "Which area of the body contains a high concentration of fascial layering?",
      "short_explanation": "The abdomen has multiple fascial layers between organs and muscles.",
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "topic": "modalities",
      "category": "Modalities",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.140Z",
      "long_explanation": "The abdomen contains visceral, muscular, and superficial fascia that interlace to support organs and movement. Restriction here can affect posture and breathing."
    },

    {
      "id": "Polarity 006",
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "source_reference": "Polarity Therapy: The Power That Heals – Franklyn Sills",
      "category": "Modalities",
      "clinical_application": "Understanding polarity helps practitioners choose hand placements for flow enhancement.",
      "synonyms": [],
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/modalities/polarity_therapy",
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T15:42:00.998299Z",
      "answers": [
        "Opposition in muscle groups",
        "Emotional tension",
        "Positive and negative energy charges in the body",
        "Magnetic therapy tools"
      ],
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "keywords": [
        "anatomy_muscles",
        "purpose_stress",
        "modality_polarity_therapy",
        "concept_tension"
      ],
      "long_explanation": "These charges help explain how energy flows between poles and centers, influencing balance and health.",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "variant": 6,
      "topic": "modalities",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "difficulty": "easy",
      "relevance_score": 5,

      "mblex_category": "Massage and Bodywork Modalities",
      "tags": [

        "purpose_stress",
        "modality_polarity_therapy",
        "concept_tension"
      ],
      "correct": 2,
      "short_explanation": "Polarity refers to the positive and negative energetic charges within the body.",
      "question": "What does the term 'polarity' in Polarity Therapy refer to?",
      "distractors": []
    },
    {
      "id": "Polarity 007",
      "long_explanation": "These channels resemble meridians and reflex arcs, allowing for diagnostic and therapeutic energetic mapping.",
      "source_reference": "Principles of Polarity Therapy – John Chitty",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/modalities/polarity_therapy",
      "variant": 7,

      "question": "In Polarity Therapy, energy is believed to flow through:",
      "mblex_category": "Massage and Bodywork Modalities",
      "answers": [
        "Skin pores",
        "Blood vessels",
        "Electromagnetic pathways",
        "The nervous system only"
      ],
      "distractors": [],
      "keywords": [

        "organ_skin"
      ],
      "short_explanation": "Energy in Polarity Therapy flows through electromagnetic pathways.",

    {
      "id": "Polarity 010",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/modalities/polarity_therapy",
      "clinical_application": "Massage therapists use this versatility to meet diverse sensitivity and therapeutic goals.",
      "tags": [
        "technique_tapping",
        "technique_pressure",
        "modality_polarity_therapy"
      ],
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "topic": "modalities",
      "correct": 2,
      "long_explanation": "The approach is intuitive and client-centered, adapting pressure to the energetic or tissue feedback.",
      "short_explanation": "Polarity Therapy uses varied touch from gentle to firm, depending on energy needs.",
      "keywords": [
        "technique_tapping",
        "technique_pressure",
        "modality_polarity_therapy"
      ],
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "distractors": [],

      "mblex_category": "Massage and Bodywork Modalities",
      "difficulty": "easy",

      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.141Z",
      "variant": 10,
      "answers": [
        "Only deep pressure",
        "Only feather-light strokes",
        "Varied pressure including gentle and firm",
        "Only tapping"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "Prenatal Massage 001",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.141Z",
      "mblex_category": "Assessment and Application",
      "topic": "modalities",
      "category": "Massage Techniques",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "subcategory": "Prenatal",
      "clinical_application": "Understanding safe zones and contraindications in each trimester helps massage therapists modify their techniques appropriately to protect both the client and fetus.",
      "useful_fact": "Side-lying position is often preferred for pregnant clients after the first trimester to avoid pressure on the vena cava.",
      "short_explanation": "Abdominal massage is typically avoided during the first trimester due to increased miscarriage risk.",
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "long_explanation": "The first trimester is a critical period for fetal development and is associated with a higher risk of miscarriage. Massage therapists are advised to avoid abdominal massage during this time unless they have advanced training in prenatal massage protocols.",
      "relevance_score": 9.5,
      "question": "At what stage of pregnancy is it generally recommended to avoid massage in the abdominal area unless specifically trained?",
      "question_type": "multiple_choice",
      "answers": [
        "First trimester",
        "Second trimester",
        "Third trimester",
        "Postpartum period"
      ],
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.483Z",
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "contraindication",
        "client",
        "anatomy_zones"
      ],
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "contraindication",
        "client",
        "anatomy_zones"
      ],
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/modalities/prenatal_massage",
      "question_rank": 1,
      "img_tags": [
        "pregnancy_trimester_chart.png"
      ],
      "distractors": [],
      "correct": 0,
      "source_reference": "Massage Therapy for Special Populations, Susan Salvo, Elsevier",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "difficulty": "Medium"
    },

      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T15:44:30.195190Z",
      "question": "Which condition is commonly supported through reflexology by stimulating the solar plexus point?",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "source_reference": "Clinical Reflexology Handbook – Kunz & Kunz",
      "clinical_application": "Massage therapists use this point to help clients experiencing stress, nervousness, or shallow breathing.",
      "tags": [
        "modality_reflexology",
        "anatomy_muscles",
        "purpose_stress",
        "concept_anxiety",
        "organ_skin"
      ],
      "keywords": [
        "modality_reflexology",
        "anatomy_muscles",
        "purpose_stress",
        "concept_anxiety",
        "organ_skin"
      ],
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/modalities/reflexology_clinical_application",
      "synonyms": [],
      "short_explanation": "Stimulating the solar plexus reflex supports the parasympathetic nervous system and emotional regulation.",

    {

      "long_explanation": "By working the diaphragm zone, therapists can assist clients in releasing tension around the respiratory system and calming the parasympathetic nervous system.",

      "clinical_application": "Especially useful for clients with shallow breathing, anxiety, or thoracic tension.",

    },
    {
      "id": "Reflexology Clinical Application 007",

    },
    {
      "id": "Reflexology Clinical Application 008",
      "clinical_application": "Common in protocols for PMS, burnout, or thyroid dysfunction.",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/modalities/reflexology_clinical_application",
      "mblex_category": "Massage and Bodywork Modalities",
      "created_at": "2025-05-26T21:51:27.242567Z",
      "synonyms": [],
      "short_explanation": "These endocrine glands regulate metabolism, stress, and hormone cycles.",
      "question": "When working with clients experiencing hormonal imbalances, which glandular reflex zones are often emphasized?",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "long_explanation": "Reflexology targeting these areas can promote endocrine balance and homeostasis, especially in stress, menstrual, or thyroid disorders.",

      "answers": [

        "Sinuses and colon",
        "Heart and kidneys"

    {
      "id": "Reflexology Clinical Application 009",

      "mblex_category": "Massage and Bodywork Modalities",

        "Ball of the foot",
        "Lateral heel",
        "Toe tips"
      ],
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "short_explanation": "The arch of the foot mirrors the spinal column in reflexology maps.",
      "variant": 9,
      "source_reference": "Spinal Reflexes and Structural Balance – Practitioner Workbook",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "tags": [
        "spinal_reflex",
        "postural_support",
        "structural_mapping"
      ],
      "clinical_application": "Often used with clients who present postural imbalances, chronic back tension, or scoliosis patterns.",
      "correct": 0,
      "long_explanation": "By stimulating the medial arch, therapists can influence spinal tension, posture, and the central nervous system through reflexive action."
    },
    {
      "id": "Reflexology Clinical Application 010",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/modalities/reflexology_clinical_application",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "correct": 0,
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "short_explanation": "Adrenal reflex points help regulate the stress response.",
      "created_at": "2025-05-26T21:51:27.242567Z",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "long_explanation": "Gentle stimulation of the adrenal reflex zone (typically midfoot lateral to the arch) may assist in calming cortisol surges and supporting the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal (HPA) axis.",
      "tags": [
        "stress_relief",
        "adrenal_glands",
        "HPA_axis_support"
      ],
      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T21:51:27.242567Z",
      "variant": 10,
      "clinical_application": "Used in burnout, fatigue, and post-traumatic stress protocols.",
      "source_reference": "Reflexology and the HPA Axis – Clinical Insights",
      "synonyms": [],
      "mblex_category": "Massage and Bodywork Modalities",
      "question": "Which of the following is a primary intention when working the adrenal reflex point?",
      "answers": [
        "Reduce stress and support adrenal recovery",
        "Stimulate digestion",
        "Relieve sinus pressure",
        "Correct scoliosis"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "Reflexology Comparison 001",
      "mblex_category": "Massage and Bodywork Modalities",
      "correct": 0,
      "clinical_application": "Use reflexology for targeted internal support (e.g., digestion); Shiatsu for systemic energetic balance.",
      "short_explanation": "Reflexology targets reflex zones; Shiatsu follows full-body meridians.",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "answers": [
        "Reflexology focuses on the feet/hands, Shiatsu uses whole-body meridian pathways",
        "Both follow zone therapy only",
        "Shiatsu is for digestion only",
        "Reflexology works only on the ears"
      ],
      "created_at": "2025-05-26T21:51:27.242567Z",
      "question": "What is the primary difference between Shiatsu and reflexology?",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/comparisons/energy_reflexology_modality",
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "tags": [
        "comparison",
        "shiatsu",
        "reflexology"
      ],
      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T21:51:27.242567Z",
      "source_reference": "The Book of Shiatsu - P. Lundberg",
      "variant": 1,
      "synonyms": [],
      "long_explanation": "Reflexology works through mapped zones on feet and hands that mirror organs, while Shiatsu applies pressure along meridian lines over the full body."
    },
    {
      "id": "Reflexology Comparison 002",
      "short_explanation": "Polarity Therapy balances energy via positive/negative poles.",

      "clinical_application": "Ideal for chronic fatigue, trauma, and energetic congestion.",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "synonyms": [],
      "long_explanation": "It integrates Ayurvedic, TCM, and osteopathic ideas to align posture and balance energy fields.",
      "variant": 2
    },
    {

      "short_explanation": "Reflexology uses foot/hand zones, not meridians.",

      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T21:51:27.242567Z",
      "source_reference": "Reflexology: Health at Your Fingertips - Kunz",
      "clinical_application": "Useful for clients preferring non-invasive organ support or reflex zone stimulation.",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "mblex_category": "Massage and Bodywork Modalities",
      "created_at": "2025-05-26T21:51:27.242567Z",
      "long_explanation": "It maps body parts to specific foot/hand regions, affecting organ systems via zone therapy."
    },

    },
    {
      "id": "Reflexology Comparison 007",

    {
      "id": "Reflexology Comparison 009",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "correct": 0,
      "created_at": "2025-05-26T21:51:27.242567Z",
      "short_explanation": "Shiatsu is tactile; Reiki is hands-off or light touch.",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T21:51:27.242567Z",
      "synonyms": [],
      "variant": 9,
      "source_reference": "Reiki Energy Medicine - Judith and Rand W.",
      "clinical_application": "Reiki is ideal for sensitivity, grief, or cancer support.",
      "answers": [

        "Shiatsu is hands-off",
        "Reiki is more anatomical",
        "Reiki uses foot zones"
      ],
      "mblex_category": "Massage and Bodywork Modalities",
      "tags": [
        "shiatsu",
        "reiki",
        "touch_vs_energy"
      ],
      "question": "What differentiates Shiatsu from Reiki in technique?",
      "long_explanation": "Shiatsu manipulates physical meridians; Reiki channels healing energy through intention and energetic flow.",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/comparisons/energy_reflexology_modality"
    },
    {
      "id": "Reflexology Comparison 010",
      "mblex_category": "Massage and Bodywork Modalities",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T21:51:27.242567Z",
      "synonyms": [],
      "created_at": "2025-05-26T21:51:27.242567Z",
      "correct": 0,
      "source_reference": "Acupressure's Potent Points - M. Gach",
      "short_explanation": "Both use focused point work for systemic effects.",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "tags": [
        "reflexology",
        "acupressure",
        "mapped_pressure"
      ],
      "long_explanation": "Though they map differently (zones vs meridians), both aim to affect organs and balance energy via pressure.",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "question": "What is a key similarity between Reflexology and Acupressure?",
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/comparisons/energy_reflexology_modality",
      "answers": [
        "Both apply pressure to specific mapped points",
        "Both use needles",
        "Both are from Ayurveda",
        "Both are for facial treatments"
      ],
      "variant": 10,
      "clinical_application": "Either can be used to support digestion, stress, and pain relief without needles."
    },

      "question": "What is Zone Therapy in relation to reflexology?",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/modalities/reflexology_intro",
      "variant": 4,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T21:57:49.922273Z",
      "clinical_application": "Used to help explain how localized pressure might affect distant parts of the body.",

      "answers": [

        "Dividing the body into longitudinal zones for therapeutic pressure",
        "A form of heat therapy",
        "A technique for diagnosing illness by skin color"
      ],
      "short_explanation": "Zone Therapy divides the body into longitudinal energy zones.",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "long_explanation": "Pressure on these zones was believed to affect all organs within that zone."

    {
      "id": "Reflexology Intro 005",

    {
      "id": "Reflexology Intro 007",
      "question": "Which statement best reflects the theory of reflexology?",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "short_explanation": "Reflexology theory links reflex zones to internal organs.",
      "long_explanation": "It suggests that stimulating reflex points can affect the function of related organs.",
      "clinical_application": "Foundational for understanding the purpose of reflex-based therapy.",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/modalities/reflexology_intro",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "variant": 7,

      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "synonyms": [],
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.142Z",
      "answers": [
        "Muscles can be manipulated to stimulate lymph flow",
        "Pressure on reflex zones corresponds to and affects organs and systems",
        "Essential oils must be applied to reflex zones",
        "Massage must always follow the direction of blood flow"
      ],
      "distractors": [],
      "category": "Modalities",
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T21:57:49.922273Z",

      "variant": 6,
      "mblex_category": "Massage and Bodywork Modalities",
      "question": "Which part of the foot is associated with the heart reflex point?",
      "short_explanation": "The heart reflex lies on the left ball of the foot below the toes.",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T22:01:42.011804Z",
      "long_explanation": "Reflexology charts place the heart on the left foot due to its anatomical position. It's typically just beneath the toes, slightly lateral to center.",
      "correct": 0,
      "question_type": "multiple choice"
    },
    {
      "id": "Reflexology Point Map Short Module 007",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "source_reference": "Foot Reflex Maps – Clinical Reflexology Manual",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "long_explanation": "The kidney reflexes are slightly medial in the arch, playing a key role in energy, detoxification, and vitality.",
      "tags": [

      ],
      "answers": [

        "Toe tips",

      ],

      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/reflexology/point_map_short_module",
      "short_explanation": "Kidney reflexes are located in the center of the arch on both feet.",

      "correct": 0,
      "question": "Where are kidney reflexes located on the feet?",

      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "synonyms": []
    },
    {
      "id": "Reflexology Point Map Short Module 008",

      "created_at": "2025-05-26T22:01:42.011804Z",

      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T22:01:42.011804Z",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/reflexology/point_map_short_module",
      "question": "Which hand reflex zone corresponds to the shoulder and upper arm?",
      "mblex_category": "Massage and Bodywork Modalities",
      "source_reference": "Hand Reflexology Charts – Therapy Guidebook",
      "correct": 0,
      "tags": [
        "limb_upper",
        "shoulder_reflex"
      ],
      "answers": [
        "Outer edge of the palm",
        "Thumb pad",
        "Wrist base",
        "Palm center"
      ],
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "synonyms": [],
      "clinical_application": "Used to address arm tension, rotator cuff issues, or restricted mobility.",
      "long_explanation": "In hand reflexology, the shoulder and arm map to the outer palm beneath the pinky and ring fingers.",
      "variant": 8
    },
    {
      "id": "Reflexology Point Map Short Module 009",
      "source_reference": "Reflexology Protocols for Pelvic Health – Naturopathic Manual",
      "correct": 0,
      "short_explanation": "The bladder reflex is generally found in the center of the heel.",
      "created_at": "2025-05-26T22:01:42.011804Z",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "long_explanation": "This area is used to influence bladder tone, urination frequency, and pelvic organ function.",
      "mblex_category": "Massage and Bodywork Modalities",
      "synonyms": [],
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "answers": [
        "Heel center",
        "Ball of foot",
        "Toe pads",
        "Arch edge"
      ],
      "variant": 9,
      "question": "Where is the bladder reflex point found?",
      "tags": [
        "organ_bladder",
        "pelvic_health"
      ],
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/reflexology/point_map_short_module",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T22:01:42.011804Z",
      "clinical_application": "Helpful in cases of fluid retention or overactive bladder."
    },
    {
      "id": "Reflexology Point Map Short Module 010",
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "correct": 2,
      "mblex_category": "Massage and Bodywork Modalities",
      "question": "What part of the hand reflects the cervical spine in hand reflexology?",
      "variant": 10,
      "source_reference": "Hand Reflex Spine Mapping – Illustrated Guide",
      "created_at": "2025-05-26T22:01:42.011804Z",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/reflexology/point_map_short_module",
      "answers": [
        "Base of the thumb",
        "Fingertip pads",
        "Inner edge of the thumb",
        "Palm center"
      ],
      "synonyms": [],
      "tags": [
        "spine_cervical",
        "neck_support"
      ],
      "long_explanation": "This thumb region corresponds to the neck and cervical vertebrae in many reflexology charts.",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T22:01:42.011804Z",
      "clinical_application": "Applied for neck pain, tension, or stiffness in hand-based treatments.",
      "short_explanation": "The cervical spine is mirrored along the inner edge of the thumb.",
      "question_type": "multiple choice"
    },
    {
      "id": "Rolfing Intro 001",
      "category": "Modalities",
      "question": "What is the primary goal of Rolfing Structural Integration?",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "answers": [
        "To relieve joint dislocations",
        "To align the body in gravity by manipulating fascia",
        "To enhance lymphatic flow",
        "To reduce emotional tension only"
      ],
      "synonyms": [],
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "short_explanation": "Rolfing aims to realign the body for optimal function using fascial manipulation.",
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0,

      },
      "source_reference": "Rolfing and Physical Reality - Ida Rolf",

    {

    {
      "id": "Modalities Schools 003",
      "question": "Shiatsu is based on which of the following principles?",
      "synonyms": [],
      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T22:05:58.088388Z",
      "long_explanation": "Rooted in Traditional Chinese Medicine, Shiatsu applies pressure along energy channels to restore vitality.",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/modalities/schools_of_thought",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.143Z",
      "topic": "modalities",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "distractors": [],
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "answers": [
        "Lymphatic drainage and joint mobility",
        "Trigger points and fascia release",
        "Energy flow through meridians and acupressure points",
        "Muscle hypertrophy and stimulation"
      ],
      "short_explanation": "Shiatsu uses meridian pathways and acupressure to balance energy.",
      "source_reference": "Shiatsu Theory and Practice - Carola Beresford-Cooke",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "correct": 2,
      "tags": [

        "modality_lymphatic_drainage",

        "anatomy_fascia",
        "anatomy_muscles",
        "anatomy_meridians",
        "joint_joint"
      ],
      "last_answered_at": null,

      "category": "Modalities",
      "clinical_application": "Commonly applied for fatigue, stress, and musculoskeletal imbalances.",

      "question": "What distinguishes Zen Shiatsu from other styles?",

        "anatomy_muscles",
        "purpose_stress",
        "joint_joint"
      ],
      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T22:08:44.014226Z",
      "topic": "modalities",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "clinical_application": "Used in fatigue, IBS, anxiety, or chronic stiffness.",
      "distractors": [],
      "long_explanation": "Stimulating meridians can calm the nervous system, support gut motility, and ease musculoskeletal restrictions.",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "synonyms": [],
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "category": "Modalities",
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "answers": [
        "Improving digestion, stress relief, joint mobility",
        "Raising body temperature",
        "Reducing cholesterol",
        "Muscle growth"
      ],
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/shiatsu/intro",
      "tags": [
        "modality_shiatsu",
        "anatomy_joint",
        "anatomy_muscles",
        "purpose_stress",

      ],
      "variant": 9,
      "created_at": "2025-05-26T22:08:44.014226Z",
      "short_explanation": "Shiatsu has many systemic applications.",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "question": "Shiatsu is often recommended for which of the following?",
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "last_answered_at": null
    },
    {
      "id": "Shiatsu 010",
      "created_at": "2025-05-26T22:08:44.014226Z",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "answers": [
        "Pregnancy",
        "High blood pressure",
        "Severe fractures or contagious illness",
        "Mild fatigue"
      ],
      "synonyms": [],
      "topic": "modalities",
      "category": "Modalities",
      "distractors": [],
      "source_reference": "Safety and Scope - Massage Law and Practice Guide",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T22:08:44.014226Z",
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "short_explanation": "Shiatsu avoids unsafe or unstable conditions.",
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "tags": [
        "modality_shiatsu",
        "technique_pressure"
      ],
      "correct": 2,
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "question": "Shiatsu typically avoids treatment in cases of:",
      "variant": 10,
      "clinical_application": "Ensures safe practice and protects both therapist and client.",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/shiatsu/intro",
      "long_explanation": "Practitioners are trained to screen for contraindications, especially structural instability and infection.",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "keywords": [
        "modality_shiatsu",
        "technique_pressure"
      ],
      "difficulty": "intermediate"
    },
    {
      "id": "Special 001",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "subcategory": "Pregnancy",
      "question": "What is an important precaution when massaging a client in the first trimester of pregnancy? [Variant 1]",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.486Z",
      "topic": "modalities",
      "difficulty": "easy",
      "long_explanation": "While massage can be safe during pregnancy, specific areas such as the sacrum, ankles, and lower abdomen are avoided during the first trimester due to increased sensitivity and potential stimulation.",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.144Z",
      "category": "Massage Considerations",
      "variant": 1,
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "modality_deep_tissue",
        "technique_pressure",
        "anatomy_tissues",
        "client",
        "bone_sacrum"
      ],
      "answers": [
        "Always avoid the upper back",
        "Use deep abdominal pressure",
        "Avoid high-risk pressure points such as the ankles and sacrum",
        "Perform deep tissue on the low back"
      ],
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "synonyms": [
        "prenatal massage",
        "pregnancy considerations"
      ],
      "distractors": [],
      "short_explanation": "Avoid certain pressure points that may stimulate uterine contractions.",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/modalities/special_populations",
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "modality_deep_tissue",
        "technique_pressure",
        "anatomy_tissues",
        "client",
        "bone_sacrum"
      ],
      "correct": 2,
      "clinical_application": "Use a gentle, nurturing approach and avoid prone positioning after the first trimester.",
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Special Populations Guidelines",
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations"
    },
    {
      "id": "Special 002",
      "clinical_application": "Use bolstering for comfort, monitor for bruising, and check for medications that affect pressure tolerance.",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "relevance_score": 4,
      "subcategory": "Elderly Clients",
      "answers": [
        "Apply maximum pressure to compensate for thick skin",
        "Shorten session time and use lighter pressure",
        "Focus only on trigger points",
        "Always perform massage in a prone position"
      ],
      "distractors": [],
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.144Z",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.486Z",
      "variant": 2,
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/modalities/special_populations",
      "correct": 1,
      "long_explanation": "Aging skin, circulation, and joint mobility require gentler techniques and adjusted positioning. Sessions are often shorter due to lower tolerance or medical conditions.",
      "category": "Massage Considerations",
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "short_explanation": "Older adults often have fragile tissues and may fatigue easily.",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "source_reference": "AMTA Geriatric Massage Guidelines",
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "anatomy_joint",
        "technique_pressure",
        "anatomy_tissues",
        "concept_focus",
        "population_geriatric",
        "joint_joint",
        "organ_skin"
      ],
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "question": "What adjustment is typically needed when working with geriatric clients? [Variant 2]",
      "synonyms": [
        "aging clients",
        "senior massage"
      ],
      "topic": "modalities",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "anatomy_joint",
        "technique_pressure",
        "anatomy_tissues",
        "concept_focus",
        "population_geriatric",
        "joint_joint",
        "organ_skin"
      ],
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "Special 003",
      "correct": 1,
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "technique_pressure",
        "purpose_relaxation",
        "ethics_consent",
        "modality_oils",
        "population_pediatric"
      ],
      "short_explanation": "Always obtain guardian consent and allow presence during the session.",
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "clinical_application": "Use age-appropriate language and modify pressure and timing for the child's needs.",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.144Z",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/modalities/special_populations",
      "topic": "modalities",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "technique_pressure",
        "purpose_relaxation",
        "ethics_consent",
        "modality_oils",
        "population_pediatric"
      ],
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "question": "When massaging children under 12, what is a legal and ethical requirement? [Variant 3]",
      "relevance_score": 3,
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "category": "Massage Considerations",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "synonyms": [
        "child clients",
        "juvenile massage"
      ],
      "subcategory": "Pediatric Massage",
      "distractors": [],
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.486Z",
      "answers": [
        "The child must stay fully clothed",
        "Parent or guardian consent and presence is required",
        "Use only essential oils for relaxation",
        "Only perform massage seated"
      ],
      "long_explanation": "Pediatric massage requires special consent and communication practices. Guardians must be informed of all procedures and often remain in the room for safety and reassurance.",
      "variant": 3,
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "source_reference": "FSMTB and State Licensing Boards"
    },
    {
      "id": "Special 004",
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "correct": 2,
      "clinical_application": "Obtain medical clearance and adjust pressure, duration, and area to the client's energy and treatment stage.",
      "source_reference": "Society for Oncology Massage (S4OM); FSMTB Guidelines",
      "category": "Massage Considerations",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "subcategory": "Chronic Illness",
      "synonyms": [
        "oncology massage",
        "cancer precautions"
      ],
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "topic": "modalities",
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.486Z",
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.144Z",
      "short_explanation": "Lymph node removal can increase risk of lymphedema with massage.",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_deep_tissue",
        "technique_friction",
        "technique_pressure",
        "anatomy_tissues",
        "condition_pain",
        "condition_cancer",
        "client",
        "system_immune",
        "modality_massage_consideration"
      ],
      "variant": 4,
      "question": "Which of the following is a key massage consideration for a client undergoing cancer treatment? [Variant 4]",
      "relevance_score": 2,
      "distractors": [],
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/modalities/special_populations",
      "tags": [
        "modality_deep_tissue",
        "technique_friction",
        "technique_pressure",
        "anatomy_tissues",
        "condition_pain",
        "condition_cancer",
        "client",
        "system_immune",
        "modality_massage_consideration"
      ],
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "answers": [
        "Always use deep tissue massage for pain",
        "Apply cross-fiber friction over the tumor site",
        "Avoid lymphatic areas if lymph nodes are removed",
        "Only perform massage on the scalp"
      ],
      "long_explanation": "Massage therapists should avoid deep or lymphatic work in areas where lymph nodes were removed or radiated, especially if there's swelling. Adjust techniques for immune sensitivity and fatigue."
    },
    {
      "id": "Special 005",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/modalities/special_populations",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "client",
        "concept_assists",
        "concept_assisted",
        "concept_targeted",
        "condition_injuries",
        "plan_plan"
      ],
      "distractors": [],
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "short_explanation": "Session plans and setups must be fully adapted to the client’s abilities and comfort.",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.144Z",
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "subcategory": "Disabilities & Mobility Impairments",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.486Z",
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "client",
        "concept_assists",
        "concept_assisted",
        "concept_targeted",
        "condition_injuries",
        "plan_plan"
      ],
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "long_explanation": "Clients with spinal injuries, amputations, or other mobility needs may require flexible positioning, longer setup time, or targeted therapeutic goals. Always ask about comfort and support needs.",
      "category": "Massage Considerations",
      "answers": [
        "Always use prone positioning",
        "Use generic protocols for efficiency",
        "Customize session plan and positioning to individual needs",
        "Avoid working with assistive devices present"
      ],
      "question": "What is a key principle when working with clients with physical disabilities or mobility challenges? [Variant 5]",
      "clinical_application": "Ensure safe transfers, use bolsters or seated massage as needed, and promote independence where possible.",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "synonyms": [
        "mobility limitations",
        "physical disability"
      ],
      "source_reference": "AMTA Guidelines for Special Populations",
      "correct": 2,
      "topic": "modalities",
      "variant": 5
    },

      ],
      "short_explanation": "Avoid certain pressure points that may stimulate uterine contractions.",
      "variant": 11,
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Special Populations Guidelines"
    },
    {
      "id": "Special 012",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.486Z",
      "topic": "modalities",
      "category": "Massage Considerations",
      "synonyms": [
        "aging clients",
        "senior massage"
      ],
      "question": "What adjustment is typically needed when working with geriatric clients? [Variant 12]",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "anatomy_joint",
        "technique_pressure",
        "anatomy_tissues",
        "concept_focus",
        "population_geriatric",
        "joint_joint",
        "organ_skin"
      ],
      "answers": [
        "Apply maximum pressure to compensate for thick skin",
        "Shorten session time and use lighter pressure",
        "Focus only on trigger points",
        "Always perform massage in a prone position"
      ],
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "distractors": [],
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/modalities/special_populations",
      "variant": 12,
      "relevance_score": 2,
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "long_explanation": "Aging skin, circulation, and joint mobility require gentler techniques and adjusted positioning. Sessions are often shorter due to lower tolerance or medical conditions.",
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "subcategory": "Elderly Clients",
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "clinical_application": "Use bolstering for comfort, monitor for bruising, and check for medications that affect pressure tolerance.",
      "correct": 1,
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.144Z",
      "short_explanation": "Older adults often have fragile tissues and may fatigue easily.",
      "source_reference": "AMTA Geriatric Massage Guidelines",
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",

        "technique_pressure",
        "anatomy_tissues",

    {
      "id": "Special 013",
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.486Z",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.144Z",
      "topic": "modalities",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "answers": [
        "The child must stay fully clothed",

      "difficulty_rating": 3,

      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,

      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "long_explanation": "Pediatric massage requires special consent and communication practices. Guardians must be informed of all procedures and often remain in the room for safety and reassurance.",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "short_explanation": "Always obtain guardian consent and allow presence during the session."
    },
    {
      "id": "Special 014",

      "relevance_score": 4,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.486Z",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/modalities/special_populations",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "short_explanation": "Lymph node removal can increase risk of lymphedema with massage.",
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "distractors": [],
      "variant": 14,
      "category": "Massage Considerations",
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "answers": [
        "Always use deep tissue massage for pain",
        "Apply cross-fiber friction over the tumor site",
        "Avoid lymphatic areas if lymph nodes are removed",
        "Only perform massage on the scalp"
      ],
      "question": "Which of the following is a key massage consideration for a client undergoing cancer treatment? [Variant 14]",
      "subcategory": "Chronic Illness",
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "clinical_application": "Obtain medical clearance and adjust pressure, duration, and area to the client's energy and treatment stage.",
      "topic": "modalities",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.144Z",
      "long_explanation": "Massage therapists should avoid deep or lymphatic work in areas where lymph nodes were removed or radiated, especially if there's swelling. Adjust techniques for immune sensitivity and fatigue.",
      "correct": 2,
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "source_reference": "Society for Oncology Massage (S4OM); FSMTB Guidelines",
      "synonyms": [
        "oncology massage",
        "cancer precautions"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "Special 015",
      "relevance_score": 3,
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "client",
        "concept_assists",
        "concept_assisted",
        "concept_targeted",
        "condition_injuries",
        "plan_plan"
      ],
      "distractors": [],
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "correct": 2,
      "source_reference": "AMTA Guidelines for Special Populations",
      "variant": 15,
      "category": "Massage Considerations",
      "clinical_application": "Ensure safe transfers, use bolsters or seated massage as needed, and promote independence where possible.",
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "topic": "modalities",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.144Z",
      "answers": [
        "Always use prone positioning",
        "Use generic protocols for efficiency",
        "Customize session plan and positioning to individual needs",
        "Avoid working with assistive devices present"
      ],
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "client",
        "concept_assists",
        "concept_assisted",
        "concept_targeted",
        "condition_injuries",
        "plan_plan"
      ],
      "long_explanation": "Clients with spinal injuries, amputations, or other mobility needs may require flexible positioning, longer setup time, or targeted therapeutic goals. Always ask about comfort and support needs.",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.486Z",
      "question": "What is a key principle when working with clients with physical disabilities or mobility challenges? [Variant 15]",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/modalities/special_populations",
      "synonyms": [
        "mobility limitations",
        "physical disability"
      ],
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "short_explanation": "Session plans and setups must be fully adapted to the client’s abilities and comfort.",
      "subcategory": "Disabilities & Mobility Impairments",
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations"
    },
    {
      "id": "Special 016",
      "difficulty": "easy",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Special Populations Guidelines",
      "topic": "modalities",
      "long_explanation": "While massage can be safe during pregnancy, specific areas such as the sacrum, ankles, and lower abdomen are avoided during the first trimester due to increased sensitivity and potential stimulation.",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "subcategory": "Pregnancy",
      "short_explanation": "Avoid certain pressure points that may stimulate uterine contractions.",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/modalities/special_populations",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "synonyms": [
        "prenatal massage",
        "pregnancy considerations"
      ],
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.486Z",
      "category": "Massage Considerations",
      "distractors": [],
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "question": "What is an important precaution when massaging a client in the first trimester of pregnancy? [Variant 16]",
      "correct": 2,
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "modality_deep_tissue",
        "technique_pressure",
        "anatomy_tissues",
        "client",
        "bone_sacrum"
      ],
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "variant": 16,
      "clinical_application": "Use a gentle, nurturing approach and avoid prone positioning after the first trimester.",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.144Z",
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "modality_deep_tissue",
        "technique_pressure",
        "anatomy_tissues",
        "client",
        "bone_sacrum"
      ],
      "answers": [
        "Always avoid the upper back",
        "Use deep abdominal pressure",
        "Avoid high-risk pressure points such as the ankles and sacrum",
        "Perform deep tissue on the low back"
      ],
      "relevance_score": 2
    },

      ],
      "source_reference": "Society for Oncology Massage (S4OM); FSMTB Guidelines",
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.486Z",
      "variant": 24,
      "category": "Massage Considerations",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/modalities/special_populations",
      "short_explanation": "Lymph node removal can increase risk of lymphedema with massage.",
      "relevance_score": 2,
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "tags": [
        "modality_deep_tissue",
        "technique_friction",
        "technique_pressure",
        "anatomy_tissues",
        "condition_pain",
        "condition_cancer",
        "client",
        "system_immune",
        "modality_massage_consideration"
      ],
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "distractors": [],
      "question": "Which of the following is a key massage consideration for a client undergoing cancer treatment? [Variant 24]",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.144Z",
      "answers": [
        "Always use deep tissue massage for pain",
        "Apply cross-fiber friction over the tumor site",
        "Avoid lymphatic areas if lymph nodes are removed",
        "Only perform massage on the scalp"
      ],
      "subcategory": "Chronic Illness",
      "synonyms": [
        "oncology massage",
        "cancer precautions"
      ],
      "clinical_application": "Obtain medical clearance and adjust pressure, duration, and area to the client's energy and treatment stage.",
      "long_explanation": "Massage therapists should avoid deep or lymphatic work in areas where lymph nodes were removed or radiated, especially if there's swelling. Adjust techniques for immune sensitivity and fatigue."
    },
    {
      "id": "Special 025",
      "topic": "modalities",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "client",
        "concept_assists",
        "concept_assisted",
        "concept_targeted",
        "condition_injuries",
        "plan_plan"
      ],
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/modalities/special_populations",
      "subcategory": "Disabilities & Mobility Impairments",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "client",
        "concept_assists",
        "concept_assisted",
        "concept_targeted",
        "condition_injuries",
        "plan_plan"
      ],
      "long_explanation": "Clients with spinal injuries, amputations, or other mobility needs may require flexible positioning, longer setup time, or targeted therapeutic goals. Always ask about comfort and support needs.",
      "short_explanation": "Session plans and setups must be fully adapted to the client’s abilities and comfort.",
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "answers": [
        "Always use prone positioning",
        "Use generic protocols for efficiency",
        "Customize session plan and positioning to individual needs",
        "Avoid working with assistive devices present"
      ],
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.486Z",
      "correct": 2,
      "question": "What is a key principle when working with clients with physical disabilities or mobility challenges? [Variant 25]",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "variant": 25,
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "category": "Massage Considerations",
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "distractors": [],
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.144Z",
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "source_reference": "AMTA Guidelines for Special Populations",
      "clinical_application": "Ensure safe transfers, use bolsters or seated massage as needed, and promote independence where possible.",
      "synonyms": [
        "mobility limitations",
        "physical disability"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "Special 026",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/modalities/special_populations",
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "modality_deep_tissue",
        "technique_pressure",
        "anatomy_tissues",
        "client",
        "bone_sacrum"
      ],
      "answers": [
        "Always avoid the upper back",
        "Use deep abdominal pressure",
        "Avoid high-risk pressure points such as the ankles and sacrum",
        "Perform deep tissue on the low back"
      ],
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "relevance_score": 4,
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "modality_deep_tissue",
        "technique_pressure",
        "anatomy_tissues",
        "client",
        "bone_sacrum"
      ],
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.486Z",
      "correct": 2,
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Special Populations Guidelines",
      "long_explanation": "While massage can be safe during pregnancy, specific areas such as the sacrum, ankles, and lower abdomen are avoided during the first trimester due to increased sensitivity and potential stimulation.",
      "short_explanation": "Avoid certain pressure points that may stimulate uterine contractions.",
      "synonyms": [
        "prenatal massage",
        "pregnancy considerations"
      ],
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.144Z",
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "topic": "modalities",
      "variant": 26,
      "clinical_application": "Use a gentle, nurturing approach and avoid prone positioning after the first trimester.",
      "subcategory": "Pregnancy",
      "distractors": [],
      "difficulty": "easy",
      "question": "What is an important precaution when massaging a client in the first trimester of pregnancy? [Variant 26]",
      "category": "Massage Considerations",
      "last_answered_at": null
    },
    {
      "id": "Special 027",
      "question": "What adjustment is typically needed when working with geriatric clients? [Variant 27]",
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "anatomy_joint",
        "technique_pressure",
        "anatomy_tissues",
        "concept_focus",
        "population_geriatric",
        "joint_joint",
        "organ_skin"
      ],
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.486Z",
      "variant": 27,
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "synonyms": [
        "aging clients",
        "senior massage"
      ],
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "anatomy_joint",
        "technique_pressure",
        "anatomy_tissues",
        "concept_focus",
        "population_geriatric",
        "joint_joint",
        "organ_skin"
      ],
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "relevance_score": 3,
      "clinical_application": "Use bolstering for comfort, monitor for bruising, and check for medications that affect pressure tolerance.",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/modalities/special_populations",
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "answers": [
        "Apply maximum pressure to compensate for thick skin",
        "Shorten session time and use lighter pressure",
        "Focus only on trigger points",
        "Always perform massage in a prone position"
      ],
      "category": "Massage Considerations",
      "source_reference": "AMTA Geriatric Massage Guidelines",
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.144Z",
      "long_explanation": "Aging skin, circulation, and joint mobility require gentler techniques and adjusted positioning. Sessions are often shorter due to lower tolerance or medical conditions.",
      "correct": 1,
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "distractors": [],
      "topic": "modalities",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "subcategory": "Elderly Clients",
      "short_explanation": "Older adults often have fragile tissues and may fatigue easily."
    },
    {
      "id": "Special 028",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "technique_pressure",
        "purpose_relaxation",
        "ethics_consent",
        "modality_oils",
        "population_pediatric"
      ],
      "topic": "modalities",
      "category": "Massage Considerations",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "technique_pressure",
        "purpose_relaxation",
        "ethics_consent",
        "modality_oils",
        "population_pediatric"
      ],
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "subcategory": "Pediatric Massage",
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/modalities/special_populations",
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "answers": [
        "The child must stay fully clothed",
        "Parent or guardian consent and presence is required",
        "Use only essential oils for relaxation",
        "Only perform massage seated"
      ],
      "relevance_score": 2,
      "clinical_application": "Use age-appropriate language and modify pressure and timing for the child's needs.",
      "source_reference": "FSMTB and State Licensing Boards",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.486Z",
      "variant": 28,
      "long_explanation": "Pediatric massage requires special consent and communication practices. Guardians must be informed of all procedures and often remain in the room for safety and reassurance.",
      "synonyms": [
        "child clients",
        "juvenile massage"
      ],
      "correct": 1,
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "short_explanation": "Always obtain guardian consent and allow presence during the session.",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.144Z",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "question": "When massaging children under 12, what is a legal and ethical requirement? [Variant 28]",
      "distractors": []
    },

      "category": "Massage Considerations",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.486Z",
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "difficulty": "easy",
      "question": "What is an important precaution when massaging a client in the first trimester of pregnancy? [Variant 36]",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "modality_deep_tissue",
        "technique_pressure",
        "anatomy_tissues",
        "client",
        "bone_sacrum"
      ],
      "subcategory": "Pregnancy",
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "long_explanation": "While massage can be safe during pregnancy, specific areas such as the sacrum, ankles, and lower abdomen are avoided during the first trimester due to increased sensitivity and potential stimulation.",
      "correct": 2,
      "short_explanation": "Avoid certain pressure points that may stimulate uterine contractions.",
      "clinical_application": "Use a gentle, nurturing approach and avoid prone positioning after the first trimester.",
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Special Populations Guidelines",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "topic": "modalities",
      "variant": 36
    },
    {
      "id": "Special 037",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "anatomy_joint",
        "technique_pressure",
        "anatomy_tissues",
        "concept_focus",
        "population_geriatric",
        "joint_joint",
        "organ_skin"
      ],
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "anatomy_joint",
        "technique_pressure",
        "anatomy_tissues",
        "concept_focus",
        "population_geriatric",
        "joint_joint",
        "organ_skin"
      ],
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/modalities/special_populations",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.486Z",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "distractors": [],
      "short_explanation": "Older adults often have fragile tissues and may fatigue easily.",
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "variant": 37,
      "category": "Massage Considerations",
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "answers": [
        "Apply maximum pressure to compensate for thick skin",
        "Shorten session time and use lighter pressure",
        "Focus only on trigger points",
        "Always perform massage in a prone position"
      ],
      "question": "What adjustment is typically needed when working with geriatric clients? [Variant 37]",
      "subcategory": "Elderly Clients",
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "clinical_application": "Use bolstering for comfort, monitor for bruising, and check for medications that affect pressure tolerance.",
      "topic": "modalities",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.144Z",
      "long_explanation": "Aging skin, circulation, and joint mobility require gentler techniques and adjusted positioning. Sessions are often shorter due to lower tolerance or medical conditions.",
      "correct": 1,
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "synonyms": [
        "aging clients",
        "senior massage"
      ],
      "source_reference": "AMTA Geriatric Massage Guidelines"
    },
    {
      "id": "Special 038",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "answers": [
        "The child must stay fully clothed",
        "Parent or guardian consent and presence is required",
        "Use only essential oils for relaxation",
        "Only perform massage seated"
      ],
      "relevance_score": 4,
      "category": "Massage Considerations",
      "correct": 1,
      "distractors": [],
      "source_reference": "FSMTB and State Licensing Boards",
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "question": "When massaging children under 12, what is a legal and ethical requirement? [Variant 38]",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/modalities/special_populations",
      "long_explanation": "Pediatric massage requires special consent and communication practices. Guardians must be informed of all procedures and often remain in the room for safety and reassurance.",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.486Z",
      "short_explanation": "Always obtain guardian consent and allow presence during the session.",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "subcategory": "Pediatric Massage",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "technique_pressure",
        "purpose_relaxation",
        "ethics_consent",
        "modality_oils",
        "population_pediatric"
      ],
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "variant": 38,
      "synonyms": [
        "child clients",
        "juvenile massage"
      ],
      "clinical_application": "Use age-appropriate language and modify pressure and timing for the child's needs.",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.144Z",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "technique_pressure",
        "purpose_relaxation",
        "ethics_consent",
        "modality_oils",
        "population_pediatric"
      ],
      "topic": "modalities"
    },
    {
      "id": "Special 039",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.486Z",
      "distractors": [],
      "long_explanation": "Massage therapists should avoid deep or lymphatic work in areas where lymph nodes were removed or radiated, especially if there's swelling. Adjust techniques for immune sensitivity and fatigue.",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "clinical_application": "Obtain medical clearance and adjust pressure, duration, and area to the client's energy and treatment stage.",
      "correct": 2,
      "short_explanation": "Lymph node removal can increase risk of lymphedema with massage.",
      "variant": 39,
      "category": "Massage Considerations",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "answers": [
        "Always use deep tissue massage for pain",
        "Apply cross-fiber friction over the tumor site",
        "Avoid lymphatic areas if lymph nodes are removed",
        "Only perform massage on the scalp"
      ],
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "source_reference": "Society for Oncology Massage (S4OM); FSMTB Guidelines",
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "topic": "modalities",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_deep_tissue",
        "technique_friction",
        "technique_pressure",
        "anatomy_tissues",
        "condition_pain",
        "condition_cancer",
        "client",
        "system_immune",
        "modality_massage_consideration"
      ],
      "question": "Which of the following is a key massage consideration for a client undergoing cancer treatment? [Variant 39]",
      "subcategory": "Chronic Illness",
      "relevance_score": 3,
      "tags": [
        "modality_deep_tissue",
        "technique_friction",
        "technique_pressure",
        "anatomy_tissues",
        "condition_pain",
        "condition_cancer",
        "client",
        "system_immune",
        "modality_massage_consideration"
      ],
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "synonyms": [
        "oncology massage",
        "cancer precautions"
      ],
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.144Z",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/modalities/special_populations",
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "Special 040",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/modalities/special_populations",
      "distractors": [],
      "long_explanation": "Clients with spinal injuries, amputations, or other mobility needs may require flexible positioning, longer setup time, or targeted therapeutic goals. Always ask about comfort and support needs.",
      "correct": 2,
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "topic": "modalities",
      "synonyms": [
        "mobility limitations",
        "physical disability"
      ],
      "relevance_score": 2,
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "client",
        "concept_assists",
        "concept_assisted",
        "concept_targeted",
        "condition_injuries",
        "plan_plan"
      ],
      "clinical_application": "Ensure safe transfers, use bolsters or seated massage as needed, and promote independence where possible.",
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "client",
        "concept_assists",
        "concept_assisted",
        "concept_targeted",
        "condition_injuries",
        "plan_plan"
      ],
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "source_reference": "AMTA Guidelines for Special Populations",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.486Z",
      "subcategory": "Disabilities & Mobility Impairments",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "answers": [
        "Always use prone positioning",
        "Use generic protocols for efficiency",
        "Customize session plan and positioning to individual needs",
        "Avoid working with assistive devices present"
      ],
      "short_explanation": "Session plans and setups must be fully adapted to the client’s abilities and comfort.",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.144Z",
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "category": "Massage Considerations",
      "variant": 40,
      "question": "What is a key principle when working with clients with physical disabilities or mobility challenges? [Variant 40]"
    },

      "long_explanation": "Pediatric massage requires special consent and communication practices. Guardians must be informed of all procedures and often remain in the room for safety and reassurance.",
