        "modality_massage",
        "technique_pressure",
        "purpose_relaxation",
        "ethics_consent",
        "modality_oils",
        "population_pediatric"
      ],
      "answers": [
        "The child must stay fully clothed",
        "Parent or guardian consent and presence is required",
        "Use only essential oils for relaxation",
        "Only perform massage seated"
      ],
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations"
    },
    {
      "id": "Special 049",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_deep_tissue",
        "technique_friction",
        "technique_pressure",
        "anatomy_tissues",
        "condition_pain",
        "condition_cancer",
        "client",
        "system_immune",
        "modality_massage_consideration"
      ],
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.486Z",
      "correct": 2,
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "variant": 49,
      "answers": [
        "Always use deep tissue massage for pain",
        "Apply cross-fiber friction over the tumor site",
        "Avoid lymphatic areas if lymph nodes are removed",
        "Only perform massage on the scalp"
      ],
      "tags": [
        "modality_deep_tissue",
        "technique_friction",
        "technique_pressure",
        "anatomy_tissues",
        "condition_pain",
        "condition_cancer",
        "client",
        "system_immune",
        "modality_massage_consideration"
      ],
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "category": "Massage Considerations",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.144Z",
      "subcategory": "Chronic Illness",
      "topic": "modalities",
      "short_explanation": "Lymph node removal can increase risk of lymphedema with massage.",
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "distractors": [],
      "source_reference": "Society for Oncology Massage (S4OM); FSMTB Guidelines",
      "synonyms": [
        "oncology massage",
        "cancer precautions"
      ],
      "long_explanation": "Massage therapists should avoid deep or lymphatic work in areas where lymph nodes were removed or radiated, especially if there's swelling. Adjust techniques for immune sensitivity and fatigue.",
      "question": "Which of the following is a key massage consideration for a client undergoing cancer treatment? [Variant 49]",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/modalities/special_populations",
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "clinical_application": "Obtain medical clearance and adjust pressure, duration, and area to the client's energy and treatment stage."
    },
    {
      "id": "Special 050",
      "source_reference": "AMTA Guidelines for Special Populations",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "correct": 2,
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "clinical_application": "Ensure safe transfers, use bolsters or seated massage as needed, and promote independence where possible.",
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "category": "Massage Considerations",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.486Z",
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "subcategory": "Disabilities & Mobility Impairments",
      "variant": 50,
      "long_explanation": "Clients with spinal injuries, amputations, or other mobility needs may require flexible positioning, longer setup time, or targeted therapeutic goals. Always ask about comfort and support needs.",
      "distractors": [],
      "short_explanation": "Session plans and setups must be fully adapted to the client’s abilities and comfort.",
      "synonyms": [
        "mobility limitations",
        "physical disability"
      ],
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/modalities/special_populations",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.144Z",
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "client",
        "concept_assists",
        "concept_assisted",
        "concept_targeted",
        "condition_injuries",
        "plan_plan"
      ],
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "topic": "modalities",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "client",
        "concept_assists",
        "concept_assisted",
        "concept_targeted",
        "condition_injuries",
        "plan_plan"
      ],
      "answers": [
        "Always use prone positioning",
        "Use generic protocols for efficiency",
        "Customize session plan and positioning to individual needs",
        "Avoid working with assistive devices present"
      ],
      "relevance_score": 4,
      "question": "What is a key principle when working with clients with physical disabilities or mobility challenges? [Variant 50]"
    },
    {
      "id": "Thai Massage 001",

        "Swedish massage"
      ],
      "question": "Thai massage is also known as:",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/modalities/thai_massage",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "short_explanation": "Nuad Thai is the traditional name for Thai massage.",
      "topic": "modalities",
      "created_at": "2025-05-26T22:11:36.359925Z",
      "correct": 0,
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "modality_swedish",
        "modality_shiatsu",
        "modality_tuina",
        "modality_thai_massage"
      ],
      "clinical_application": "Clients may ask for 'Thai yoga massage' or 'Nuad Thai' by name, so practitioners should be familiar with all terms.",
      "long_explanation": "Nuad Thai is the official term in Thailand, referring to a combination of acupressure, assisted yoga, and energy line work.",
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "modality_swedish",
        "modality_shiatsu",
        "modality_tuina",
        "modality_thai_massage"
      ],
      "stats": {

        "attempts": 0
      },
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "variant": 1,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T22:11:36.359925Z",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "relevance_score": 5
    },
    {
      "id": "Thai Massage 002",
      "category": "Modalities",
      "tags": [

        "technique_compression",
        "technique_cupping",
        "technique_pressure",
        "modality_thai_massage"
      ],
      "short_explanation": "Thai massage blends stretching and pressure work.",
      "distractors": [],
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "variant": 2,
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/modalities/thai_massage",
      "question": "Thai massage is characterized by:",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "correct": 2,
      "synonyms": [],
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "topic": "modalities",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",

        "modality_thai_massage"
      ],
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T22:11:36.359925Z",
      "source_reference": "The Art of Traditional Thai Massage - C. Nalin",
      "long_explanation": "It includes joint mobilization, acupressure, and assisted poses to promote energy flow through Sen lines.",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "clinical_application": "Ideal for active individuals needing movement therapy or flexibility enhancement.",
      "created_at": "2025-05-26T22:11:36.359925Z",
      "answers": [
        "Deep pressure using only elbows",
        "Gentle stroking",
        "Passive stretching and compression along energy lines",
        "Cupping and scraping"
      ],
      "question_type": "multiple choice"
    },
    {
      "id": "Thai Massage 003",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "short_explanation": "Thai massage is a clothed, mat-based treatment.",
      "created_at": "2025-05-26T22:11:36.359925Z",
      "category": "Modalities",
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "synonyms": [],
      "clinical_application": "Useful for clients uncomfortable with oil-based massage or desiring deeper movement integration.",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "client",
        "modality_thai_massage"
      ],
      "source_reference": "Thai Yoga Massage Handbook - Lotus School",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "question": "Thai massage is traditionally performed with the client:",
      "distractors": [],
      "correct": 1,
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/modalities/thai_massage",
      "variant": 3,
      "topic": "modalities",
      "answers": [
        "Nude on a heated table",
        "Clothed on a floor mat",
        "Standing in water",
        "Seated on a stool"
      ],
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T22:11:36.359925Z",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "client",
        "modality_thai_massage"
      ],
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "long_explanation": "Clients wear loose clothing and lie on a mat, which allows full-body assisted movements and leverage-based techniques."
    },

    {

      "question_type": "multiple choice",

      },
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/modalities/thai_massage",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "purpose_stress",
        "contraindication",
        "modality_thai_massage"
      ],
      "short_explanation": "Deep stretches may harm unstable structures.",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "topic": "modalities",
      "synonyms": [],
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "correct": 1,
      "question": "Which of the following is a contraindication for Thai massage?",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T22:11:36.359925Z",
      "source_reference": "Massage Cautions and Contraindications - Clinical Manual",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "category": "Modalities",
      "long_explanation": "Thai massage involves traction and mobilization, which may be unsafe post-surgery or with disc pathology.",
      "created_at": "2025-05-26T22:11:36.359925Z",
      "answers": [
        "Mild stress",
        "Recent surgery or herniated discs",
        "Fatigue",
        "Mild headache"
      ],
      "clinical_application": "Always screen for spinal issues, surgeries, and pregnancy before treatment.",
      "variant": 7
    },

    {
      "id": "Thai Massage 009",
      "correct": 2,
      "question": "How long is a traditional Thai massage session?",
      "short_explanation": "Traditional sessions are longer than Western styles.",
      "synonyms": [],
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "category": "Modalities",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T22:11:36.359925Z",
      "answers": [
        "10–15 minutes",
        "30 minutes",
        "60–120 minutes",
        "All day"
      ],
      "variant": 9,
      "long_explanation": "Longer sessions allow thorough work along all Sen lines and gradual integration of joint movement.",

    {
      "id": "Trager Method Intro 001",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "answers": [
        "Dr. Ida Rolf",
        "Milton Trager",
        "John Upledger",
        "William Fitzgerald"
      ],
      "synonyms": [],
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "tags": [
        "modality_trager",
        "modality_rolfing",
        "founder_ida_rolf"
      ],
      "short_explanation": "Milton Trager developed the Trager Method.",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "clinical_application": "Introduces students to the founder and his intent to blend physical and neurological healing.",
      "topic": "modalities",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/modalities/trager_method_intro",
      "correct": 1,
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "question": "Who developed the Trager Method?",
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.145Z",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T22:12:39.645901Z",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "long_explanation": "Milton Trager, a medical doctor and boxer, created the method to promote effortless movement through gentle neuro-muscular reeducation.",
      "distractors": [],
      "keywords": [
        "modality_trager",
        "modality_rolfing",
        "founder_ida_rolf"
      ],
      "source_reference": "Trager International – Historical Overview",
      "category": "Modalities",
      "variant": 1
    },
    {
      "id": "Trager Method Intro 002",
      "topic": "modalities",
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "correct": 2,
      "keywords": [
        "modality_deep_tissue",
        "modality_trager",
        "anatomy_fascia",
        "anatomy_tissues",
        "anatomy_muscles",
        "anatomy_nervous_system",
        "anatomy_meridians"
      ],
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "source_reference": "Neurophysiological Effects of the Trager Method – Manual Therapy Journal",
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T22:12:39.645901Z",
      "variant": 2,
      "category": "Modalities",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/modalities/trager_method_intro",
      "short_explanation": "To re-educate the nervous system and promote effortless movement.",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "tags": [
        "modality_deep_tissue",
        "modality_trager",
        "anatomy_fascia",
        "anatomy_tissues",
        "anatomy_muscles",
        "anatomy_nervous_system",
        "anatomy_meridians"
      ],
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "distractors": [],
      "question": "What is the primary goal of the Trager Method?",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.145Z",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "answers": [
        "To release fascia and align posture",
        "To correct energy imbalances through meridians",
        "To re-educate the nervous system and promote effortless movement",
        "To stimulate deep tissue for muscle growth"
      ],
      "synonyms": [],
      "clinical_application": "Explains how the method targets chronic tension through nervous system retraining.",
      "long_explanation": "The method is based on the belief that gentle, mindful input can retrain the body and brain to move freely and without pain."
    },
    {
      "id": "Trager Method Intro 003",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/modalities/trager_method_intro",
      "category": "Modalities",
      "topic": "modalities",
      "variant": 3,
      "answers": [
        "Oscillations",
        "Effleurage",
        "Mentastics",
        "Palpations"
      ],
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.145Z",
      "synonyms": [],
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "distractors": [],
      "correct": 2,
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "short_explanation": "Mentastics are gentle, rhythmic movements used in Trager Therapy.",
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "long_explanation": "They are designed to introduce lightness, ease, and awareness to the body through mindful movement.",

      "clinical_application": "Clients may be assigned mentastics for home care between sessions."

      "question": "What is Tui Na?",

      "long_explanation": "Tui Na combines Traditional Chinese Medicine principles with massage techniques to move qi and blood in the body.",

        "A Chinese hands-on body treatment using acupressure and rhythmic movements",
        "A reflexology style from Thailand",
        "A Japanese energy healing method"
      ],
      "clinical_application": "Ideal for clients seeking non-invasive therapy for musculoskeletal tension or internal disorders.",
      "variant": 1
    },
    {
      "id": "Tui Na 002",
      "clinical_application": "Used as part of a holistic protocol to balance internal health and energy flow.",

    {
      "id": "Tui Na 003",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "clinical_application": "Effective in chronic pain, joint stiffness, and sports recovery.",

      "topic": "modalities",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "long_explanation": "These manipulations influence muscles, joints, and meridian pathways to treat pain and promote circulation.",

      "long_explanation": "Tui is the first half of the name and reflects the directional movement used to affect energy flow.",

      "distractors": [],
      "clinical_application": "Massage therapists must assess for acute trauma or post-surgical recovery.",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/modalities/tui_na",
      "long_explanation": "While it’s generally safe, applying pressure to unstable or broken tissues can worsen injury.",
      "question": "Which of the following is a contraindication for Tui Na?",
      "short_explanation": "Tui Na should be avoided in areas with fractures or open wounds.",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.145Z",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "source_reference": "Massage Contraindications – Clinical Guide",
      "difficulty_rating": 2,

      "category": "Modalities",
      "synonyms": [],

    {
      "id": "Tui Na 009",

      ],
      "question": "Tui Na aims to balance the flow of which vital energy?",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.145Z",
      "variant": 9,
      "topic": "modalities",
      "clinical_application": "Supports stress relief, immune function, and organ balance.",
      "source_reference": "Energetics of Chinese Medicine – Qi Flow Manual",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "long_explanation": "By unblocking meridians and restoring qi flow, Tui Na enhances physical and emotional vitality.",
      "answers": [
        "Qi (Chi)",
        "Ojas",
        "Aura",
        "Yin"
      ],
      "last_answered_at": null,

      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/modalities/tui_na",

      "distractors": [],
      "short_explanation": "Tui Na is performed with the client clothed on a table or chair.",
      "variant": 10,

      "answers": [
        "Standing and dancing",

        "Strapped into resistance bands"
      ],
      "question": "Tui Na is typically performed with the client:",
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "clinical_application": "Makes it ideal for clinical or multi-client settings.",
      "topic": "modalities",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.145Z",
      "long_explanation": "It does not require oil or disrobing and uses focused manipulation over clothing or cloth sheets.",

      "question_type": "multiple choice",

    {
      "id": "Contraindications 001",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "topic": "pathology",
      "answers": [
        "Mild muscle soreness",
        "Deep vein thrombosis (DVT)",
        "Dry skin",
        "Joint stiffness"
      ],
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "variant": 1,
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "question": "Which condition requires immediate avoidance of massage and physician referral?",
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "source_reference": "Medical Massage Guidelines – Clinical Safety Manual",
      "distractors": [],
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.146Z",
      "long_explanation": "Massaging a limb with a known or suspected DVT may dislodge the clot, leading to life-threatening complications such as pulmonary embolism.",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "synonyms": [],
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/contraindications",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T22:16:00.713584Z",
      "clinical_application": "Massage therapists must immediately refer and avoid any pressure near the site of a suspected DVT.",
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "anatomy_joint",
        "anatomy_muscles",
        "condition_pain",
        "condition_soreness",
        "joint_joint",
        "organ_skin"
      ],
      "short_explanation": "Deep vein thrombosis (DVT) is a critical contraindication due to embolism risk.",
      "correct": 1,
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "anatomy_joint",
        "anatomy_muscles",
        "condition_pain",
        "condition_soreness",
        "joint_joint",
        "organ_skin"
      ],
      "category": "Pathology"
    },
    {
      "id": "Contraindications 002",
      "synonyms": [],
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "short_explanation": "Acute fever signals an underlying systemic infection or immune response.",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.146Z",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T22:16:00.713584Z",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "distractors": [],
      "question": "Which is considered a total contraindication for massage?",
      "correct": 0,
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "anatomy_tissues",
        "condition_pain",
        "purpose_stress",
        "contraindication",
        "concept_anxiety"
      ],
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "answers": [
        "Acute fever",
        "Old scar tissue",
        "Chronic back pain",
        "Mild anxiety"
      ],
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "category": "Pathology",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "clinical_application": "Therapists must postpone treatment until fever fully resolves.",
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "anatomy_tissues",
        "condition_pain",
        "purpose_stress",
        "contraindication",
        "concept_anxiety"
      ],
      "topic": "pathology",
      "long_explanation": "Fever indicates that the body is actively fighting an illness, and massage could exacerbate symptoms or spread infection.",
      "source_reference": "Massage Therapy Pathology – Red Flags and Safety",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/contraindications",
      "variant": 2
    },
    {
      "id": "Contraindications 003",
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "long_explanation": "Applying pressure over a hematoma can worsen bleeding, inflammation, and tissue damage.",
      "correct": 2,
      "source_reference": "Tissue Injury and Contraindications – Clinical Reference Guide",
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "topic": "pathology",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.146Z",
      "clinical_application": "Therapists should visually inspect for bruising or swelling and avoid the area.",
      "short_explanation": "Hematoma is a localized collection of blood and should not be massaged.",
      "synonyms": [],
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "anatomy_tissues"
      ],
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "anatomy_tissues"
      ],
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T22:16:00.713584Z",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/contraindications",
      "category": "Pathology",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "question": "Massage should be avoided over which of the following conditions?",
      "distractors": [],
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "answers": [
        "Scar tissue",
        "Bruising",
        "Hematoma",
        "Stretch marks"
      ],
      "variant": 3
    },
    {
      "id": "Contraindications 004",
      "short_explanation": "Massage during the first trimester without clearance is risky due to miscarriage concerns.",
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "correct": 0,
      "relevance_score": 5,

        "modality_oils",
        "joint_joint"
      ],
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "long_explanation": "Many practitioners avoid first-trimester massage unless cleared, as uterine stimulation may pose a theoretical risk.",
      "distractors": [],
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "variant": 4,
      "keywords": [

      ],
      "answers": [

        "Allergies to oils",
        "Joint damage",
        "It affects digestion"
      ],
      "category": "Pathology",
      "source_reference": "Prenatal Massage Safety Standards – Women's Health Manual",
      "question": "Why is massage contraindicated in the first trimester of pregnancy without clearance?",
      "synonyms": [],
      "difficulty_rating": 2,

      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T22:16:00.713584Z",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "clinical_application": "Practitioners should always get written clearance before proceeding.",
      "topic": "pathology"
    },
    {
      "id": "Contraindications 005",

      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/contraindications",

      "short_explanation": "Medical clearance is essential when massaging cancer clients.",
      "long_explanation": "Massage may increase circulation, which can interfere with treatment protocols or metastasis if not coordinated with healthcare providers.",

      "category": "Pathology",
      "clinical_application": "Therapists should work within scope and integrate with oncology teams.",

        "Medical clearance",

        "Essential oils",
        "Vigorous friction"
      ],

      "short_explanation": "A local contraindication limits massage only in a specific area.",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "long_explanation": "Conditions like bruises, skin infections, or minor injuries may require you to avoid just the affected region.",

      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "answers": [

      ],

      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.146Z"
    },
    {
      "id": "Contraindications 008",
      "long_explanation": "Massage may be performed if the client is stable, medicated, and has no acute symptoms.",

      ],
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "question": "Which of the following is an example of a conditional contraindication?",
      "short_explanation": "Controlled high blood pressure is a conditional contraindication.",

      "answers": [

        "High blood pressure under control",
        "Deep vein thrombosis"

      "stats": {

        "incorrect": 0,

      },

    {

    {
      "id": "Contraindications 001",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "tags": [
        "condition_scoliosis",
        "contraindication",
        "client",
        "modality_massage_therapy"
      ],
      "keywords": [
        "condition_scoliosis",
        "contraindication",
        "client",
        "modality_massage_therapy"
      ],
      "answers": [
        "Chronic sinusitis",
        "Uncontrolled hypertension",
        "Deep vein thrombosis (DVT)",
        "Mild scoliosis"
      ],
      "subcategory": "Absolute Contraindications",
      "clinical_application": "Always screen for unexplained leg swelling, tenderness, and warmth in clients — these are DVT red flags.",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.147Z",
      "topic": "pathology",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/contraindications/contraindications_full",
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.489Z",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Candidate Handbook; AMTA Safety Guidelines",
      "long_explanation": "Deep vein thrombosis is a medical emergency. Massage can dislodge a clot, potentially leading to pulmonary embolism, stroke, or cardiac arrest. Medical clearance is required before resuming massage.",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "short_explanation": "Massage is contraindicated with active DVT due to clot migration risk.",
      "question": "Which of the following conditions is an absolute contraindication to massage therapy?",
      "correct": 2,
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "synonyms": [
        "DVT",
        "thrombosis",
        "clot"
      ],
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "category": "Pathology & Contraindications",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "distractors": []
    },
    {
      "id": "Contraindications 002",
      "category": "Pathology & Contraindications",
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "technique_pressure",
        "contraindication",
        "concept_localized"
      ],
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/contraindications/contraindications_full",
      "relevance_score": 4,
      "clinical_application": "Visually inspect for enlarged or twisted superficial veins and avoid direct pressure.",
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "technique_pressure",
        "contraindication",
        "concept_localized"
      ],
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.147Z",
      "synonyms": [
        "varicosities",
        "twisted veins"
      ],
      "answers": [
        "Systemic infection",
        "Varicose veins",
        "HIV-positive status",
        "Mild fatigue"
      ],
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "subcategory": "Local Contraindications",
      "short_explanation": "Massage is avoided over varicose veins but allowed elsewhere.",
      "question": "Which of the following is considered a local contraindication for massage?",
      "topic": "pathology",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "source_reference": "Trail Guide to the Body; AMTA Position Statements",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.489Z",
      "correct": 1,
      "long_explanation": "Varicose veins are fragile and pressure can damage the valve system or worsen pooling. Gentle work may be allowed with medical clearance, but deeper strokes are avoided over the area.",
      "distractors": [],
      "question_type": "multiple choice"
    },
    {
      "id": "Contraindications 003",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "question": "Massage should be avoided entirely if a client presents with:",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.489Z",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "purpose_stress",
        "client_presents",
        "concept_anxiety"
      ],
      "clinical_application": "Ensure client screening includes illness questions to protect both client and therapist.",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "difficulty": "easy",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.147Z",
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "answers": [
        "Seasonal allergies",
        "Cold and clammy hands",
        "Systemic contagious infection",
        "History of anxiety"
      ],
      "topic": "pathology",
      "short_explanation": "Contagious infections require complete postponement of massage.",
      "correct": 2,
      "category": "Pathology & Contraindications",
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "purpose_stress",
        "client_presents",
        "concept_anxiety"
      ],
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "subcategory": "Infectious Conditions",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "synonyms": [
        "systemic infection",
        "contagious illness"
      ],
      "distractors": [],
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/contraindications/contraindications_full",
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "long_explanation": "When a client has a fever, flu, strep throat, or other systemic infection, massage can worsen symptoms and pose risk of transmission. Reschedule when client is symptom-free for 24+ hours.",
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Outline; AMTA Clinical Guidelines"
    },
    {
      "id": "Contraindications 004",
      "category": "Pathology & Contraindications",
      "topic": "pathology",
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "distractors": [],
      "subcategory": "Medical Conditions Requiring Clearance",
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "clinical_application": "Always check for surgical dates, scar healing status, and medical advice before proceeding.",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.147Z",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Clinical Guidelines; AMTA Surgery Protocols",
      "long_explanation": "Recent surgeries involve fragile tissues, inflammation, risk of dehiscence, and potential blood clots. A physician's note ensures safety and timing for bodywork.",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "synonyms": [
        "surgical site",
        "recent operation"
      ],
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.489Z",
      "answers": [
        "Pregnancy in the first trimester",
        "Recent surgery with staples",
        "Chronic anxiety",
        "Seasonal affective disorder"
      ],
      "relevance_score": 4,
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "question": "Which condition requires medical clearance before massage?",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/contraindications/contraindications_full",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "short_explanation": "Massage near surgical sites should not proceed without physician approval.",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "anatomy_tissues",
        "purpose_stress",
        "purpose_healing",
        "concept_anxiety"
      ],
      "tags": [

        "concept_anxiety"
      ],
      "correct": 1
    },
    {
      "id": "Contraindications 005",
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "long_explanation": "Athlete's foot is contagious and can spread to hands or other areas. Local massage should be avoided, and therapist hygiene precautions strictly followed.",
      "distractors": [],
      "subcategory": "Skin Conditions",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "answers": [
        "Cellulitis",
        "Fungal infection (athlete's foot)",
        "Eczema in remission",
        "General dryness"
      ],
      "relevance_score": 4,
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.147Z",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "clinical_application": "If the infection is localized to the feet, work can be done on other body areas safely.",
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "contraindication",
        "concept_localized",
        "population_athlete",
        "organ_skin"
      ],

      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.489Z",
      "short_explanation": "Avoid massage directly on fungal infections to prevent spread.",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/contraindications/contraindications_full",
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,

      },

      "synonyms": [

      ],
      "correct": 1,
      "question": "Which of the following skin conditions is a local contraindication?",
      "topic": "pathology",
      "category": "Pathology & Contraindications"
    },
    {
      "id": "Contraindications 006",
      "answers": [
        "Hypertension managed with medication",
        "Congestive heart failure (CHF) with edema",
        "Mild arrhythmia",
        "Recent myocardial infarction"
      ],
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Pathology Guidelines",
      "category": "Pathology & Contraindications",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.489Z",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "keywords": [
        "contraindication",
        "client",

      ],
      "subcategory": "Absolute Contraindications",
      "question": "Which of the following cardiovascular conditions is an absolute contraindication for massage therapy?",
      "tags": [
        "contraindication",
        "client",
        "modality_massage_therapy",
        "organ_heart"
      ],
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "difficulty": "hard",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/contraindications/contraindications_full",
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.147Z",
      "correct": 3,
      "synonyms": [
        "heart attack",
        "MI"
      ],
      "clinical_application": "Always ask about recent cardiac events or hospitalizations before working with cardiac clients.",
      "long_explanation": "A myocardial infarction (heart attack) indicates cardiac instability. Massage should not be performed until the client has received medical clearance and is no longer in an acute phase.",
      "topic": "pathology",
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "distractors": [],
      "short_explanation": "Massage is contraindicated after a recent heart attack.",
      "difficulty_rating": 4,
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "last_answered_at": null
    },
    {
      "id": "Contraindications 007",
      "correct": 1,
      "topic": "pathology",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/contraindications/contraindications_full",
      "synonyms": [
        "bone thinning",
        "low bone density"
      ],
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.489Z",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.147Z",
      "distractors": [],
      "tags": [
        "technique_compression",
        "anatomy_joint",
        "technique_pressure",
        "anatomy_bones",
        "purpose_benefit",
        "contraindication",
        "client",
        "modality_massage_plan",
        "joint_joint",
        "plan_plan"
      ],
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "answers": [
        "Chemotherapy within 24 hours",
        "Client has osteoporosis",
        "Open wound on the arm",
        "Severe cellulitis"
      ],
      "keywords": [
        "technique_compression",
        "anatomy_joint",
        "technique_pressure",
        "anatomy_bones",
        "purpose_benefit",
        "contraindication",
        "client",
        "modality_massage_plan",
        "joint_joint",
        "plan_plan"
      ],
      "long_explanation": "Clients with osteoporosis have fragile bones. Light pressure, support positioning, and avoiding joint compression allow massage to be performed safely with modification.",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "relevance_score": 4,
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "subcategory": "Special Populations",
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Special Populations Guide",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "question": "Which situation requires a modified massage plan but is not a contraindication?",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "clinical_application": "Use bolsters, avoid deep techniques, and educate elderly clients on benefits of gentle touch.",
      "category": "Pathology & Contraindications",
      "short_explanation": "Osteoporosis requires pressure adjustments but not avoidance of massage."
    },
    {
      "id": "Contraindications 008",
      "short_explanation": "Warts are contagious; avoid the area but massage can be performed elsewhere.",
      "distractors": [],
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "answers": [
        "Warts on the feet",
        "Psoriasis covering 90% of the body",
        "Widespread sunburn",
        "General eczema flare-up"
      ],
      "clinical_application": "Apply universal precautions and offer a hand or seated massage as an alternative.",
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "category": "Pathology & Contraindications",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.489Z",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.147Z",
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "subcategory": "Skin and Dermatologic Issues",
      "topic": "pathology",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/contraindications/contraindications_full",
      "question": "Which of the following skin conditions is a localized contraindication?",
      "synonyms": [
        "verruca",
        "foot wart"
      ],
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Dermatology Standards",
      "relevance_score": 4,
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "technique_pressure",
        "concept_localized",
        "condition_localized_contraindication",
        "organ_skin"
      ],
      "difficulty": "easy",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "technique_pressure",
        "concept_localized",
        "condition_localized_contraindication",
        "organ_skin"
      ],
      "correct": 0,
      "long_explanation": "Warts are caused by HPV and can spread through skin contact or contamination. Avoid direct pressure on affected sites, especially plantar warts on the feet."
    },
    {
      "id": "Contraindications 009",
      "synonyms": [
        "blood thinning drugs",
        "bleeding risk"
      ],
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "answers": [
        "Use deep pressure to improve circulation",
        "Avoid massage altogether",
        "Apply moderate pressure but only with medical clearance",
        "Use light pressure and avoid bruising"
      ],
      "clinical_application": "Watch for signs of easy bruising, hematomas, or capillary fragility in elderly clients or those on long-term medication.",
      "question": "What caution should be taken when working with clients on blood thinners?",
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "distractors": [],
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.489Z",
      "topic": "pathology",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "long_explanation": "Medications like warfarin or apixaban reduce clotting ability. Even moderate pressure can cause bruising or internal bleeding. Always use light techniques unless cleared by a physician.",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "technique_pressure",
        "client",
        "concept_caution",
        "concept_thinners",
        "concept_blood_thinners"
      ],
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/contraindications/contraindications_full",
      "category": "Pathology & Contraindications",
      "source_reference": "AMTA Medication Guidelines",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "correct": 3,
      "subcategory": "Medication and Medical Devices",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "technique_pressure",
        "client",
        "concept_caution",
        "concept_thinners",
        "concept_blood_thinners"
      ],
      "short_explanation": "Clients on anticoagulants bruise easily; use caution.",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.147Z"
    },
    {
      "id": "Contraindications 010",
      "long_explanation": "This early phase (24–72 hours post-injury) involves redness, swelling, and pain. Massage can increase inflammation and disrupt natural healing if applied too soon.",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "technique_compression",
        "anatomy_muscles",
        "condition_pain",
        "purpose_relaxation",
        "purpose_healing",
        "contraindication",
        "condition_acute_injury",
        "condition_injury",
        "concept_recovery"
      ],
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "subcategory": "Acute Conditions",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "topic": "pathology",
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/contraindications/contraindications_full",
      "synonyms": [
        "acute phase",
        "initial injury"
      ],
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Inflammation & Recovery Module",
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "technique_compression",
        "anatomy_muscles",
        "condition_pain",
        "purpose_relaxation",
        "purpose_healing",
        "contraindication",
        "condition_acute_injury",
        "condition_injury",
        "concept_recovery"
      ],
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "question": "Massage is generally contraindicated during which stage of acute injury?",
      "short_explanation": "Massage is avoided during the inflammatory stage of injury.",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "relevance_score": 4,
      "answers": [
        "Proliferation phase",
        "Remodeling phase",
        "Inflammatory phase",
        "Recovery phase"
      ],
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.147Z",
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "clinical_application": "Use RICE (rest, ice, compression, elevation) during inflammation. Massage is introduced later for scar remodeling or muscle relaxation.",
      "correct": 2,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.489Z",
      "category": "Pathology & Contraindications",
      "distractors": []
    },
    {
      "id": "Contraindications 011",
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "question": "Which of the following conditions is an absolute contraindication to massage therapy?",
      "long_explanation": "Deep vein thrombosis is a medical emergency. Massage can dislodge a clot, potentially leading to pulmonary embolism, stroke, or cardiac arrest. Medical clearance is required before resuming massage.",
      "distractors": [],
      "tags": [
        "condition_scoliosis",
        "contraindication",
        "client",
        "modality_massage_therapy"
      ],
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "category": "Pathology & Contraindications",
      "correct": 2,
      "subcategory": "Absolute Contraindications",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "topic": "pathology",
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Candidate Handbook; AMTA Safety Guidelines",
      "short_explanation": "Massage is contraindicated with active DVT due to clot migration risk.",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.489Z",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/contraindications/contraindications_full",
      "keywords": [
        "condition_scoliosis",
        "contraindication",
        "client",
        "modality_massage_therapy"
      ],
      "clinical_application": "Always screen for unexplained leg swelling, tenderness, and warmth in clients — these are DVT red flags.",
      "answers": [
        "Chronic sinusitis",
        "Uncontrolled hypertension",
        "Deep vein thrombosis (DVT)",
        "Mild scoliosis"
      ],
      "synonyms": [
        "DVT",
        "thrombosis",
        "clot"
      ],
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.147Z"
    },
    {
      "id": "Contraindications 012",
      "answers": [
        "Systemic infection",
        "Varicose veins",
        "HIV-positive status",
        "Mild fatigue"
      ],
      "long_explanation": "Varicose veins are fragile and pressure can damage the valve system or worsen pooling. Gentle work may be allowed with medical clearance, but deeper strokes are avoided over the area.",
      "correct": 1,
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "technique_pressure",
        "contraindication",
        "concept_localized"
      ],
      "subcategory": "Local Contraindications",
      "relevance_score": 4,
      "clinical_application": "Visually inspect for enlarged or twisted superficial veins and avoid direct pressure.",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "category": "Pathology & Contraindications",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.489Z",
      "topic": "pathology",
      "short_explanation": "Massage is avoided over varicose veins but allowed elsewhere.",
      "source_reference": "Trail Guide to the Body; AMTA Position Statements",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/contraindications/contraindications_full",
      "question": "Which of the following is considered a local contraindication for massage?",
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.147Z",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "technique_pressure",
        "contraindication",
        "concept_localized"
      ],
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "distractors": [],
      "synonyms": [
        "varicosities",
        "twisted veins"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "Contraindications 013",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.147Z",
      "distractors": [],
      "category": "Pathology & Contraindications",
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Outline; AMTA Clinical Guidelines",
      "answers": [
        "Seasonal allergies",
        "Cold and clammy hands",
        "Systemic contagious infection",
        "History of anxiety"
      ],
      "long_explanation": "When a client has a fever, flu, strep throat, or other systemic infection, massage can worsen symptoms and pose risk of transmission. Reschedule when client is symptom-free for 24+ hours.",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.489Z",
      "correct": 2,
      "topic": "pathology",
      "clinical_application": "Ensure client screening includes illness questions to protect both client and therapist.",
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "difficulty": "easy",
      "short_explanation": "Contagious infections require complete postponement of massage.",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "question": "Massage should be avoided entirely if a client presents with:",
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "purpose_stress",
        "client_presents",
        "concept_anxiety"
      ],
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/contraindications/contraindications_full",
      "synonyms": [
        "systemic infection",
        "contagious illness"
      ],
      "subcategory": "Infectious Conditions",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "purpose_stress",
        "client_presents",
        "concept_anxiety"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "Contraindications 014",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.147Z",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.489Z",
      "answers": [
        "Pregnancy in the first trimester",
        "Recent surgery with staples",
        "Chronic anxiety",
        "Seasonal affective disorder"
      ],
      "distractors": [],
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Clinical Guidelines; AMTA Surgery Protocols",
      "short_explanation": "Massage near surgical sites should not proceed without physician approval.",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/contraindications/contraindications_full",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "anatomy_tissues",
        "purpose_stress",
        "purpose_healing",
        "concept_anxiety"
      ],
      "long_explanation": "Recent surgeries involve fragile tissues, inflammation, risk of dehiscence, and potential blood clots. A physician's note ensures safety and timing for bodywork.",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "category": "Pathology & Contraindications",
      "correct": 1,
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "anatomy_tissues",
        "purpose_stress",
        "purpose_healing",
        "concept_anxiety"
      ],
      "subcategory": "Medical Conditions Requiring Clearance",
      "topic": "pathology",
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "question": "Which condition requires medical clearance before massage?",
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "synonyms": [
        "surgical site",
        "recent operation"
      ],
      "clinical_application": "Always check for surgical dates, scar healing status, and medical advice before proceeding.",
      "relevance_score": 4
    },

      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.147Z"
    },
    {
      "id": "Contraindications 018",
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Dermatology Standards",
      "topic": "pathology",
      "long_explanation": "Warts are caused by HPV and can spread through skin contact or contamination. Avoid direct pressure on affected sites, especially plantar warts on the feet.",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "technique_pressure",
        "concept_localized",
        "condition_localized_contraindication",
        "organ_skin"
      ],
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "technique_pressure",
        "concept_localized",
        "condition_localized_contraindication",
        "organ_skin"
      ],
      "subcategory": "Skin and Dermatologic Issues",
      "distractors": [],
      "relevance_score": 4,
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "clinical_application": "Apply universal precautions and offer a hand or seated massage as an alternative.",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.147Z",
      "difficulty": "easy",
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "category": "Pathology & Contraindications",
      "question": "Which of the following skin conditions is a localized contraindication?",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.489Z",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/contraindications/contraindications_full",
      "answers": [
        "Warts on the feet",
        "Psoriasis covering 90% of the body",
        "Widespread sunburn",
        "General eczema flare-up"
      ],
      "short_explanation": "Warts are contagious; avoid the area but massage can be performed elsewhere.",
      "correct": 0,
      "synonyms": [
        "verruca",
        "foot wart"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "Contraindications 019",
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "subcategory": "Medication and Medical Devices",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "question": "What caution should be taken when working with clients on blood thinners?",
      "answers": [
        "Use deep pressure to improve circulation",
        "Avoid massage altogether",
        "Apply moderate pressure but only with medical clearance",
        "Use light pressure and avoid bruising"
      ],
      "source_reference": "AMTA Medication Guidelines",
      "clinical_application": "Watch for signs of easy bruising, hematomas, or capillary fragility in elderly clients or those on long-term medication.",
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "technique_pressure",
        "client",
        "concept_caution",
        "concept_thinners",
        "concept_blood_thinners"
      ],
      "category": "Pathology & Contraindications",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "technique_pressure",
        "client",
        "concept_caution",
        "concept_thinners",
        "concept_blood_thinners"
      ],
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/contraindications/contraindications_full",
      "long_explanation": "Medications like warfarin or apixaban reduce clotting ability. Even moderate pressure can cause bruising or internal bleeding. Always use light techniques unless cleared by a physician.",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.147Z",
      "topic": "pathology",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "short_explanation": "Clients on anticoagulants bruise easily; use caution.",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "correct": 3,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.489Z",
      "distractors": [],
      "synonyms": [
        "blood thinning drugs",
        "bleeding risk"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "Contraindications 020",
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "technique_compression",
        "anatomy_muscles",
        "condition_pain",
        "purpose_relaxation",
        "purpose_healing",
        "contraindication",
        "condition_acute_injury",
        "condition_injury",
        "concept_recovery"
      ],
      "short_explanation": "Massage is avoided during the inflammatory stage of injury.",
      "question": "Massage is generally contraindicated during which stage of acute injury?",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.147Z",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/contraindications/contraindications_full",
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "answers": [
        "Proliferation phase",
        "Remodeling phase",
        "Inflammatory phase",
        "Recovery phase"
      ],
      "synonyms": [
        "acute phase",
        "initial injury"
      ],
      "distractors": [],
      "subcategory": "Acute Conditions",
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Inflammation & Recovery Module",
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "technique_compression",
        "anatomy_muscles",
        "condition_pain",
        "purpose_relaxation",
        "purpose_healing",
        "contraindication",
        "condition_acute_injury",
        "condition_injury",
        "concept_recovery"
      ],
      "correct": 2,
      "clinical_application": "Use RICE (rest, ice, compression, elevation) during inflammation. Massage is introduced later for scar remodeling or muscle relaxation.",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "topic": "pathology",
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.489Z",
      "category": "Pathology & Contraindications",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "relevance_score": 4,
      "long_explanation": "This early phase (24–72 hours post-injury) involves redness, swelling, and pain. Massage can increase inflammation and disrupt natural healing if applied too soon."
    },
    {
      "id": "Contraindications 021",
      "short_explanation": "Massage is contraindicated with active DVT due to clot migration risk.",
      "category": "Pathology & Contraindications",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "correct": 2,
      "distractors": [],
      "answers": [
        "Chronic sinusitis",
        "Uncontrolled hypertension",
        "Deep vein thrombosis (DVT)",
        "Mild scoliosis"
      ],
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/contraindications/contraindications_full",
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "clinical_application": "Always screen for unexplained leg swelling, tenderness, and warmth in clients — these are DVT red flags.",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.147Z",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.489Z",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "long_explanation": "Deep vein thrombosis is a medical emergency. Massage can dislodge a clot, potentially leading to pulmonary embolism, stroke, or cardiac arrest. Medical clearance is required before resuming massage.",
      "question": "Which of the following conditions is an absolute contraindication to massage therapy?",
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "tags": [
        "condition_scoliosis",
        "contraindication",
        "client",
        "modality_massage_therapy"
      ],
      "subcategory": "Absolute Contraindications",
      "keywords": [
        "condition_scoliosis",
        "contraindication",
        "client",
        "modality_massage_therapy"
      ],
      "topic": "pathology",
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Candidate Handbook; AMTA Safety Guidelines",
      "synonyms": [
        "DVT",
        "thrombosis",
        "clot"
      ],
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "Contraindications 022",
      "correct": 1,
      "long_explanation": "Varicose veins are fragile and pressure can damage the valve system or worsen pooling. Gentle work may be allowed with medical clearance, but deeper strokes are avoided over the area.",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "distractors": [],
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.147Z",
      "source_reference": "Trail Guide to the Body; AMTA Position Statements",
      "category": "Pathology & Contraindications",
      "question": "Which of the following is considered a local contraindication for massage?",
      "clinical_application": "Visually inspect for enlarged or twisted superficial veins and avoid direct pressure.",
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "subcategory": "Local Contraindications",
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "topic": "pathology",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "answers": [
        "Systemic infection",
        "Varicose veins",
        "HIV-positive status",
        "Mild fatigue"
      ],
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "short_explanation": "Massage is avoided over varicose veins but allowed elsewhere.",
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "technique_pressure",
        "contraindication",
        "concept_localized"
      ],
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/contraindications/contraindications_full",
      "relevance_score": 4,
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "technique_pressure",
        "contraindication",
        "concept_localized"
      ],
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.489Z",
      "synonyms": [
        "varicosities",
        "twisted veins"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "Contraindications 023",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/contraindications/contraindications_full",
      "long_explanation": "When a client has a fever, flu, strep throat, or other systemic infection, massage can worsen symptoms and pose risk of transmission. Reschedule when client is symptom-free for 24+ hours.",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "distractors": [],
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.489Z",
      "correct": 2,
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Outline; AMTA Clinical Guidelines",
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "clinical_application": "Ensure client screening includes illness questions to protect both client and therapist.",
      "category": "Pathology & Contraindications",
      "synonyms": [
        "systemic infection",
        "contagious illness"
      ],
      "subcategory": "Infectious Conditions",
      "answers": [
        "Seasonal allergies",
        "Cold and clammy hands",
        "Systemic contagious infection",
        "History of anxiety"
      ],
      "topic": "pathology",
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "question": "Massage should be avoided entirely if a client presents with:",
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "purpose_stress",
        "client_presents",
        "concept_anxiety"
      ],
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "difficulty": "easy",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "purpose_stress",
        "client_presents",
        "concept_anxiety"
      ],
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "short_explanation": "Contagious infections require complete postponement of massage.",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.147Z"
    },
    {
      "id": "Contraindications 024",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.489Z",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "synonyms": [
        "surgical site",
        "recent operation"
      ],
      "relevance_score": 4,
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/contraindications/contraindications_full",
      "distractors": [],
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "anatomy_tissues",
        "purpose_stress",
        "purpose_healing",
        "concept_anxiety"
      ],
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "long_explanation": "Recent surgeries involve fragile tissues, inflammation, risk of dehiscence, and potential blood clots. A physician's note ensures safety and timing for bodywork.",
      "clinical_application": "Always check for surgical dates, scar healing status, and medical advice before proceeding.",
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "question": "Which condition requires medical clearance before massage?",
      "subcategory": "Medical Conditions Requiring Clearance",
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Clinical Guidelines; AMTA Surgery Protocols",
      "category": "Pathology & Contraindications",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "anatomy_tissues",
        "purpose_stress",
        "purpose_healing",
        "concept_anxiety"
      ],
      "correct": 1,
      "short_explanation": "Massage near surgical sites should not proceed without physician approval.",
      "answers": [
        "Pregnancy in the first trimester",
        "Recent surgery with staples",
        "Chronic anxiety",
        "Seasonal affective disorder"
      ],
      "topic": "pathology",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.147Z"
    },
    {
      "id": "Contraindications 025",
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "subcategory": "Skin Conditions",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "question": "Which of the following skin conditions is a local contraindication?",
      "answers": [
        "Cellulitis",
        "Fungal infection (athlete's foot)",
        "Eczema in remission",
        "General dryness"
      ],
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Outline; CDC Guidelines",
      "clinical_application": "If the infection is localized to the feet, work can be done on other body areas safely.",
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "contraindication",
        "concept_localized",
        "population_athlete",
        "organ_skin"
      ],
      "category": "Pathology & Contraindications",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "contraindication",
        "concept_localized",
        "population_athlete",
        "organ_skin"
      ],
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/contraindications/contraindications_full",
      "long_explanation": "Athlete's foot is contagious and can spread to hands or other areas. Local massage should be avoided, and therapist hygiene precautions strictly followed.",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.147Z",
      "topic": "pathology",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "short_explanation": "Avoid massage directly on fungal infections to prevent spread.",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "relevance_score": 4,
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "correct": 1,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.489Z",
      "distractors": [],
      "synonyms": [
        "foot fungus",
        "tinea"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "Contraindications 026",
      "answers": [
        "Hypertension managed with medication",
        "Congestive heart failure (CHF) with edema",
        "Mild arrhythmia",
        "Recent myocardial infarction"
      ],
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.489Z",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/contraindications/contraindications_full",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "tags": [
        "contraindication",
        "client",
        "modality_massage_therapy",
        "organ_heart"
      ],
      "difficulty": "hard",
      "category": "Pathology & Contraindications",
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "long_explanation": "A myocardial infarction (heart attack) indicates cardiac instability. Massage should not be performed until the client has received medical clearance and is no longer in an acute phase.",
      "subcategory": "Absolute Contraindications",
      "correct": 3,
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "topic": "pathology",
      "difficulty_rating": 4,
      "distractors": [],
      "synonyms": [
        "heart attack",
        "MI"
      ],
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Pathology Guidelines",
      "short_explanation": "Massage is contraindicated after a recent heart attack.",
      "question": "Which of the following cardiovascular conditions is an absolute contraindication for massage therapy?",
      "clinical_application": "Always ask about recent cardiac events or hospitalizations before working with cardiac clients.",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.147Z",
      "keywords": [
        "contraindication",
        "client",
        "modality_massage_therapy",
        "organ_heart"
      ],
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0
    },
    {
      "id": "Contraindications 027",
      "keywords": [
        "technique_compression",
        "anatomy_joint",
        "technique_pressure",
        "anatomy_bones",
        "purpose_benefit",
        "contraindication",
        "client",
        "modality_massage_plan",
        "joint_joint",
        "plan_plan"
      ],
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "relevance_score": 4,
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Special Populations Guide",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/contraindications/contraindications_full",
      "short_explanation": "Osteoporosis requires pressure adjustments but not avoidance of massage.",
      "correct": 1,
      "synonyms": [
        "bone thinning",
        "low bone density"
      ],
      "distractors": [],
      "topic": "pathology",
      "long_explanation": "Clients with osteoporosis have fragile bones. Light pressure, support positioning, and avoiding joint compression allow massage to be performed safely with modification.",
      "category": "Pathology & Contraindications",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.489Z",
      "answers": [
        "Chemotherapy within 24 hours",
        "Client has osteoporosis",
        "Open wound on the arm",
        "Severe cellulitis"
      ],
      "question": "Which situation requires a modified massage plan but is not a contraindication?",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.147Z",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "tags": [
        "technique_compression",
        "anatomy_joint",
        "technique_pressure",
        "anatomy_bones",
        "purpose_benefit",
        "contraindication",
        "client",
        "modality_massage_plan",
        "joint_joint",
        "plan_plan"
      ],
      "clinical_application": "Use bolsters, avoid deep techniques, and educate elderly clients on benefits of gentle touch.",
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "subcategory": "Special Populations"
    },
