        "contraindication",
        "condition_acute_injury",
        "condition_injury",
        "concept_recovery"
      ],
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "question": "Massage is generally contraindicated during which stage of acute injury?",
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "relevance_score": 4,
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Inflammation & Recovery Module",
      "subcategory": "Acute Conditions",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/contraindications/contraindications_full"
    },
    {
      "id": "Contraindications 031",
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Candidate Handbook; AMTA Safety Guidelines",
      "tags": [
        "condition_scoliosis",
        "contraindication",
        "client",
        "modality_massage_therapy"
      ],
      "category": "Pathology & Contraindications",
      "question": "Which of the following conditions is an absolute contraindication to massage therapy?",
      "clinical_application": "Always screen for unexplained leg swelling, tenderness, and warmth in clients — these are DVT red flags.",
      "keywords": [
        "condition_scoliosis",
        "contraindication",
        "client",
        "modality_massage_therapy"
      ],
      "correct": 2,
      "distractors": [],
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "topic": "pathology",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "short_explanation": "Massage is contraindicated with active DVT due to clot migration risk.",
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "long_explanation": "Deep vein thrombosis is a medical emergency. Massage can dislodge a clot, potentially leading to pulmonary embolism, stroke, or cardiac arrest. Medical clearance is required before resuming massage.",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/contraindications/contraindications_full",
      "synonyms": [
        "DVT",
        "thrombosis",
        "clot"
      ],
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "subcategory": "Absolute Contraindications",
      "answers": [
        "Chronic sinusitis",
        "Uncontrolled hypertension",
        "Deep vein thrombosis (DVT)",
        "Mild scoliosis"
      ],
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.489Z",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.147Z"
    },
    {
      "id": "Contraindications 032",
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "subcategory": "Local Contraindications",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "question": "Which of the following is considered a local contraindication for massage?",
      "answers": [
        "Systemic infection",
        "Varicose veins",
        "HIV-positive status",
        "Mild fatigue"
      ],
      "source_reference": "Trail Guide to the Body; AMTA Position Statements",
      "clinical_application": "Visually inspect for enlarged or twisted superficial veins and avoid direct pressure.",
      "category": "Pathology & Contraindications",
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "technique_pressure",
        "contraindication",
        "concept_localized"
      ],
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "technique_pressure",
        "contraindication",
        "concept_localized"
      ],
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/contraindications/contraindications_full",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.147Z",
      "long_explanation": "Varicose veins are fragile and pressure can damage the valve system or worsen pooling. Gentle work may be allowed with medical clearance, but deeper strokes are avoided over the area.",
      "topic": "pathology",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "short_explanation": "Massage is avoided over varicose veins but allowed elsewhere.",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "relevance_score": 4,
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "correct": 1,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.489Z",
      "distractors": [],
      "synonyms": [
        "varicosities",
        "twisted veins"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "Contraindications 033",
      "topic": "pathology",
      "subcategory": "Infectious Conditions",
      "correct": 2,
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "question": "Massage should be avoided entirely if a client presents with:",
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "answers": [
        "Seasonal allergies",
        "Cold and clammy hands",
        "Systemic contagious infection",
        "History of anxiety"
      ],
      "category": "Pathology & Contraindications",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.147Z",
      "distractors": [],
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "purpose_stress",
        "client_presents",
        "concept_anxiety"
      ],
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/contraindications/contraindications_full",
      "long_explanation": "When a client has a fever, flu, strep throat, or other systemic infection, massage can worsen symptoms and pose risk of transmission. Reschedule when client is symptom-free for 24+ hours.",
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Outline; AMTA Clinical Guidelines",
      "difficulty": "easy",
      "short_explanation": "Contagious infections require complete postponement of massage.",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "synonyms": [
        "systemic infection",
        "contagious illness"
      ],
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.489Z",
      "clinical_application": "Ensure client screening includes illness questions to protect both client and therapist.",
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "purpose_stress",
        "client_presents",
        "concept_anxiety"
      ],
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "difficulty_rating": 2
    },
    {
      "id": "Contraindications 034",
      "distractors": [],
      "topic": "pathology",
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "relevance_score": 4,
      "synonyms": [
        "surgical site",
        "recent operation"
      ],
      "correct": 1,
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Clinical Guidelines; AMTA Surgery Protocols",
      "subcategory": "Medical Conditions Requiring Clearance",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/contraindications/contraindications_full",
      "short_explanation": "Massage near surgical sites should not proceed without physician approval.",
      "answers": [
        "Pregnancy in the first trimester",
        "Recent surgery with staples",
        "Chronic anxiety",
        "Seasonal affective disorder"
      ],
      "clinical_application": "Always check for surgical dates, scar healing status, and medical advice before proceeding.",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "anatomy_tissues",
        "purpose_stress",
        "purpose_healing",
        "concept_anxiety"
      ],
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.489Z",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.147Z",
      "category": "Pathology & Contraindications",
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "anatomy_tissues",
        "purpose_stress",
        "purpose_healing",
        "concept_anxiety"
      ],
      "question": "Which condition requires medical clearance before massage?",
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "long_explanation": "Recent surgeries involve fragile tissues, inflammation, risk of dehiscence, and potential blood clots. A physician's note ensures safety and timing for bodywork.",
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "last_answered_at": null
    },
    {
      "id": "Contraindications 035",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.489Z",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/contraindications/contraindications_full",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "contraindication",
        "concept_localized",
        "population_athlete",
        "organ_skin"
      ],
      "relevance_score": 4,
      "category": "Pathology & Contraindications",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "synonyms": [
        "foot fungus",
        "tinea"
      ],
      "short_explanation": "Avoid massage directly on fungal infections to prevent spread.",
      "subcategory": "Skin Conditions",
      "question": "Which of the following skin conditions is a local contraindication?",
      "clinical_application": "If the infection is localized to the feet, work can be done on other body areas safely.",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.147Z",
      "correct": 1,
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "answers": [
        "Cellulitis",
        "Fungal infection (athlete's foot)",
        "Eczema in remission",
        "General dryness"
      ],
      "long_explanation": "Athlete's foot is contagious and can spread to hands or other areas. Local massage should be avoided, and therapist hygiene precautions strictly followed.",
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Outline; CDC Guidelines",
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "topic": "pathology",
      "distractors": [],
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "contraindication",
        "concept_localized",
        "population_athlete",
        "organ_skin"
      ],
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "difficulty_rating": 3
    },
    {
      "id": "Contraindications 036",
      "question": "Which of the following cardiovascular conditions is an absolute contraindication for massage therapy?",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "answers": [
        "Hypertension managed with medication",
        "Congestive heart failure (CHF) with edema",
        "Mild arrhythmia",
        "Recent myocardial infarction"
      ],
      "distractors": [],
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/contraindications/contraindications_full",
      "difficulty_rating": 4,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.489Z",
      "correct": 3,
      "keywords": [
        "contraindication",
        "client",
        "modality_massage_therapy",
        "organ_heart"
      ],
      "topic": "pathology",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "clinical_application": "Always ask about recent cardiac events or hospitalizations before working with cardiac clients.",
      "long_explanation": "A myocardial infarction (heart attack) indicates cardiac instability. Massage should not be performed until the client has received medical clearance and is no longer in an acute phase.",
      "category": "Pathology & Contraindications",
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Pathology Guidelines",
      "short_explanation": "Massage is contraindicated after a recent heart attack.",
      "subcategory": "Absolute Contraindications",
      "tags": [
        "contraindication",
        "client",
        "modality_massage_therapy",
        "organ_heart"
      ],
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "synonyms": [
        "heart attack",
        "MI"
      ],
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.147Z",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "difficulty": "hard",
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations"
    },
    {
      "id": "Contraindications 037",
      "keywords": [
        "technique_compression",
        "anatomy_joint",

        "anatomy_bones",

        "contraindication",

      ],
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.147Z",
      "relevance_score": 4,
      "question": "Which situation requires a modified massage plan but is not a contraindication?",
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Special Populations Guide",
      "synonyms": [
        "bone thinning",
        "low bone density"
      ],
      "distractors": [],
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/contraindications/contraindications_full",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "subcategory": "Special Populations",
      "short_explanation": "Osteoporosis requires pressure adjustments but not avoidance of massage.",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "tags": [
        "technique_compression",
        "anatomy_joint",
        "technique_pressure",
        "anatomy_bones",
        "purpose_benefit",
        "contraindication",
        "client",
        "modality_massage_plan",
        "joint_joint",
        "plan_plan"
      ],
      "topic": "pathology",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "category": "Pathology & Contraindications",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.489Z",
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "correct": 1,
      "clinical_application": "Use bolsters, avoid deep techniques, and educate elderly clients on benefits of gentle touch.",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "answers": [
        "Chemotherapy within 24 hours",
        "Client has osteoporosis",
        "Open wound on the arm",
        "Severe cellulitis"
      ],
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "long_explanation": "Clients with osteoporosis have fragile bones. Light pressure, support positioning, and avoiding joint compression allow massage to be performed safely with modification."
    },
    {
      "id": "Contraindications 038",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "long_explanation": "Warts are caused by HPV and can spread through skin contact or contamination. Avoid direct pressure on affected sites, especially plantar warts on the feet.",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "difficulty": "easy",
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Dermatology Standards",
      "correct": 0,
      "question": "Which of the following skin conditions is a localized contraindication?",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/contraindications/contraindications_full",
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.489Z",
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "technique_pressure",
        "concept_localized",
        "condition_localized_contraindication",
        "organ_skin"
      ],
      "short_explanation": "Warts are contagious; avoid the area but massage can be performed elsewhere.",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.147Z",
      "subcategory": "Skin and Dermatologic Issues",
      "clinical_application": "Apply universal precautions and offer a hand or seated massage as an alternative.",
      "relevance_score": 4,
      "category": "Pathology & Contraindications",
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "distractors": [],
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "answers": [
        "Warts on the feet",
        "Psoriasis covering 90% of the body",
        "Widespread sunburn",
        "General eczema flare-up"
      ],
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "synonyms": [
        "verruca",
        "foot wart"
      ],
      "topic": "pathology",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "technique_pressure",

      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "Contraindications 039",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/contraindications/contraindications_full",
      "category": "Pathology & Contraindications",
      "subcategory": "Medication and Medical Devices",
      "synonyms": [
        "blood thinning drugs",
        "bleeding risk"
      ],
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "long_explanation": "Medications like warfarin or apixaban reduce clotting ability. Even moderate pressure can cause bruising or internal bleeding. Always use light techniques unless cleared by a physician.",
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "technique_pressure",
        "client",
        "concept_caution",
        "concept_thinners",
        "concept_blood_thinners"
      ],
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.489Z",
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "correct": 3,
      "distractors": [],
      "answers": [
        "Use deep pressure to improve circulation",
        "Avoid massage altogether",
        "Apply moderate pressure but only with medical clearance",
        "Use light pressure and avoid bruising"
      ],
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "source_reference": "AMTA Medication Guidelines",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.147Z",
      "short_explanation": "Clients on anticoagulants bruise easily; use caution.",
      "topic": "pathology",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "technique_pressure",
        "client",
        "concept_caution",
        "concept_thinners",
        "concept_blood_thinners"
      ],
      "clinical_application": "Watch for signs of easy bruising, hematomas, or capillary fragility in elderly clients or those on long-term medication.",
      "question": "What caution should be taken when working with clients on blood thinners?"
    },
    {
      "id": "Contraindications 040",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "technique_compression",
        "anatomy_muscles",
        "condition_pain",
        "purpose_relaxation",
        "purpose_healing",
        "contraindication",

        "condition_injury",

      ],
      "distractors": [],
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "technique_compression",
        "anatomy_muscles",
        "condition_pain",
        "purpose_relaxation",
        "purpose_healing",
        "contraindication",
        "condition_acute_injury",
        "condition_injury",
        "concept_recovery"
      ],
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.148Z",
      "clinical_application": "Use RICE (rest, ice, compression, elevation) during inflammation. Massage is introduced later for scar remodeling or muscle relaxation.",
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "topic": "pathology",
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "subcategory": "Acute Conditions",
      "long_explanation": "This early phase (24–72 hours post-injury) involves redness, swelling, and pain. Massage can increase inflammation and disrupt natural healing if applied too soon.",
      "relevance_score": 4,
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Inflammation & Recovery Module",
      "short_explanation": "Massage is avoided during the inflammatory stage of injury.",
      "category": "Pathology & Contraindications",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "synonyms": [
        "acute phase",
        "initial injury"
      ],
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "answers": [
        "Proliferation phase",
        "Remodeling phase",
        "Inflammatory phase",
        "Recovery phase"
      ],
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/contraindications/contraindications_full",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "correct": 2,
      "question": "Massage is generally contraindicated during which stage of acute injury?",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.489Z"
    },

      ],
      "synonyms": [
        "systemic infection",
        "contagious illness"
      ],
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Outline; AMTA Clinical Guidelines",
      "category": "Pathology & Contraindications",
      "difficulty": "easy",
      "long_explanation": "When a client has a fever, flu, strep throat, or other systemic infection, massage can worsen symptoms and pose risk of transmission. Reschedule when client is symptom-free for 24+ hours."
    },
    {
      "id": "Contraindications 044",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.148Z",
      "topic": "pathology",
      "clinical_application": "Always check for surgical dates, scar healing status, and medical advice before proceeding.",
      "relevance_score": 4,
      "synonyms": [
        "surgical site",
        "recent operation"
      ],
      "long_explanation": "Recent surgeries involve fragile tissues, inflammation, risk of dehiscence, and potential blood clots. A physician's note ensures safety and timing for bodywork.",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Clinical Guidelines; AMTA Surgery Protocols",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "correct": 1,
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "anatomy_tissues",
        "purpose_stress",
        "purpose_healing",
        "concept_anxiety"
      ],
      "short_explanation": "Massage near surgical sites should not proceed without physician approval.",
      "distractors": [],
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "subcategory": "Medical Conditions Requiring Clearance",
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "category": "Pathology & Contraindications",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/contraindications/contraindications_full",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "question": "Which condition requires medical clearance before massage?",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.489Z",
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "answers": [
        "Pregnancy in the first trimester",
        "Recent surgery with staples",
        "Chronic anxiety",
        "Seasonal affective disorder"
      ],
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "anatomy_tissues",
        "purpose_stress",
        "purpose_healing",
        "concept_anxiety"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "Contraindications 045",
      "answers": [
        "Cellulitis",
        "Fungal infection (athlete's foot)",
        "Eczema in remission",
        "General dryness"
      ],
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.489Z",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/contraindications/contraindications_full",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "contraindication",
        "concept_localized",
        "population_athlete",
        "organ_skin"
      ],
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "category": "Pathology & Contraindications",
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "relevance_score": 4,
      "subcategory": "Skin Conditions",
      "long_explanation": "Athlete's foot is contagious and can spread to hands or other areas. Local massage should be avoided, and therapist hygiene precautions strictly followed.",
      "correct": 1,
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "topic": "pathology",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "distractors": [],
      "synonyms": [
        "foot fungus",
        "tinea"
      ],
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Outline; CDC Guidelines",
      "short_explanation": "Avoid massage directly on fungal infections to prevent spread.",
      "question": "Which of the following skin conditions is a local contraindication?",
      "clinical_application": "If the infection is localized to the feet, work can be done on other body areas safely.",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.148Z",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "contraindication",
        "concept_localized",
        "population_athlete",
        "organ_skin"
      ],
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0
    },
    {
      "id": "Contraindications 046",
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "question": "Which of the following cardiovascular conditions is an absolute contraindication for massage therapy?",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "answers": [
        "Hypertension managed with medication",
        "Congestive heart failure (CHF) with edema",
        "Mild arrhythmia",
        "Recent myocardial infarction"
      ],
      "difficulty_rating": 4,
      "subcategory": "Absolute Contraindications",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.148Z",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.489Z",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/contraindications/contraindications_full",
      "synonyms": [
        "heart attack",
        "MI"
      ],
      "distractors": [],
      "tags": [
        "contraindication",
        "client",
        "modality_massage_therapy",
        "organ_heart"
      ],
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Pathology Guidelines",
      "keywords": [
        "contraindication",
        "client",
        "modality_massage_therapy",
        "organ_heart"
      ],
      "category": "Pathology & Contraindications",
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "clinical_application": "Always ask about recent cardiac events or hospitalizations before working with cardiac clients.",
      "short_explanation": "Massage is contraindicated after a recent heart attack.",
      "topic": "pathology",
      "correct": 3,
      "long_explanation": "A myocardial infarction (heart attack) indicates cardiac instability. Massage should not be performed until the client has received medical clearance and is no longer in an acute phase.",
      "difficulty": "hard"
    },
    {
      "id": "Contraindications 047",
      "short_explanation": "Osteoporosis requires pressure adjustments but not avoidance of massage.",
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "category": "Pathology & Contraindications",
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/contraindications/contraindications_full",
      "tags": [
        "technique_compression",
        "anatomy_joint",
        "technique_pressure",
        "anatomy_bones",
        "purpose_benefit",
        "contraindication",
        "client",
        "modality_massage_plan",
        "joint_joint",
        "plan_plan"
      ],
      "question": "Which situation requires a modified massage plan but is not a contraindication?",
      "synonyms": [
        "bone thinning",
        "low bone density"
      ],
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.489Z",
      "correct": 1,
      "keywords": [
        "technique_compression",
        "anatomy_joint",
        "technique_pressure",
        "anatomy_bones",
        "purpose_benefit",
        "contraindication",
        "client",
        "modality_massage_plan",
        "joint_joint",
        "plan_plan"
      ],
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "clinical_application": "Use bolsters, avoid deep techniques, and educate elderly clients on benefits of gentle touch.",
      "subcategory": "Special Populations",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.148Z",
      "topic": "pathology",
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Special Populations Guide",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "distractors": [],
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "long_explanation": "Clients with osteoporosis have fragile bones. Light pressure, support positioning, and avoiding joint compression allow massage to be performed safely with modification.",
      "answers": [
        "Chemotherapy within 24 hours",
        "Client has osteoporosis",
        "Open wound on the arm",
        "Severe cellulitis"
      ],
      "relevance_score": 4
    },
    {
      "id": "Contraindications 048",
      "clinical_application": "Apply universal precautions and offer a hand or seated massage as an alternative.",
      "answers": [
        "Warts on the feet",
        "Psoriasis covering 90% of the body",
        "Widespread sunburn",
        "General eczema flare-up"
      ],
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "technique_pressure",
        "concept_localized",
        "condition_localized_contraindication",
        "organ_skin"
      ],
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.489Z",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/contraindications/contraindications_full",
      "correct": 0,
      "topic": "pathology",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "short_explanation": "Warts are contagious; avoid the area but massage can be performed elsewhere.",
      "distractors": [],
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "relevance_score": 4,
      "question": "Which of the following skin conditions is a localized contraindication?",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.148Z",
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "subcategory": "Skin and Dermatologic Issues",
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "technique_pressure",
        "concept_localized",
        "condition_localized_contraindication",
        "organ_skin"
      ],
      "synonyms": [
        "verruca",
        "foot wart"
      ],
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Dermatology Standards",
      "category": "Pathology & Contraindications",
      "difficulty": "easy",
      "long_explanation": "Warts are caused by HPV and can spread through skin contact or contamination. Avoid direct pressure on affected sites, especially plantar warts on the feet."
    },
    {
      "id": "Contraindications 049",
      "topic": "pathology",
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "technique_pressure",
        "client",
        "concept_caution",
        "concept_thinners",
        "concept_blood_thinners"
      ],
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "question": "What caution should be taken when working with clients on blood thinners?",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.148Z",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.489Z",
      "clinical_application": "Watch for signs of easy bruising, hematomas, or capillary fragility in elderly clients or those on long-term medication.",
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "distractors": [],
      "source_reference": "AMTA Medication Guidelines",
      "short_explanation": "Clients on anticoagulants bruise easily; use caution.",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "answers": [
        "Use deep pressure to improve circulation",
        "Avoid massage altogether",
        "Apply moderate pressure but only with medical clearance",
        "Use light pressure and avoid bruising"
      ],
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "category": "Pathology & Contraindications",
      "subcategory": "Medication and Medical Devices",
      "correct": 3,
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "synonyms": [
        "blood thinning drugs",
        "bleeding risk"
      ],
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/contraindications/contraindications_full",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "technique_pressure",
        "client",
        "concept_caution",
        "concept_thinners",
        "concept_blood_thinners"
      ],
      "long_explanation": "Medications like warfarin or apixaban reduce clotting ability. Even moderate pressure can cause bruising or internal bleeding. Always use light techniques unless cleared by a physician."
    },
    {
      "id": "Contraindications 050",
      "question": "Massage is generally contraindicated during which stage of acute injury?",
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "technique_compression",
        "anatomy_muscles",
        "condition_pain",
        "purpose_relaxation",
        "purpose_healing",
        "contraindication",
        "condition_acute_injury",
        "condition_injury",
        "concept_recovery"
      ],
      "distractors": [],
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Inflammation & Recovery Module",
      "synonyms": [
        "acute phase",
        "initial injury"
      ],
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "short_explanation": "Massage is avoided during the inflammatory stage of injury.",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/contraindications/contraindications_full",
      "long_explanation": "This early phase (24–72 hours post-injury) involves redness, swelling, and pain. Massage can increase inflammation and disrupt natural healing if applied too soon.",
      "answers": [
        "Proliferation phase",
        "Remodeling phase",
        "Inflammatory phase",
        "Recovery phase"
      ],
      "correct": 2,
      "relevance_score": 4,
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "topic": "pathology",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "technique_compression",
        "anatomy_muscles",
        "condition_pain",
        "purpose_relaxation",
        "purpose_healing",
        "contraindication",
        "condition_acute_injury",
        "condition_injury",
        "concept_recovery"
      ],
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "category": "Pathology & Contraindications",
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "subcategory": "Acute Conditions",
      "clinical_application": "Use RICE (rest, ice, compression, elevation) during inflammation. Massage is introduced later for scar remodeling or muscle relaxation.",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.489Z",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.148Z"
    },
    {
      "id": "Endangerment Sites 001",
      "question": "What is an endangerment site in massage therapy?",
      "source_reference": "Manual Therapy Safety Text – Endangerment Sites",
      "answers": [
        "An area that is always painful to touch",
        "A location where vital structures are close to the surface and can be injured",
        "A bony landmark with limited circulation",
        "A common site for cramping"
      ],
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.149Z",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "keywords": [
        "condition_pain",
        "ethics_boundary",
        "modality_massage_therapy",
        "anatomy_endangerment_site"
      ],
      "short_explanation": "An endangerment site is where vital structures lie close to the surface.",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "topic": "pathology",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "variant": 1,
      "tags": [
        "condition_pain",
        "ethics_boundary",
        "modality_massage_therapy",
        "anatomy_endangerment_site"
      ],
      "synonyms": [],
      "long_explanation": "These sites are vulnerable to injury because arteries, nerves, and organs are more superficial and can be compressed or damaged by massage pressure.",
      "category": "Pathology",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/endangerment_sites",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "correct": 1,
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T22:17:31.600763Z",
      "distractors": [],
      "clinical_application": "Helps therapists identify risk zones and adjust pressure accordingly."
    },
    {
      "id": "Endangerment Sites 002",
      "short_explanation": "The anterior triangle of the neck contains multiple vital structures.",
      "clinical_application": "Therapists must use light touch or avoid this area entirely.",
      "distractors": [],
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/endangerment_sites",
      "category": "Pathology",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "variant": 2,
      "keywords": [
        "anatomy_muscles",
        "anatomy_endangerment_site",
        "muscle_deltoid"
      ],
      "long_explanation": "It houses the carotid artery, jugular vein, vagus nerve, and parts of the thyroid, all of which are susceptible to pressure damage.",
      "topic": "pathology",
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T22:17:31.600763Z",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "question": "Which of the following is an endangerment site?",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "answers": [
        "Deltoid muscle belly",
        "Posterior forearm",
        "Anterior triangle of the neck",
        "Sole of the foot"
      ],
      "correct": 2,
      "source_reference": "Anatomical Boundaries in Massage – Safety Manual",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.149Z",
      "synonyms": [],
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_muscles",
        "anatomy_endangerment_site",
        "muscle_deltoid"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "Endangerment Sites 003",
      "clinical_application": "Used to protect neurovascular integrity when working around the shoulder.",
      "answers": [
        "It contains major arteries, veins, and lymph nodes",
        "It is surrounded by muscle tissue",
        "It has few nerve endings",
        "It is located near the scapula"
      ],
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_nerve",

        "anatomy_muscles",

      ],
      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T22:17:31.600763Z",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/endangerment_sites",
      "correct": 0,
      "variant": 3,
      "topic": "pathology",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "short_explanation": "The axilla contains arteries, veins, lymph nodes, and nerves.",
      "distractors": [],
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "question": "Why is the axilla considered an endangerment site?",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.149Z",
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "keywords": [
        "anatomy_nerve",

        "anatomy_endangerment_site",
        "bone_scapula"
      ],
      "synonyms": [],
      "source_reference": "Clinical Anatomy for Massage – Axillary Region Guide",
      "category": "Pathology",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "long_explanation": "Structures like the brachial plexus and axillary artery are located here, requiring minimal or no pressure during massage."
    },
    {
      "id": "Endangerment Sites 004",
      "synonyms": [],
      "question": "Which structure is located in the anterior triangle of the neck?",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T22:17:31.600763Z",
      "long_explanation": "Too much pressure here can trigger vagal responses, lower heart rate, or cause dizziness.",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/endangerment_sites",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.149Z",
      "topic": "pathology",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "distractors": [],

        "Femoral artery",
        "Carotid artery and vagus nerve",
        "Median nerve"
      ],
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "short_explanation": "The carotid artery and vagus nerve run through the anterior triangle.",
      "source_reference": "Massage Risk Zones – Neck and Head Guidelines",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "correct": 2,
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_nerve",
        "anatomy_artery",

        "artery_femoral_artery"
      ],
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "difficulty": "intermediate",

      "clinical_application": "Massage therapists must work around this area with caution or use light touch only.",

    {

      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T22:17:31.600763Z",

      "relevance_score": 5,
      "category": "Pathology",
      "question": "Which of the following should be avoided due to proximity to the kidneys?",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "short_explanation": "The low back near the 12th rib is close to the kidneys.",
      "correct": 1,
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "answers": [
        "Upper trapezius",
        "Low back near the 12th rib",
        "Gluteal fold",
        "Posterior thigh"
      ],
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.149Z",
      "variant": 5,
      "clinical_application": "Practitioners often avoid this zone or use gentle techniques.",
      "keywords": [

        "organ_kidneys"
      ],
      "distractors": [],

      },
      "source_reference": "Massage and Visceral Safety – Kidney Precautions",
      "long_explanation": "Deep pressure here can bruise or stress the kidneys, especially in dehydrated or sensitive individuals."
    },
    {
      "id": "Endangerment Sites 006",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.149Z",
      "synonyms": [],
      "distractors": [],
      "clinical_application": "Massage therapists must use flat, superficial strokes if any.",
      "question": "Why is the popliteal fossa considered an endangerment site?",
      "category": "Pathology",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/endangerment_sites",
      "tags": [

        "artery_femoral_artery"
      ],
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "answers": [
        "It has thick connective tissue",
        "It contains the femoral artery",
        "It contains blood vessels and nerves behind the knee",
        "It is prone to soreness after exercise"
      ],
      "variant": 6,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T22:17:31.600763Z",
      "keywords": [
        "anatomy_nerve",
        "anatomy_artery",
        "anatomy_tissues",
        "anatomy_connective_tissue",

        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "long_explanation": "This space behind the knee holds the popliteal artery and tibial nerve, which can be compressed by direct pressure.",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "short_explanation": "The popliteal fossa contains major blood vessels and nerves.",

    },
    {
      "id": "Endangerment Sites 007",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "synonyms": [],
      "long_explanation": "These structures lie in a shallow region at the elbow crease and must not be compressed.",
      "question": "Which of the following structures lies within the cubital (antecubital) fossa?",
      "keywords": [
        "anatomy_nerve",
        "anatomy_artery",

      "clinical_application": "Therapists use draping, communication, and caution when working near this area.",

      "topic": "pathology",
      "long_explanation": "Deep or percussive techniques can cause harm when vital structures are superficial.",

      "question": "Which technique is appropriate when working near an endangerment site?",
      "answers": [
        "Deep pressure with knuckles",

      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/endangerment_sites",

    {
      "id": "Endangerment Sites 010",
      "short_explanation": "The subclavian region contains lymph nodes and major vessels.",

      "clinical_application": "Excessive pressure can affect circulation and lymphatic drainage.",

      "last_answered_at": null,
      "topic": "pathology",
      "question": "Why must care be taken when massaging the subclavian region under the clavicle?",
      "answers": [

        "It leads to muscle cramping",
        "It is a common site for trigger points"

    {
      "id": "Scoliosis Kyphosis Lordosis 001",
      "clinical_application": "Massage can address compensatory tension and postural imbalance in clients with scoliosis.",
      "answers": [
        "An exaggerated forward curvature of the lumbar spine",
        "An abnormal lateral curvature of the spine",
        "An inward curve of the thoracic spine",
        "A twisted pelvis"
      ],
      "correct": 1,
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "source_reference": "Salvo, Trail Guide to the Body, FSMTB",
      "unit": "scoliosis kyphosis lordosis",
      "category": "Pathology",
      "topic": "pathology",
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_spine",
        "condition_scoliosis"
      ],
      "img": "spine_condition_001.png",
      "question": "What is scoliosis?",
      "keywords": [
        "anatomy_spine",
        "condition_scoliosis"
      ],
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.491Z",
      "distractors": [],
      "long_explanation": "This condition involves a lateral curvature of the spine, often shaped like an S or C, which may lead to asymmetry and muscular imbalance.",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.149Z",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "short_explanation": "Scoliosis is a sideways curve in the spine."
    },
    {
      "id": "Scoliosis Kyphosis Lordosis 002",
      "answers": [
        "Cervical",
        "Lumbar",
        "Thoracic",
        "Sacral"
      ],
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_spine",
        "condition_kyphosis"
      ],
      "unit": "scoliosis kyphosis lordosis",
      "img": "spine_condition_002.png",
      "short_explanation": "Kyphosis affects the thoracic spine.",
      "correct": 2,
      "clinical_application": "Massage helps reduce upper back tension and promote postural awareness in kyphotic clients.",
      "distractors": [],
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.491Z",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "topic": "pathology",
      "source_reference": "Salvo, Trail Guide to the Body, FSMTB",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "keywords": [
        "anatomy_spine",
        "condition_kyphosis"
      ],
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.149Z",
      "long_explanation": "Excessive kyphosis causes the thoracic spine to curve outward, leading to a hunched back and postural strain.",
      "category": "Pathology",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "question": "Kyphosis primarily affects which region of the spine?"
    },
    {
      "id": "Scoliosis Kyphosis Lordosis 003",
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.149Z",
      "topic": "pathology",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "long_explanation": "Lordosis is an excessive inward curve of the lumbar spine, often linked to weak abdominal muscles and tight hip flexors.",
      "tags": [],
      "category": "Pathology",
      "answers": [
        "Thoracic outward curve",
        "Lumbar inward curve",
        "Cervical outward curve",
        "Sacral backward curve"
      ],
      "source_reference": "Salvo, Trail Guide to the Body, FSMTB",
      "short_explanation": "Lordosis increases the inward lumbar curve.",
      "unit": "scoliosis kyphosis lordosis",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "keywords": [],
      "correct": 1,
      "img": "spine_condition_003.png",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.491Z",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "clinical_application": "Massage supports lumbar relief and stretches contributing muscles.",
      "distractors": [],
      "question": "Which spinal curve is exaggerated in lordosis?"
    },
    {
      "id": "Scoliosis Kyphosis Lordosis 004",
      "topic": "pathology",
      "distractors": [],
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "short_explanation": "Kyphosis is often seen as rounded shoulders or hunching.",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "keywords": [
        "condition_kyphosis"
      ],
      "source_reference": "Salvo, Trail Guide to the Body, FSMTB",
      "correct": 2,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.491Z",
      "clinical_application": "Massage can lengthen shortened anterior muscles and improve thoracic mobility.",
      "category": "Pathology",
      "tags": [
        "condition_kyphosis"
      ],
      "long_explanation": "Excessive kyphotic posture is associated with slumped shoulders and protruded upper back, affecting respiratory and muscular function.",
      "question": "What posture is commonly associated with excessive kyphosis?",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "img": "spine_condition_004.png",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.149Z",
      "unit": "scoliosis kyphosis lordosis",
      "answers": [
        "Sway back",
        "Flat back",
        "Rounded shoulders and hunching",
        "Elevated pelvis"
      ]
    },

    },
    {
      "id": "Scoliosis Kyphosis Lordosis 007",
      "img": "spine_condition_007.png",
      "answers": [
        "Strengthening the spinal curves",
        "Even, deep pressure on both sides of the spine",

      "short_explanation": "Massage for scoliosis targets balance and mobility.",

      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.491Z",

      "distractors": [],
      "source_reference": "Salvo, Trail Guide to the Body, FSMTB",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.149Z",
      "question": "Massage therapy for scoliosis should focus on:",
      "keywords": [
        "technique_pressure",
        "anatomy_muscles",

        "condition_scoliosis",
        "purpose_stress",

      "last_answered_at": null,
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "long_explanation": "Rather than applying even pressure, therapists aim to release tension on the convex side and support function on the concave side of the spinal curve.",
      "topic": "pathology"
    },
    {
      "id": "Scoliosis Kyphosis Lordosis 008",
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "long_explanation": "A rounded thoracic spine can reduce lung expansion and strain the neck and upper back muscles.",
      "source_reference": "Salvo, Trail Guide to the Body, FSMTB",
      "unit": "scoliosis kyphosis lordosis",
      "tags": [
        "condition_kyphosis",
        "organ_lungs"
      ],
      "clinical_application": "Massage therapy enhances breathing capacity and reduces postural fatigue.",

      "img": "spine_condition_008.png",
      "correct": 1,
      "distractors": [],
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.149Z",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.491Z",
      "short_explanation": "Kyphosis may impair thoracic motion and neck posture.",
      "keywords": [
        "condition_kyphosis",
        "organ_lungs"
      ],
      "topic": "pathology",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "answers": [
        "Improved lung capacity",
        "Neck strain and restricted thoracic movement",
        "Increased lumbar flexibility",
        "Greater spinal stability"
      ],
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "difficulty": "intermediate"
    },
    {
      "id": "Scoliosis Kyphosis Lordosis 009",
      "source_reference": "Salvo, Trail Guide to the Body, FSMTB",
      "unit": "scoliosis kyphosis lordosis",
      "clinical_application": "Massage can reduce pain, decompress tissues, and stretch hip flexors.",
      "distractors": [],
      "img": "spine_condition_009.png",
      "short_explanation": "Lordosis may cause sciatic pain and lumbar strain.",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.491Z",

      "difficulty": "intermediate",

      ],

    {
      "id": "Scoliosis Kyphosis Lordosis 010",
      "long_explanation": "These conditions affect the front-to-back curvature of the spine, unlike scoliosis, which impacts the frontal plane.",
      "clinical_application": "Massage interventions can help correct muscular patterns that exaggerate these curves.",
      "topic": "pathology",
      "short_explanation": "Kyphosis and lordosis alter sagittal spinal curves.",

    {

      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.152Z",
      "topic": "pathology",
      "long_explanation": "When vein valves collapse or fail, blood pools and veins become distended, especially in the legs.",
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_artery"
      ],
      "correct": 1,
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "clinical_application": "Massage should avoid direct pressure on varicosities and focus on circulation enhancement around affected areas.",
      "question": "What causes varicose veins?",
      "short_explanation": "Varicose veins are caused by faulty vein valves.",
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0,

      },

      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.493Z",
      "question": "Osteoporosis primarily affects the:",
      "question_rank": 3,
      "long_explanation": "This condition reduces bone density, increasing the risk of fractures, especially in the spine, hips, and wrists.",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "clinical_application": "Massage must be gentle and adapted to avoid excessive pressure that could damage fragile bones.",
      "distractors": [],
      "topic": "pathology"
    },
    {
      "id": "Pathology Beginner 004",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "answers": [
        "Inflamed vein",
        "Blocked artery",
        "Pooling of blood outside a vessel",
        "Bone fracture"
      ],
      "stats": {

      },
      "topic": "pathology",
      "img": "pathology_004.png",

      "clinical_application": "Massage is contraindicated locally but may support drainage in surrounding tissues.",

      ],
      "correct": 2,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.493Z",
      "short_explanation": "A hematoma is pooled blood outside a vessel.",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "question_rank": 4,
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "keywords": [
        "anatomy_artery",
        "anatomy_bones"
      ],
      "long_explanation": "Often the result of trauma or injury, it appears as swelling or bruising and should not be massaged directly.",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.152Z",
      "category": "Pathology",
      "source_reference": "Salvo, FSMTB, Massage Pathology Manual"
    },
    {
      "id": "Pathology Beginner 005",
      "source_reference": "Salvo, FSMTB, Massage Pathology Manual",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.152Z",
      "keywords": [
        "anatomy_artery"
      ],
      "category": "Pathology",
      "topic": "pathology",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.493Z",
      "long_explanation": "It may result from trauma, IV injection, or clot formation and often presents with redness, pain, and swelling.",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "unit": "pathology beginner",
      "distractors": [],
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "clinical_application": "Massage is locally contraindicated to avoid aggravating the inflammation or dislodging a clot.",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "short_explanation": "Phlebitis is inflammation of a vein.",
      "img": "pathology_005.png",
      "question_rank": 5,
      "answers": [
        "A blood clot in a coronary artery",
        "Inflammation of a vein",
        "Air bubbles in the bloodstream",
        "Infection of lymph nodes"
      ],
      "question": "Phlebitis refers to:",
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_artery"
      ],
      "correct": 1,
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      }
    },
    {
      "id": "Pathology Beginner 006",
      "correct": 2,
      "unit": "pathology beginner",
      "long_explanation": "This condition is serious and can lead to embolism if a clot travels through the bloodstream.",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.152Z",
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_artery",
        "anatomy_bones"
      ],
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.493Z",
      "topic": "pathology",
      "distractors": [],
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "source_reference": "Salvo, FSMTB, Massage Pathology Manual",
      "img": "pathology_006.png",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "question": "Thrombophlebitis involves:",
      "keywords": [
        "anatomy_artery",
        "anatomy_bones"
      ],
      "clinical_application": "Massage is strongly contraindicated and clients should be referred to medical care.",
      "answers": [
        "Arterial bleeding",
        "Clot formation in a lymph vessel",
        "Clotting in an inflamed vein",
        "Bone marrow overgrowth"
      ],
      "category": "Pathology",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "short_explanation": "Thrombophlebitis involves a clot in an inflamed vein.",
      "question_rank": 6
    },
    {
      "id": "Pathology Beginner 007",
      "category": "Pathology",
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "keywords": [
        "anatomy_artery",
        "technique_pressure"
      ],
      "long_explanation": "It may lodge in the lungs (pulmonary embolism), brain (stroke), or heart, causing sudden symptoms.",
      "clinical_application": "Massage is contraindicated when embolic risk is present due to serious complications.",
      "img": "pathology_007.png",
      "topic": "pathology",
      "distractors": [],
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "question_rank": 7,
      "question": "What is an embolus?",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.152Z",
      "source_reference": "Salvo, FSMTB, Massage Pathology Manual",
      "correct": 0,
      "answers": [
        "A blood clot that travels through the bloodstream",
        "A bruise from deep pressure",
        "A blocked lymph node",
        "An inflamed artery"
      ],
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.493Z",
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_artery",
        "technique_pressure"
      ],
      "short_explanation": "An embolus is a traveling blood clot.",
      "unit": "pathology beginner"
    },
    {
      "id": "Pathology Beginner 008",
      "short_explanation": "An aneurysm is a ballooning artery wall.",
      "question": "An aneurysm is:",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.152Z",
      "distractors": [],
      "unit": "pathology beginner",
      "clinical_application": "Massage should avoid the affected area and encourage medical evaluation if suspected.",
      "img": "pathology_008.png",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "category": "Pathology",
      "correct": 2,
      "long_explanation": "It may rupture under pressure, leading to internal bleeding and life-threatening consequences.",
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "question_rank": 8,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.493Z",
      "source_reference": "Salvo, FSMTB, Massage Pathology Manual",
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_joint",
        "anatomy_artery",
        "joint_joint",
        "organ_lungs"
      ],
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "answers": [
        "A thickening of joint cartilage",
        "A pooling of blood within the lungs",
        "A ballooning or weakening of an artery wall",
        "A displaced vertebral disc"
      ],
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "keywords": [
        "anatomy_joint",
        "anatomy_artery",
        "joint_joint",

      ],

    {
      "id": "Pathology Beginner 009",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.493Z",
      "question": "Lymphedema is caused by:",
      "answers": [
        "A blocked artery",
        "Nerve compression",
        "Lymphatic system obstruction causing swelling",
        "Increased red blood cell production"
      ],
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "unit": "pathology beginner",
      "question_rank": 9,
      "short_explanation": "Lymphedema results from lymphatic blockage.",
      "keywords": [
        "technique_compression",
        "anatomy_nerve",
        "anatomy_artery",
        "anatomy_cells",
        "anatomy_red_blood"
      ],
      "long_explanation": "This condition leads to swelling due to impaired drainage and may be congenital or result from surgery or infection.",
      "clinical_application": "Massage therapists trained in manual lymphatic drainage (MLD) may treat under supervision.",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "correct": 2,
      "category": "Pathology",
      "topic": "pathology",

    {
      "id": "Pathology Beginner 010",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "source_reference": "Salvo, FSMTB, Massage Pathology Manual",
      "unit": "pathology beginner",
      "distractors": [],
      "keywords": [

      ],
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.152Z",
      "img": "pathology_010.png",
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "question_rank": 10,
      "correct": 2,
      "clinical_application": "Therapists must avoid massage until medically cleared.",
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "condition_scoliosis",
        "contraindication"
      ],
      "long_explanation": "Applying pressure near inflamed, clot-prone veins risks dislodging a thrombus and causing embolism.",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.493Z",
      "topic": "pathology",
      "short_explanation": "Thrombophlebitis is a contraindication for massage.",
      "answers": [
        "Mild scoliosis",
        "Stable osteoporosis",
        "Thrombophlebitis",
        "Mild varicose veins"
      ],
      "category": "Pathology",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "question": "Massage is generally contraindicated in which of the following?"
    },
    {
      "id": "Massage Pathology 001",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T22:21:29.264728Z",
      "tags": [
        "contraindications",
        "infectious_disease",
        "pathology_safety"
      ],
      "variant": 1,
      "synonyms": [],
      "source_reference": "Massage Therapy and Pathology – Ruth Werner",
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "created_at": "2025-05-26T22:21:29.264728Z",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/general_pathologies",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "clinical_application": "Massage therapists must screen for respiratory symptoms and defer treatment if TB is suspected.",
      "correct": 1,
      "answers": [
        "Tension headache",
        "Active tuberculosis",
        "Chronic sinusitis",
        "Plantar fasciitis"
      ],
      "long_explanation": "Due to airborne transmission risk and systemic illness, clients with active TB must not receive massage until medically cleared.",
      "short_explanation": "Active tuberculosis is highly contagious and poses a serious risk during close-contact treatments.",
      "question": "Which of the following conditions is considered an absolute contraindication for massage therapy?",
      "relevance_score": 5
    },
    {
      "id": "Massage Pathology 002",
      "created_at": "2025-05-26T22:21:29.264728Z",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T22:21:29.264728Z",
      "synonyms": [],
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "clinical_application": "Massage can offer relief with gentle pressure and patient communication.",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/general_pathologies",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "answers": [
        "Joint inflammation",
        "Widespread pain and fatigue",
        "Muscle atrophy",
        "Bruising and swelling"
      ],
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "correct": 1,
      "variant": 2,
      "short_explanation": "Fibromyalgia causes persistent pain, fatigue, and tender points across the body.",
      "long_explanation": "Though poorly understood, fibromyalgia involves central sensitization and is often triggered or worsened by stress.",
      "source_reference": "Pathophysiology for the Massage Therapist – Werner",
      "question": "What is fibromyalgia primarily characterized by?",
      "tags": [
        "fibromyalgia",
        "chronic_pain",
        "massage_modification"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "Massage Pathology 003",
      "tags": [
        "infection",
        "skin_conditions",
        "referral_required"
      ],
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T22:21:29.264728Z",
      "question": "What is the massage recommendation for a client with acute cellulitis?",
      "clinical_application": "Therapists must recognize signs like redness, warmth, and swelling before starting any session.",
      "long_explanation": "Massage may spread the infection or worsen inflammation. The client must receive antibiotic treatment and medical clearance.",
      "created_at": "2025-05-26T22:21:29.264728Z",
      "correct": 2,
      "variant": 3,
      "answers": [
        "Avoid only the affected limb",
        "Proceed with light effleurage",
        "Refer out immediately, do not treat",
        "Apply heat and friction"
      ],
      "short_explanation": "Cellulitis is a bacterial infection requiring medical care.",
      "source_reference": "Clinical Massage Protocols – CEU Safety Manual",
      "synonyms": [],
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/general_pathologies",
      "relevance_score": 5
    },
    {
      "id": "Massage Pathology 004",
      "question": "Which pathology might cause radiating pain down the leg due to nerve compression?",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "answers": [
        "Sciatica",
        "Gout",
        "Bursitis",
        "Carpal tunnel syndrome"
      ],
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/general_pathologies",
      "long_explanation": "It often originates from lumbar disc issues or muscular entrapment (e.g., piriformis syndrome), causing leg pain and numbness.",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T22:21:29.264728Z",
      "variant": 4,
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "source_reference": "Nervous System Disorders – Pathology in Practice",
      "synonyms": [],
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "short_explanation": "Sciatica results from compression or irritation of the sciatic nerve.",
      "tags": [
        "sciatica",
        "nerve_pathology",
        "pain_referral"
      ],
      "created_at": "2025-05-26T22:21:29.264728Z",
      "clinical_application": "Massage can relieve associated muscular tension and support recovery when cleared.",
      "correct": 0
    },
    {
      "id": "Massage Pathology 005",
      "clinical_application": "Avoid massage during flares and use gentle techniques during remission.",
      "synonyms": [],
      "tags": [
        "autoimmune",
        "arthritis",
        "joint_disease"
      ],
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "question": "Which of the following is an autoimmune condition that may affect joint mobility?",
      "correct": 1,
      "created_at": "2025-05-26T22:21:29.264728Z",
      "short_explanation": "RA is an autoimmune disease that targets synovial joints.",
      "long_explanation": "It causes symmetrical joint inflammation, deformity, and may require massage adaptation depending on flare status.",
      "answers": [
        "Osteoarthritis",
        "Rheumatoid arthritis",
        "Gout",
        "Tendinitis"
      ],
      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T22:21:29.264728Z",
      "variant": 5,
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/general_pathologies",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "source_reference": "Massage and Joint Pathologies – Clinical Overview"
    },
    {
      "id": "Massage Pathology 006",
      "created_at": "2025-05-26T22:21:29.264728Z",
      "correct": 0,
      "tags": [
        "respiratory_disorder",
        "positioning",
        "massage_modification"
      ],
      "synonyms": [],
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "long_explanation": "Massage may aid relaxation and improve breath mechanics but prone pressure may restrict breathing.",
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "source_reference": "Breath and Bodywork – Respiratory Support Strategies",
      "clinical_application": "Support with bolsters and focus on gentle chest and back work.",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T22:21:29.264728Z",
      "answers": [
        "Avoid prone position and use chest-opening techniques",
        "Avoid any form of massage",
        "Apply deep abdominal pressure",
        "Only use vibration"
      ],
      "short_explanation": "Chronic asthma requires careful positioning and breath support.",
      "question": "What is the best massage approach for a client with chronic asthma?",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/general_pathologies",
      "variant": 6
    },
    {
      "id": "Massage Pathology 007",
      "correct": 2,
      "source_reference": "Red Flag Symptoms in Manual Therapy – Emergency Protocols",
      "answers": [
        "Client yawning",
        "Mild soreness",
        "Dizziness or blurred vision",
        "Muscle twitching"
      ],
      "variant": 7,
      "question": "Which sign indicates a massage should be stopped immediately?",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "long_explanation": "Such signs suggest potential emergency; stop the session and assess further or refer out.",
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "synonyms": [],
      "created_at": "2025-05-26T22:21:29.264728Z",
      "short_explanation": "These symptoms may indicate vagal response or neurological event.",
      "clinical_application": "Always monitor client responses during treatment.",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "tags": [
        "emergency_response",
        "safety_signs",
        "neurological_symptoms"
      ],
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/general_pathologies",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T22:21:29.264728Z"
    },
    {
      "id": "Massage Pathology 008",
      "source_reference": "Massage for Structural Conditions – Scoliosis Protocols",
      "answers": [
        "Deep tissue along the convex side only",
        "Avoid all spinal work",
        "Balance tension with bilateral work and positioning",
        "Use only percussive strokes"
      ],
      "question": "What is the typical massage consideration for scoliosis?",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T22:21:29.264728Z",
      "synonyms": [],
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/general_pathologies",
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "clinical_application": "Useful for pain management, breathing support, and posture training.",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "short_explanation": "Scoliosis creates asymmetrical spinal tension.",
      "tags": [
        "musculoskeletal",
        "scoliosis",
        "posture_disorders"
      ],
      "correct": 2,
      "created_at": "2025-05-26T22:21:29.264728Z",
      "long_explanation": "A balanced approach helps reduce compensatory strain and improves alignment over time.",
      "variant": 8,
      "question_type": "multiple choice"
    },
    {
      "id": "Massage Pathology 009",
      "correct": 3,
      "short_explanation": "Each condition may flare or worsen with direct pressure.",
      "question": "Which skin condition is a local contraindication for massage?",
      "synonyms": [],
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "long_explanation": "Local massage may cause irritation or spread infection; modify or avoid depending on the area and stage.",
      "clinical_application": "Visually assess the skin and communicate with the client.",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T22:21:29.264728Z",
      "source_reference": "Massage and Skin Health – Pathology Handbook",
      "tags": [
        "skin_conditions",
        "local_contraindications",
        "dermatology"
      ],
      "created_at": "2025-05-26T22:21:29.264728Z",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "answers": [
        "Psoriasis",
        "Eczema",
        "Severe sunburn",
        "All of the above"
      ],
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/general_pathologies",
      "variant": 9,
      "question_type": "multiple choice"
    },
    {
      "id": "Massage Pathology 010",
      "synonyms": [],
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "long_explanation": "Pressure must be light and controlled, especially near the spine, hips, and ribs.",
      "clinical_application": "Use bolstering and avoid joint traction or percussive techniques.",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/general_pathologies",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "answers": [
        "Avoid all contact",
        "Use deep tissue for circulation",
        "Use gentle techniques and avoid compression",
        "Only massage the extremities"
      ],
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "tags": [
        "osteoporosis",
        "geriatric_massage",
        "bone_safety"
      ],
      "short_explanation": "Osteoporosis weakens bones and increases fracture risk.",
      "created_at": "2025-05-26T22:21:29.264728Z",
      "source_reference": "Massage for Elder Populations – Clinical Geriatric Manual",
      "question": "What massage guideline applies to a client with osteoporosis?",
      "correct": 2,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T22:21:29.264728Z",
      "variant": 10
    },
    {
      "id": "Pathology 001",
      "tags": [
        "technique_friction",
        "technique_tapotement",

      ],
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.150Z",
      "clinical_application": "Monitor client response carefully and adjust pressure based on sensitivity.",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/pathology_ii",
      "short_explanation": "Clients with fibromyalgia respond best to light, soothing touch.",
      "long_explanation": "Fibromyalgia is characterized by chronic widespread pain, fatigue, and tender points. Gentle massage can reduce symptoms, but aggressive techniques may cause flare-ups.",
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "category": "Pathology & Contraindications",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.492Z",
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "answers": [
        "Avoid any form of touch therapy",
        "Use rapid tapotement to stimulate nerves",
        "Use gentle, rhythmic techniques and avoid overstimulation",
        "Apply deep friction to reduce pain"
      ],
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "variant": 1,
      "keywords": [

        "concept_soothing"
      ],

      "synonyms": [
