        "chronic pain syndrome"
      ],
      "subcategory": "Musculoskeletal Disorders",
      "topic": "pathology",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "distractors": [],
      "correct": 2,
      "question": "What is a key massage consideration when working with a client who has fibromyalgia? [Variant 1]",
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Pathology Guide; AMTA Fibromyalgia Protocol",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "last_answered_at": null
    },
    {
      "id": "Pathology 002",
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "short_explanation": "DVT is a medical emergency and contraindicates massage.",
      "relevance_score": 4,
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/pathology_ii",
      "synonyms": [
        "thrombosis",
        "venous clot"
      ],
      "answers": [
        "Varicose veins",
        "Deep vein thrombosis (DVT)",
        "Raynaud’s phenomenon",
        "Intermittent claudication"
      ],
      "correct": 1,
      "variant": 2,
      "keywords": [
        "condition_pain",
        "client",
        "modality_massage_contraindication"
      ],
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "distractors": [],
      "question": "Which condition requires immediate medical referral and is a massage contraindication? [Variant 2]",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.150Z",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Pathology & Safety Standards",
      "category": "Pathology & Contraindications",
      "clinical_application": "Always screen for signs of DVT in the intake interview before performing massage.",
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "tags": [
        "condition_pain",
        "client",
        "modality_massage_contraindication"
      ],
      "long_explanation": "Massage over or near an active DVT can dislodge a clot and lead to life-threatening embolism. Clients with symptoms such as swelling, pain, and redness should be referred immediately.",
      "subcategory": "Circulatory Disorders",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.492Z",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "topic": "pathology"
    },
    {
      "id": "Pathology 003",
      "keywords": [
        "technique_pressure",
        "anatomy_nervous_system",
        "purpose_relaxation",
        "purpose_stress",
        "purpose_benefit",
        "client",
        "modality_massage_adaptation",
        "concept_focus"
      ],
      "distractors": [],
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "synonyms": [
        "nervous system",
        "neuro disorders"
      ],
      "subcategory": "Nervous System Disorders",
      "source_reference": "AMTA MS Guidelines; FSMTB Special Populations",
      "answers": [
        "Only use heat and hydrotherapy",
        "Avoid massage altogether",
        "Use moderate pressure, avoid overstimulation and fatigue",
        "Focus only on the affected limbs"
      ],
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.492Z",
      "clinical_application": "Avoid heat or long sessions. Monitor for fatigue and sensory sensitivity.",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/pathology_ii",
      "correct": 2,
      "relevance_score": 3,
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "question": "What is a massage adaptation for a client with multiple sclerosis (MS)? [Variant 3]",
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "short_explanation": "Clients with MS benefit from relaxing massage with careful pacing.",
      "tags": [
        "technique_pressure",
        "anatomy_nervous_system",
        "purpose_relaxation",
        "purpose_stress",
        "purpose_benefit",
        "client",
        "modality_massage_adaptation",
        "concept_focus"
      ],
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.150Z",
      "variant": 3,
      "long_explanation": "Multiple sclerosis affects the nervous system, leading to fatigue, spasticity, and coordination issues. Massage can reduce stress and support function but must be adjusted for client tolerance.",
      "category": "Pathology & Contraindications",
      "topic": "pathology",
      "question_type": "multiple choice"
    },
    {
      "id": "Pathology 004",
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.150Z",
      "category": "Pathology & Contraindications",
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "condition_pain",
        "contraindication",
        "client",
        "organ_skin"
      ],
      "variant": 4,
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "correct": 1,
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/pathology_ii",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "clinical_application": "Inspect the skin before massage and avoid areas with signs of infection or inflammation.",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "condition_pain",
        "contraindication",
        "client",
        "organ_skin"
      ],
      "synonyms": [
        "skin redness",
        "acute infection"
      ],
      "distractors": [],
      "answers": [
        "Psoriasis (in remission)",
        "Cellulitis",
        "Eczema with dry skin",
        "Benign moles"
      ],
      "subcategory": "Skin Disorders",
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "topic": "pathology",
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Infection Control; CDC Guidelines",
      "question": "Massage over which condition is contraindicated due to risk of infection? [Variant 4]",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.492Z",
      "short_explanation": "Cellulitis is a bacterial skin infection and contraindicates massage.",
      "long_explanation": "Massage can spread infection in cases of cellulitis. Symptoms include redness, warmth, pain, and swelling. Refer clients with suspected infections for medical care.",
      "difficulty": "easy",
      "relevance_score": 2
    },
    {
      "id": "Pathology 005",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "short_explanation": "Gentle, relaxing massage is ideal during remission periods.",
      "synonyms": [
        "systemic lupus",
        "autoimmune flare"
      ],
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "category": "Pathology & Contraindications",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.492Z",
      "distractors": [],
      "long_explanation": "Lupus is an autoimmune disease that may cause joint pain, skin rashes, and systemic inflammation. Avoid aggressive techniques and adapt based on symptom severity.",
      "answers": [
        "Apply deep tissue work to joints",
        "Use vigorous friction to reduce inflammation",
        "Use light to moderate pressure and avoid flare-up areas",
        "Only massage the head and neck"
      ],
      "subcategory": "Autoimmune Conditions",
      "clinical_application": "Avoid direct massage during flare-ups and check for medications that affect sensitivity.",
      "question": "Which massage consideration is appropriate for a client with lupus? [Variant 5]",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_deep_tissue",
        "technique_friction",
        "anatomy_joint",
        "technique_pressure",
        "anatomy_tissues",
        "condition_pain",
        "purpose_relaxation",

        "joint_joint",
        "organ_skin"
      ],
      "correct": 2,

    {
      "id": "Pathology 006",
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "topic": "pathology",
      "variant": 6,
      "relevance_score": 4,
      "short_explanation": "Clients with fibromyalgia respond best to light, soothing touch.",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "synonyms": [
        "fibro",
        "chronic pain syndrome"
      ],
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.492Z",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "long_explanation": "Fibromyalgia is characterized by chronic widespread pain, fatigue, and tender points. Gentle massage can reduce symptoms, but aggressive techniques may cause flare-ups.",
      "tags": [
        "technique_friction",
        "technique_tapotement",
        "anatomy_nerve",
        "technique_pressure",
        "condition_pain",
        "condition_fibromyalgia",
        "purpose_relaxation",
        "client",
        "modality_massage_consideration",
        "concept_soothing"
      ],
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.150Z",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/pathology_ii",
      "keywords": [
        "technique_friction",
        "technique_tapotement",
        "anatomy_nerve",
        "technique_pressure",
        "condition_pain",
        "condition_fibromyalgia",
        "purpose_relaxation",
        "client",
        "modality_massage_consideration",
        "concept_soothing"
      ],
      "subcategory": "Musculoskeletal Disorders",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "category": "Pathology & Contraindications",
      "correct": 2,
      "distractors": [],
      "answers": [
        "Avoid any form of touch therapy",
        "Use rapid tapotement to stimulate nerves",
        "Use gentle, rhythmic techniques and avoid overstimulation",
        "Apply deep friction to reduce pain"
      ],
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Pathology Guide; AMTA Fibromyalgia Protocol",
      "question": "What is a key massage consideration when working with a client who has fibromyalgia? [Variant 6]",
      "clinical_application": "Monitor client response carefully and adjust pressure based on sensitivity."
    },
    {
      "id": "Pathology 007",
      "relevance_score": 3,
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.150Z",
      "distractors": [],
      "category": "Pathology & Contraindications",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Pathology & Safety Standards",
      "answers": [
        "Varicose veins",
        "Deep vein thrombosis (DVT)",
        "Raynaud’s phenomenon",
        "Intermittent claudication"
      ],
      "long_explanation": "Massage over or near an active DVT can dislodge a clot and lead to life-threatening embolism. Clients with symptoms such as swelling, pain, and redness should be referred immediately.",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.492Z",
      "correct": 1,
      "topic": "pathology",
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "clinical_application": "Always screen for signs of DVT in the intake interview before performing massage.",
      "short_explanation": "DVT is a medical emergency and contraindicates massage.",
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "variant": 7,
      "question": "Which condition requires immediate medical referral and is a massage contraindication? [Variant 7]",
      "tags": [
        "condition_pain",

      ],

      "clinical_application": "Avoid direct massage during flare-ups and check for medications that affect sensitivity.",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,

      "synonyms": [

        "autoimmune flare"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "Pathology 016",
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "synonyms": [
        "fibro",
        "chronic pain syndrome"
      ],
      "long_explanation": "Fibromyalgia is characterized by chronic widespread pain, fatigue, and tender points. Gentle massage can reduce symptoms, but aggressive techniques may cause flare-ups.",
      "keywords": [
        "technique_friction",
        "technique_tapotement",
        "anatomy_nerve",
        "technique_pressure",
        "condition_pain",
        "condition_fibromyalgia",
        "purpose_relaxation",
        "client",
        "modality_massage_consideration",
        "concept_soothing"
      ],
      "answers": [
        "Avoid any form of touch therapy",
        "Use rapid tapotement to stimulate nerves",
        "Use gentle, rhythmic techniques and avoid overstimulation",
        "Apply deep friction to reduce pain"
      ],
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "category": "Pathology & Contraindications",
      "distractors": [],
      "tags": [
        "technique_friction",
        "technique_tapotement",
        "anatomy_nerve",
        "technique_pressure",
        "condition_pain",
        "condition_fibromyalgia",
        "purpose_relaxation",
        "client",
        "modality_massage_consideration",
        "concept_soothing"
      ],
      "short_explanation": "Clients with fibromyalgia respond best to light, soothing touch.",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.150Z",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "question": "What is a key massage consideration when working with a client who has fibromyalgia? [Variant 16]",
      "subcategory": "Musculoskeletal Disorders",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.492Z",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/pathology_ii",
      "variant": 16,
      "relevance_score": 2,
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Pathology Guide; AMTA Fibromyalgia Protocol",
      "correct": 2,
      "topic": "pathology",
      "clinical_application": "Monitor client response carefully and adjust pressure based on sensitivity."
    },
    {
      "id": "Pathology 017",
      "synonyms": [
        "thrombosis",
        "venous clot"
      ],
      "distractors": [],
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.150Z",
      "subcategory": "Circulatory Disorders",
      "keywords": [
        "condition_pain",
        "client",
        "modality_massage_contraindication"
      ],
      "long_explanation": "Massage over or near an active DVT can dislodge a clot and lead to life-threatening embolism. Clients with symptoms such as swelling, pain, and redness should be referred immediately.",
      "tags": [
        "condition_pain",
        "client",
        "modality_massage_contraindication"
      ],
      "answers": [
        "Varicose veins",
        "Deep vein thrombosis (DVT)",
        "Raynaud’s phenomenon",
        "Intermittent claudication"
      ],
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Pathology & Safety Standards",
      "category": "Pathology & Contraindications",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/pathology_ii",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "short_explanation": "DVT is a medical emergency and contraindicates massage.",
      "question": "Which condition requires immediate medical referral and is a massage contraindication? [Variant 17]",
      "topic": "pathology",
      "correct": 1,
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "clinical_application": "Always screen for signs of DVT in the intake interview before performing massage.",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.492Z",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "variant": 17,
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations"
    },
    {
      "id": "Pathology 018",
      "category": "Pathology & Contraindications",
      "answers": [
        "Only use heat and hydrotherapy",
        "Avoid massage altogether",
        "Use moderate pressure, avoid overstimulation and fatigue",
        "Focus only on the affected limbs"
      ],
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.492Z",
      "keywords": [
        "technique_pressure",
        "anatomy_nervous_system",
        "purpose_relaxation",
        "purpose_stress",
        "purpose_benefit",
        "client",
        "modality_massage_adaptation",
        "concept_focus"
      ],
      "topic": "pathology",
      "source_reference": "AMTA MS Guidelines; FSMTB Special Populations",
      "relevance_score": 4,
      "distractors": [],
      "short_explanation": "Clients with MS benefit from relaxing massage with careful pacing.",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.150Z",
      "question": "What is a massage adaptation for a client with multiple sclerosis (MS)? [Variant 18]",
      "synonyms": [
        "nervous system",
        "neuro disorders"
      ],
      "tags": [
        "technique_pressure",
        "anatomy_nervous_system",
        "purpose_relaxation",
        "purpose_stress",
        "purpose_benefit",
        "client",
        "modality_massage_adaptation",
        "concept_focus"
      ],
      "variant": 18,
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "clinical_application": "Avoid heat or long sessions. Monitor for fatigue and sensory sensitivity.",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "long_explanation": "Multiple sclerosis affects the nervous system, leading to fatigue, spasticity, and coordination issues. Massage can reduce stress and support function but must be adjusted for client tolerance.",
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "correct": 2,
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/pathology_ii",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "subcategory": "Nervous System Disorders",
      "last_answered_at": null
    },
    {
      "id": "Pathology 019",
      "distractors": [],
      "topic": "pathology",
      "answers": [
        "Psoriasis (in remission)",
        "Cellulitis",
        "Eczema with dry skin",
        "Benign moles"
      ],
      "category": "Pathology & Contraindications",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "subcategory": "Skin Disorders",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/pathology_ii",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.492Z",
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "condition_pain",
        "contraindication",
        "client",
        "organ_skin"
      ],
      "long_explanation": "Massage can spread infection in cases of cellulitis. Symptoms include redness, warmth, pain, and swelling. Refer clients with suspected infections for medical care.",
      "short_explanation": "Cellulitis is a bacterial skin infection and contraindicates massage.",
      "question": "Massage over which condition is contraindicated due to risk of infection? [Variant 19]",
      "variant": 19,
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.150Z",
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "clinical_application": "Inspect the skin before massage and avoid areas with signs of infection or inflammation.",
      "correct": 1,
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Infection Control; CDC Guidelines",
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",

      ]
    },

      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/pathology_ii",
      "variant": 20,
      "question": "Which massage consideration is appropriate for a client with lupus? [Variant 20]",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.150Z",
      "topic": "pathology",
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "long_explanation": "Lupus is an autoimmune disease that may cause joint pain, skin rashes, and systemic inflammation. Avoid aggressive techniques and adapt based on symptom severity.",
      "answers": [
        "Apply deep tissue work to joints",
        "Use vigorous friction to reduce inflammation",
        "Use light to moderate pressure and avoid flare-up areas",
        "Only massage the head and neck"
      ],
      "clinical_application": "Avoid direct massage during flare-ups and check for medications that affect sensitivity."
    },

      "short_explanation": "DVT is a medical emergency and contraindicates massage.",
      "clinical_application": "Always screen for signs of DVT in the intake interview before performing massage.",
      "question": "Which condition requires immediate medical referral and is a massage contraindication? [Variant 27]",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.150Z",
      "keywords": [
        "condition_pain",
        "client",
        "modality_massage_contraindication"
      ],
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0
    },
    {
      "id": "Pathology 028",
      "keywords": [
        "technique_pressure",
        "anatomy_nervous_system",
        "purpose_relaxation",
        "purpose_stress",
        "purpose_benefit",
        "client",
        "modality_massage_adaptation",
        "concept_focus"
      ],
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/pathology_ii",
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "subcategory": "Nervous System Disorders",
      "relevance_score": 2,
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "distractors": [],
      "long_explanation": "Multiple sclerosis affects the nervous system, leading to fatigue, spasticity, and coordination issues. Massage can reduce stress and support function but must be adjusted for client tolerance.",
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "tags": [
        "technique_pressure",
        "anatomy_nervous_system",
        "purpose_relaxation",
        "purpose_stress",
        "purpose_benefit",
        "client",
        "modality_massage_adaptation",
        "concept_focus"
      ],
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "question": "What is a massage adaptation for a client with multiple sclerosis (MS)? [Variant 28]",
      "short_explanation": "Clients with MS benefit from relaxing massage with careful pacing.",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.150Z",
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "category": "Pathology & Contraindications",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.492Z",
      "clinical_application": "Avoid heat or long sessions. Monitor for fatigue and sensory sensitivity.",
      "variant": 28,
      "synonyms": [
        "nervous system",
        "neuro disorders"
      ],
      "answers": [
        "Only use heat and hydrotherapy",
        "Avoid massage altogether",
        "Use moderate pressure, avoid overstimulation and fatigue",
        "Focus only on the affected limbs"
      ],
      "topic": "pathology",
      "source_reference": "AMTA MS Guidelines; FSMTB Special Populations",
      "correct": 2
    },
    {
      "id": "Pathology 029",
      "clinical_application": "Inspect the skin before massage and avoid areas with signs of infection or inflammation.",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "condition_pain",
        "contraindication",
        "client",
        "organ_skin"
      ],
      "question": "Massage over which condition is contraindicated due to risk of infection? [Variant 29]",
      "subcategory": "Skin Disorders",
      "answers": [
        "Psoriasis (in remission)",
        "Cellulitis",
        "Eczema with dry skin",
        "Benign moles"
      ],
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/pathology_ii",
      "long_explanation": "Massage can spread infection in cases of cellulitis. Symptoms include redness, warmth, pain, and swelling. Refer clients with suspected infections for medical care.",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "correct": 1,
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Infection Control; CDC Guidelines",
      "category": "Pathology & Contraindications",
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "difficulty_rating": 2,
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "distractors": [],
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.151Z",
      "short_explanation": "Cellulitis is a bacterial skin infection and contraindicates massage.",
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "condition_pain",
        "contraindication",
        "client",
        "organ_skin"
      ],
      "topic": "pathology",
      "difficulty": "easy",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.492Z",
      "synonyms": [
        "skin redness",
        "acute infection"
      ],
      "variant": 29,
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "last_answered_at": null
    },
    {
      "id": "Pathology 030",
      "question": "Which massage consideration is appropriate for a client with lupus? [Variant 30]",
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Autoimmune Conditions; AMTA Lupus Resource",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.492Z",
      "clinical_application": "Avoid direct massage during flare-ups and check for medications that affect sensitivity.",
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "answers": [
        "Apply deep tissue work to joints",
        "Use vigorous friction to reduce inflammation",
        "Use light to moderate pressure and avoid flare-up areas",
        "Only massage the head and neck"
      ],
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "category": "Pathology & Contraindications",
      "topic": "pathology",
      "synonyms": [
        "systemic lupus",
        "autoimmune flare"
      ],
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.151Z",
      "distractors": [],
      "short_explanation": "Gentle, relaxing massage is ideal during remission periods.",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/pathology_ii",
      "variant": 30,
      "correct": 2,
      "tags": [
        "modality_deep_tissue",
        "technique_friction",
        "anatomy_joint",
        "technique_pressure",
        "anatomy_tissues",
        "condition_pain",
        "purpose_relaxation",
        "client",
        "modality_massage_consideration",
        "joint_joint",
        "organ_skin"
      ],
      "relevance_score": 4,
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "long_explanation": "Lupus is an autoimmune disease that may cause joint pain, skin rashes, and systemic inflammation. Avoid aggressive techniques and adapt based on symptom severity.",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "keywords": [
        "modality_deep_tissue",

    },
    {
      "id": "Pathology 031",
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "clinical_application": "Monitor client response carefully and adjust pressure based on sensitivity.",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "variant": 31,
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "synonyms": [
        "fibro",
        "chronic pain syndrome"
      ],
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.492Z",
      "distractors": [],
      "relevance_score": 3,
      "long_explanation": "Fibromyalgia is characterized by chronic widespread pain, fatigue, and tender points. Gentle massage can reduce symptoms, but aggressive techniques may cause flare-ups.",
      "short_explanation": "Clients with fibromyalgia respond best to light, soothing touch.",
      "question": "What is a key massage consideration when working with a client who has fibromyalgia? [Variant 31]",
      "tags": [
        "technique_friction",
        "technique_tapotement",
        "anatomy_nerve",
        "technique_pressure",
        "condition_pain",
        "condition_fibromyalgia",
        "purpose_relaxation",
        "client",
        "modality_massage_consideration",
        "concept_soothing"
      ],
      "category": "Pathology & Contraindications",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.151Z",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/pathology_ii",
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Pathology Guide; AMTA Fibromyalgia Protocol",
      "correct": 2,
      "topic": "pathology",
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0
      },
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "subcategory": "Musculoskeletal Disorders",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "answers": [
        "Avoid any form of touch therapy",
        "Use rapid tapotement to stimulate nerves",
        "Use gentle, rhythmic techniques and avoid overstimulation",
        "Apply deep friction to reduce pain"
      ],
      "keywords": [
        "technique_friction",
        "technique_tapotement",
        "anatomy_nerve",
        "technique_pressure",
        "condition_pain",
        "condition_fibromyalgia",
        "purpose_relaxation",
        "client",

      ],
      "difficulty": "moderate"
    },
    {
      "id": "Pathology 032",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/pathology_ii",
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "question": "Which condition requires immediate medical referral and is a massage contraindication? [Variant 32]",
      "category": "Pathology & Contraindications",
      "distractors": [],
      "long_explanation": "Massage over or near an active DVT can dislodge a clot and lead to life-threatening embolism. Clients with symptoms such as swelling, pain, and redness should be referred immediately.",
      "short_explanation": "DVT is a medical emergency and contraindicates massage.",
      "synonyms": [
        "thrombosis",
        "venous clot"
      ],
      "relevance_score": 2,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.492Z",
      "answers": [
        "Varicose veins",
        "Deep vein thrombosis (DVT)",
        "Raynaud’s phenomenon",
        "Intermittent claudication"
      ],
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.151Z",

      ],

      ],
      "clinical_application": "Always screen for signs of DVT in the intake interview before performing massage.",
      "topic": "pathology",
      "subcategory": "Circulatory Disorders"
    },

      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations"
    },
    {
      "id": "Pathology 040",
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_deep_tissue",
        "technique_friction",
        "anatomy_joint",
        "technique_pressure",
        "anatomy_tissues",
        "condition_pain",
        "purpose_relaxation",
        "client",
        "modality_massage_consideration",
        "joint_joint",
        "organ_skin"
      ],
      "clinical_application": "Avoid direct massage during flare-ups and check for medications that affect sensitivity.",
      "long_explanation": "Lupus is an autoimmune disease that may cause joint pain, skin rashes, and systemic inflammation. Avoid aggressive techniques and adapt based on symptom severity.",
      "synonyms": [
        "systemic lupus",
        "autoimmune flare"
      ],
      "topic": "pathology",
      "relevance_score": 2,
      "answers": [
        "Apply deep tissue work to joints",
        "Use vigorous friction to reduce inflammation",
        "Use light to moderate pressure and avoid flare-up areas",
        "Only massage the head and neck"
      ],
      "category": "Pathology & Contraindications",
      "short_explanation": "Gentle, relaxing massage is ideal during remission periods.",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.151Z",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "tags": [
        "modality_deep_tissue",
        "technique_friction",
        "anatomy_joint",
        "technique_pressure",
        "anatomy_tissues",
        "condition_pain",
        "purpose_relaxation",
        "client",
        "modality_massage_consideration",
        "joint_joint",
        "organ_skin"
      ],
      "subcategory": "Autoimmune Conditions",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Autoimmune Conditions; AMTA Lupus Resource",
      "question": "Which massage consideration is appropriate for a client with lupus? [Variant 40]",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/pathology_ii",
      "correct": 2,
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0,

    {
      "id": "Pathology 041",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "tags": [

        "concept_soothing"
      ],
      "clinical_application": "Monitor client response carefully and adjust pressure based on sensitivity.",
      "answers": [
        "Avoid any form of touch therapy",
        "Use rapid tapotement to stimulate nerves",
        "Use gentle, rhythmic techniques and avoid overstimulation",
        "Apply deep friction to reduce pain"
      ],
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/pathology_ii",
      "question": "What is a key massage consideration when working with a client who has fibromyalgia? [Variant 41]",
      "variant": 41,
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "correct": 2,
      "synonyms": [
        "fibro",
        "chronic pain syndrome"
      ],
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "distractors": [],
      "short_explanation": "Clients with fibromyalgia respond best to light, soothing touch.",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "subcategory": "Musculoskeletal Disorders",
      "long_explanation": "Fibromyalgia is characterized by chronic widespread pain, fatigue, and tender points. Gentle massage can reduce symptoms, but aggressive techniques may cause flare-ups.",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.151Z",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "keywords": [

    {
      "id": "Pathology 042",
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.151Z",
      "synonyms": [

      ],

      "stats": {

      },
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "answers": [
        "Varicose veins",
        "Deep vein thrombosis (DVT)",
        "Raynaud’s phenomenon",
        "Intermittent claudication"
      ],
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/pathology_ii",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "topic": "pathology",
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",
      "question": "Which condition requires immediate medical referral and is a massage contraindication? [Variant 42]",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "short_explanation": "DVT is a medical emergency and contraindicates massage.",
      "distractors": [],
      "clinical_application": "Always screen for signs of DVT in the intake interview before performing massage.",
      "long_explanation": "Massage over or near an active DVT can dislodge a clot and lead to life-threatening embolism. Clients with symptoms such as swelling, pain, and redness should be referred immediately.",
      "relevance_score": 4,
      "keywords": [
        "condition_pain",
        "client",
        "modality_massage_contraindication"
      ],
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "correct": 1,
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Pathology & Safety Standards",
      "tags": [
        "condition_pain",
        "client",
        "modality_massage_contraindication"
      ],
      "category": "Pathology & Contraindications",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.492Z",
      "variant": 42
    },
    {
      "id": "Pathology 043",

      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "long_explanation": "Multiple sclerosis affects the nervous system, leading to fatigue, spasticity, and coordination issues. Massage can reduce stress and support function but must be adjusted for client tolerance.",

      "topic": "pathology",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/pathology/pathology_ii",

      ],
      "variant": 43,
      "clinical_application": "Avoid heat or long sessions. Monitor for fatigue and sensory sensitivity.",
      "question": "What is a massage adaptation for a client with multiple sclerosis (MS)? [Variant 43]",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "answers": [
        "Only use heat and hydrotherapy",
        "Avoid massage altogether",
        "Use moderate pressure, avoid overstimulation and fatigue",
        "Focus only on the affected limbs"
      ],
      "short_explanation": "Clients with MS benefit from relaxing massage with careful pacing.",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.492Z",
      "mblex_category": "Pathology, Contraindications, Areas of Caution, Special Populations",

    {
      "id": "SOAP Notes Intermediate 002",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",

      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "topic": "soap_notes",
      "question": "In which section would you include postural deviations noted during visual assessment?",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "short_explanation": "Postural findings are objective data from visual assessment.",
      "distractors": [],
      "long_explanation": "Observations like asymmetry, shifts, or muscular patterns belong in the Objective section.",
      "clinical_application": "Improves assessment clarity and interdisciplinary relevance.",
      "keywords": [
        "exam_assessment",
        "plan_plan"
      ],
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "correct": 1,
      "source_reference": "Visual Assessment in SOAP Notes – Posture and Pain Manual",

        "plan_plan"
      ],
      "category": "Soap Notes",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T22:23:28.820251Z",
      "variant": 2
    },

    {
      "id": "SOAP Notes Intermediate 005",

        "client",

      "answers": [
        "Using anatomical language",

        "Listing functional goals"

    {
      "id": "SOAP Notes Intermediate 006",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "question": "A therapist notices limited cervical rotation during AROM. Where is this noted?",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "answers": [
        "Assessment",
        "Plan",
        "Subjective",
        "Objective"
      ],
      "category": "Soap Notes",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T22:23:28.820251Z",
      "topic": "soap_notes",
      "synonyms": [],
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "variant": 6,
      "distractors": [],
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/soap_notes/notes_intermediate",
      "relevance_score": 5,

        "ethics_boundary",
        "plan_plan"

      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.154Z",

      "stats": {

        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "keywords": [
        "exam_assessment",
        "ethics_boundary",
        "plan_plan"
      ],
      "long_explanation": "AROM limitations observed by the therapist should be noted under Objective, not subjective or assessment.",
      "clinical_application": "Improves documentation reliability in musculoskeletal conditions.",
      "source_reference": "Objective vs Subjective – SOAP Accuracy Training",
      "short_explanation": "Range of motion findings are Objective observations."
    },

      "short_explanation": "The 'A' in SOAP stands for Assessment.",
      "question_rank": 3,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-24T14:54:13.494Z",
      "unit": "soap notes easy",
      "long_explanation": "Assessment is the therapist's professional interpretation of the client's condition based on subjective and objective data.",

      "short_explanation": "The client provides the information for the Subjective section.",

    {
      "id": "SOAP Notes Hard 001",
      "long_explanation": "Progressive SOAP notes build a clinical narrative over time, supporting evolving treatment decisions.",
      "topic": "soap_notes",
      "variant": 1,
      "short_explanation": "Therapists should track changes in each session segment.",
      "correct": 1,
      "question": "In complex SOAP notes, how should therapists record evolving client conditions?",
      "answers": [
        "Cross out old notes and rewrite",
        "Use progressive documentation with updates in each section",
        "Delete prior history",
        "Only update during reassessment"
      ],
      "tags": [
        "assessment_soap",
        "client",
        "ethics_documentation",
        "documentation_soap_notes"
      ],
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "clinical_application": "Clarifies how to update each session while maintaining a legal timeline.",

    {
      "id": "SOAP Notes Hard 002",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T22:22:33.101045Z",
      "answers": [
        "A note written only in medical codes",
        "A legally sound and clinically accurate record",
        "A handwritten note kept privately",
        "A referral from another practitioner"
      ],
      "question": "What is a defensible SOAP note?",
      "long_explanation": "They show intent, accuracy, and alignment with ethical and insurance requirements.",
      "short_explanation": "Defensible notes support both treatment logic and legal standards.",
      "source_reference": "Ethical Records in Healthcare – Legal Documentation Manual",
      "tags": [
        "assessment_soap",
        "documentation_soap_note"
      ],
      "distractors": [],
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/soap_notes/notes_hard",

    {
      "id": "SOAP Notes Hard 003",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.153Z",

      ],
      "topic": "soap_notes",

      "answers": [

    {
      "id": "SOAP Notes Hard 004",
      "topic": "soap_notes",
      "long_explanation": "Only measurable data (e.g., posture, tone, pain scale) should be used.",
      "clinical_application": "Reinforces scientific credibility and consistency in professional records.",
      "keywords": [
        "assessment_soap",
        "client",
        "ethics_documentation",

      "variant": 4,
      "synonyms": [],
      "short_explanation": "Objectivity requires documentation without opinion or bias.",
      "source_reference": "SOAP Note Mastery – Objective Reporting",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.153Z",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "question": "What method helps reduce bias in the Objective section of SOAP notes?",
      "category": "Soap Notes",
      "distractors": [],
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "answers": [
        "Using client metaphors",
        "Stating only measurable findings",
        "Avoiding documentation of findings",
        "Adding emotional responses"
      ],
      "question_type": "multiple choice"
    },

    {
      "id": "SOAP Notes Hard 006",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T22:22:33.101045Z",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/soap_notes/notes_hard",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "question": "What legal risk arises from incomplete SOAP notes?",
      "long_explanation": "Massage records are legal documents and must be complete for protection.",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "clinical_application": "Encourages therapists to treat notes as essential professional tools.",
      "topic": "soap_notes",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "tags": [
        "assessment_soap",
        "client",
        "ethics_documentation",
        "documentation_soap_notes"
      ],
      "correct": 2,
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "synonyms": [],
      "source_reference": "Legal Protection through SOAP Notes – Practice Standard Text",
      "short_explanation": "If documentation is missing or incomplete, liability increases.",
      "answers": [
        "Therapist receives lower tips",
        "Client may request a refund",
        "Lack of documentation in claims or complaints",
        "Therapist gets fewer clients"
      ],
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.153Z",

    {
      "id": "SOAP Notes Hard 007",
      "topic": "soap_notes",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.153Z",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "synonyms": [],
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T22:22:33.101045Z",
      "question": "If a client provides inconsistent symptom reports, how should this be reflected?",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "tags": [
        "client",
        "ethics_documentation",
        "plan_plan"
      ],
      "category": "Soap Notes",
      "source_reference": "Therapist Ethics – Subjective Reporting Guidelines",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "variant": 7,
      "distractors": [],
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "short_explanation": "SOAP integrity means recording inconsistencies without bias.",
      "answers": [
        "Document both versions factually in Subjective",
        "Ignore conflicting statements",
        "Record only the recent version",
        "Put it in Plan as a client goal"
      ],
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/soap_notes/notes_hard",
      "long_explanation": "This shows clinical awareness and protects against distortion.",
      "clinical_application": "Avoids assumptions or editing based on therapist judgment.",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "correct": 0,
      "keywords": [
        "client",
        "ethics_documentation",
        "plan_plan"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "SOAP Notes Hard 008",

      "stats": {

    {
      "id": "SOAP Notes Hard 009",
      "topic": "soap_notes",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.153Z",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "source_reference": "Objective Entries in Uncertain Cases – Documentation Workshop",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T22:22:33.101045Z",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "answers": [
        "Use vague terms like 'weird'",
        "Leave it blank",
        "Note uncertainty clearly and reference reassessment",
        "Include client opinion instead"
      ],
      "short_explanation": "Transparency builds trust and allows accurate follow-up.",
      "tags": [
        "client",
        "documentation_objective_section"
      ],
      "synonyms": [],
      "clinical_application": "Improves clarity without overstating confidence.",
      "distractors": [],

      ],
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/soap_notes/notes_hard",
      "category": "Soap Notes",
      "question": "How should a therapist address unclear findings in the Objective section?",
      "correct": 2,
      "long_explanation": "Unclear findings should be marked with intention to recheck."
    },

      "short_explanation": "Postural findings are objective data from visual assessment.",

      "variant": 5,
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.154Z",
      "long_explanation": "Only clinical, ethical, and outcome-related data should be recorded to maintain professionalism.",
      "category": "Soap Notes",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/soap_notes/notes_intermediate",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "answers": [
        "Using anatomical language",
        "Recording client preferences",
        "Documenting irrelevant personal beliefs",
        "Listing functional goals"
      ],
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "short_explanation": "Irrelevant beliefs are inappropriate in SOAP notes."
    },
    {
      "id": "SOAP Notes Intermediate 006",
      "variant": 6,
      "keywords": [
        "exam_assessment",
        "ethics_boundary",
        "plan_plan"
      ],
      "source_reference": "Objective vs Subjective – SOAP Accuracy Training",
      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T22:23:28.820251Z",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,

      },
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "tags": [

        "ethics_boundary",
        "plan_plan"
      ],
      "clinical_application": "Improves documentation reliability in musculoskeletal conditions.",
      "category": "Soap Notes",
      "synonyms": [],
      "correct": 3,
      "short_explanation": "Range of motion findings are Objective observations.",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "distractors": [],
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.154Z",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/soap_notes/notes_intermediate",
      "question": "A therapist notices limited cervical rotation during AROM. Where is this noted?",
      "long_explanation": "AROM limitations observed by the therapist should be noted under Objective, not subjective or assessment.",
      "answers": [
        "Assessment",
        "Plan",
        "Subjective",
        "Objective"
      ],
      "topic": "soap_notes",
      "difficulty": "intermediate"
    },
    {
      "id": "SOAP Notes Intermediate 007",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/soap_notes/notes_intermediate",
      "tags": [

      "stats": {

      "synonyms": [],
      "clinical_application": "Essential for integrated care environments and insurance claims.",
      "answers": [
        "They allow for billing justification",
        "They replace intake forms",
        "They ensure therapists use essential oils",
        "They substitute for client records"
      ],
      "long_explanation": "They provide a standardized communication tool for justifying care and coordinating treatment with other providers.",
      "keywords": [

      "short_explanation": "SOAP notes support billing and interdisciplinary clarity.",

    {
      "id": "SOAP Notes Intermediate 008",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "source_reference": "SOAP Structure and Logic – Intermediate Therapist Guide",
      "question": "The 'Plan' section may include all EXCEPT:",
      "variant": 8,
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.154Z",
      "topic": "soap_notes",
      "correct": 2,
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T22:23:28.820251Z",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/soap_notes/notes_intermediate",
      "stats": {
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "distractors": [],
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "tags": [
        "client",
        "plan_plan"
      ],
      "long_explanation": "The Plan section includes referrals, recommendations, and session scheduling – not client-reported symptoms.",
      "category": "Soap Notes",
      "short_explanation": "Complaints go in Subjective, not Plan.",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "keywords": [
        "client",
        "plan_plan"
      ],
      "clinical_application": "Ensures SOAP notes remain organized and legally sound.",
      "answers": [
        "Referral to another provider",
        "Future session frequency",
        "Client complaints",
        "Home care recommendations"
      ],
      "synonyms": [],
      "difficulty_rating": 3
    },
    {
      "id": "SOAP Notes Intermediate 009",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "topic": "soap_notes",
      "stats": {
        "correct": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "attempts": 0
      },
      "variant": 9,
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0,
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "question": "What would 'palpation of trigger point in left levator scapulae' be classified as?",
      "clinical_application": "Clarifies evidence of muscular dysfunction or pain patterns.",
      "answers": [
        "Subjective",
        "Objective",
        "Assessment",
        "Plan"
      ],
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/soap_notes/notes_intermediate",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "keywords": [
        "modality_trigger_point",
        "exam_assessment",
        "muscle_levator_scapulae",
        "plan_plan"
      ],
      "correct": 1,
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.154Z",
      "short_explanation": "Trigger point palpation is an Objective finding.",
      "category": "Soap Notes",
      "synonyms": [],
      "long_explanation": "Direct palpation results are recorded under Objective as they reflect measurable, therapist-observed data.",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "source_reference": "Trigger Point Integration – SOAP Objective Section Manual",
      "tags": [
        "modality_trigger_point",
        "exam_assessment",
        "muscle_levator_scapulae",
        "plan_plan"
      ],
      "distractors": [],
      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T22:23:28.820251Z"
    },
    {
      "id": "SOAP Notes Intermediate 010",
      "relevance_score": 5,
      "distractors": [],
      "stats": {
        "attempts": 0,
        "incorrect": 0,
        "correct": 0
      },
      "answers": [
        "They are reviewed by insurance, peers, and legal authorities",
        "They are displayed in the clinic lobby",
        "They are used in marketing",
        "They reduce massage duration"
      ],
      "keywords": [
        "modality_massage",
        "assessment_soap",
        "documentation_soap_notes",
        "business_marketing"
      ],
      "topic": "soap_notes",
      "correct": 0,
      "synonyms": [],
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "question_type": "multiple choice",
      "tags": [
        "modality_massage",
        "assessment_soap",
        "documentation_soap_notes",
        "business_marketing"
      ],
      "long_explanation": "Legible, structured notes can be used by insurance, courts, or health professionals to validate care.",
      "last_answered_at": null,
      "clinical_application": "Protects legal liability and enhances clinical reputation.",
      "short_explanation": "SOAP clarity protects both therapist and client.",
      "created_at": "2025-05-24T14:34:08.154Z",
      "difficulty_rating": 3,
      "source_reference": "Professional SOAP Standards – Compliance Documentation Manual",
      "variant": 10,
      "updated_at": "2025-05-26T22:23:28.820251Z",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/soap_notes/notes_intermediate",
      "question": "Why is clarity and legibility important in SOAP notes?",
      "category": "Soap Notes",
      "difficulty_adjustment": 0
    },
    {
      "id": "Rom Arom Prom 001",
      "long_explanation": "ROM (Range of Motion) refers to the full movement potential of a joint, usually measured in degrees. It's essential for evaluating flexibility and function.",
      "correct": 1,
      "question_rank": 1,
      "topic": "techniques",
      "source_reference": "FSMTB, Trail Guide to Movement, Salvo",
      "tags": [
        "anatomy_joint",
        "mobility_assessment",
        "clinical_evaluation"
      ],
      "answers": [
        "Rate of Motion",
        "Range of Motion",
        "Rhythm of Massage",
        "Rotation of Muscles"
      ],
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "img": "rom_icon.png",
      "category": "Techniques",
      "clinical_application": "Massage therapists assess ROM to tailor treatments, reduce restrictions, and restore joint mobility, especially post-injury or in chronic conditions.",
      "question": "What does ROM stand for in massage and physical therapy?",
      "short_explanation": "ROM means how far a joint can move through its natural range.",
      "unit": "rom arom prom",
      "keywords": [
        "ROM",
        "range of motion",
        "flexibility",
        "joint mobility"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "Rom Arom Prom 002",
      "category": "Techniques",
      "answers": [
        "Movement produced by a therapist",
        "Joint movement without any effort",
        "Movement performed entirely by the client using their own muscles",
        "Movement resisted by equipment"
      ],
      "source_reference": "FSMTB, Trail Guide to Movement, Salvo",
      "question_rank": 2,
      "tags": [
        "client_active",
        "assessment_arom",
        "anatomy_muscles"
      ],
      "img": "arom_example.png",
      "clinical_application": "Therapists assess AROM to gauge muscle strength, neurological control, and joint integrity before applying deeper techniques.",
      "short_explanation": "AROM is movement initiated and controlled by the client.",
      "topic": "techniques",
      "long_explanation": "AROM involves voluntary contraction of muscles by the client without external aid. It's used to evaluate neuromuscular function and pain tolerance.",
      "keywords": [
        "active motion",
        "self-initiated movement",
        "client control"
      ],
      "question": "Which best defines Active Range of Motion (AROM)?",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "unit": "rom arom prom",
      "correct": 2
    },
    {
      "id": "Rom Arom Prom 003",
      "keywords": [
        "passive motion",
        "joint mobility",
        "therapist-guided"
      ],
      "source_reference": "FSMTB, Trail Guide to Movement, Salvo",
      "topic": "techniques",
      "correct": 0,
      "short_explanation": "PROM means the therapist moves the client's joint without muscle effort from the client.",
      "tags": [
        "therapist_passive",
        "assessment_prom",
        "rehab_technique"
      ],
      "img": "prom_diagram.png",
      "question": "What does PROM stand for?",
      "question_rank": 3,
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "long_explanation": "PROM is used when the therapist or device moves the joint while the client remains passive. It's important for assessing joint health and flexibility when muscle activity is limited.",
      "unit": "rom arom prom",
      "clinical_application": "PROM is often used post-surgery, with stroke clients, or in pain management to restore mobility without overloading the system.",
      "answers": [
        "Passive Range of Motion",
        "Progressive Rate of Massage",
        "Primary Rotation of Muscle",
        "Peripheral Range of Movement"
      ],
      "category": "Techniques"
    },
    {
      "id": "Rom Arom Prom 004",
      "topic": "techniques",
      "category": "Techniques",
      "short_explanation": "PROM involves movement from an external source.",
      "clinical_application": "Used in rehab settings when clients can't move independently or when guarding is present due to pain or neurological conditions.",
      "question_rank": 4,
      "img": "passive_range_illustration.png",
      "unit": "rom arom prom",
      "question": "In PROM, joint movement is:",
      "source_reference": "FSMTB, Trail Guide to Movement, Salvo",
      "long_explanation": "Unlike AROM, PROM is done without client effort. It helps evaluate ligament flexibility, joint function, and connective tissue integrity.",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "correct": 2,
      "answers": [
        "Generated by the client's muscles",
        "Assisted by a mechanical device",
        "Produced by an outside force like a therapist",
        "Not allowed at all"
      ],
      "keywords": [
        "passive motion",
        "therapist movement",
        "flexibility test"
      ],
      "tags": [
        "joint_assist",
        "mobility_passive",
        "rehab_care"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "Rom Arom Prom 005",
      "img": "rom_measurement_tool.png",
      "source_reference": "FSMTB, Trail Guide to Movement, Salvo",
      "short_explanation": "ROM helps determine how flexible or limited a joint is.",
      "long_explanation": "Measuring ROM is foundational for evaluating posture, functional mobility, and treatment progress. It provides baseline and comparison data for care planning.",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "unit": "rom arom prom",
      "topic": "techniques",
      "question": "Which of the following is a key benefit of assessing ROM?",
      "clinical_application": "Therapists use ROM data to detect asymmetries, joint dysfunctions, or progress after therapeutic interventions.",
      "correct": 2,
      "category": "Techniques",
      "keywords": [
        "ROM benefit",
        "functional test",
        "flexibility"
      ],
      "tags": [
        "assessment",
        "joint_function",
        "movement_evaluation"
      ],
      "answers": [
        "It identifies reflex activity",
        "It diagnoses nerve damage",
        "It measures joint flexibility and mobility",
        "It strengthens core muscles"
      ],
      "question_rank": 5
    },
    {
      "id": "Rom Arom Prom 006",
      "tags": [
        "clinical_screening",
        "muscle_condition",
        "client_evaluation"
      ],
      "keywords": [
        "weakness",
        "neuromuscular",
        "pain restriction"
      ],
      "category": "Techniques",
      "question": "What does a limitation in AROM typically suggest?",
      "correct": 1,
      "unit": "rom arom prom",
      "question_rank": 6,
      "long_explanation": "If a client can't fully move a joint through AROM, it often indicates muscle strain, nerve inhibition, or pain guarding rather than joint damage.",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "clinical_application": "Identifying this helps differentiate whether the problem is muscular, neural, or joint-related--informing the massage strategy.",
      "topic": "techniques",
      "answers": [
        "Joint damage only",
        "Muscle weakness, pain, or coordination issues",
        "Skin conditions",
        "Heart rate fluctuation"
      ],
      "source_reference": "FSMTB, Trail Guide to Movement, Salvo",
      "short_explanation": "Limited AROM usually points to muscular or neural restrictions.",
      "img": "arom_limitation_chart.png"
    },
    {
      "id": "Rom Arom Prom 007",
      "correct": 2,
      "question": "What may a difference between PROM and AROM indicate?",
      "answers": [
        "Excessive energy levels",
        "A cognitive disorder",
        "Muscle-related issues or neuromuscular restrictions",
        "Spinal fracture"
      ],
      "source_reference": "FSMTB, Trail Guide to Movement, Salvo",
      "clinical_application": "Useful in detecting early-stage weakness, guarding, or neuromuscular disorders like MS or Parkinson's.",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "img": "arom_vs_prom_graph.png",
      "short_explanation": "When PROM exceeds AROM, it's often a muscle or nerve issue.",
      "topic": "techniques",
      "unit": "rom arom prom",
      "category": "Techniques",
      "question_rank": 7,
      "tags": [
        "neuromuscular",
        "comparison_screening",
        "rom_discrepancy"
      ],
      "keywords": [
        "AROM PROM difference",
        "motor control",
        "functional deficit"
      ],
      "long_explanation": "PROM being greater than AROM suggests the joint is capable of full range, but the client's muscles or nervous system can't engage it."
    },
    {
      "id": "Rom Arom Prom 008",
      "tags": [
        "rom_range",
        "motion_analysis"
      ],
      "keywords": [
        "PROM > AROM",
        "flexibility",
        "passive range"
      ],
      "category": "Techniques",
      "question": "Which is usually greater: AROM or PROM?",
      "correct": 1,
      "unit": "rom arom prom",
      "question_rank": 8,
      "long_explanation": "In healthy individuals, PROM exceeds AROM slightly due to the absence of muscular limitations or pain inhibition.",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "topic": "techniques",
      "clinical_application": "Important for distinguishing muscular limitations vs. joint or capsular restrictions in therapy.",
      "answers": [
        "AROM",
        "PROM",
        "They are always equal",
        "Neither is measurable"
      ],
      "source_reference": "FSMTB, Trail Guide to Movement, Salvo",
      "short_explanation": "PROM is generally greater because it doesn't rely on client effort.",
      "img": "range_comparison_chart.png"
    },
    {
      "id": "Rom Arom Prom 009",
      "clinical_application": "Applied in medical massage, hospice care, or post-injury evaluations when client movement is contraindicated.",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "question_rank": 9,
      "correct": 0,
      "answers": [
        "When a client is unconscious or in too much pain to move",
        "During strength training",
        "Only during meditation",
        "For stress relief"
      ],
      "category": "Techniques",
      "source_reference": "FSMTB, Trail Guide to Movement, Salvo",
      "tags": [
        "acute_pain",
        "passive_assessment",
        "limited_client_mobility"
      ],
      "unit": "rom arom prom",
      "question": "When would PROM assessment be most appropriate?",
      "short_explanation": "PROM is ideal when clients cannot move voluntarily.",
      "topic": "techniques",
      "img": "prom_care_case.png",
      "keywords": [
        "passive exam",
        "non-voluntary movement",
        "rehab"
      ],
      "long_explanation": "PROM allows for safe assessment of joint mobility when clients are immobilized, in acute pain, or unconscious."
    },
    {
      "id": "Rom Arom Prom 010",
      "unit": "rom arom prom",
      "topic": "techniques",
      "clinical_application": "Goniometers are used in assessments, insurance documentation, and rehab progress reports.",
      "long_explanation": "This tool helps therapists quantify ROM in degrees, allowing consistent tracking of mobility gains or losses.",
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "question": "What tool might a therapist use to measure ROM?",
      "question_rank": 10,
      "keywords": [
        "goniometer",
        "angle measurement",
        "mobility tracking"
      ],
      "category": "Techniques",
      "answers": [
        "Sphygmomanometer",
        "Reflex hammer",
        "Goniometer",
        "Stethoscope"
      ],
      "short_explanation": "A goniometer measures joint angles during movement.",
      "img": "goniometer_tool.png",
      "source_reference": "FSMTB, Trail Guide to Movement, Salvo",
      "correct": 2,
      "tags": [
        "measurement_tools",
        "goniometry",
        "clinical_data"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "Massage 001",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/techniques/beginning_a_massage_session_tips",
      "tags": [
        "hygiene",
        "client_care",
        "professionalism"
      ],
      "long_explanation": "Washing hands prevents germ transfer and demonstrates care. Greeting the client with warmth helps ease nerves and create a therapeutic connection.",
      "category": "Techniques",
      "correct": 2,
      "img": "greet_client_handwash.png",
      "source_reference": "FSMTB, Salvo’s Massage Therapy",
      "question": "What is an essential first step before beginning a massage session?",
      "clinical_application": "Builds rapport, sets a professional tone, and ensures hygiene—key in therapeutic relationships.",
      "answers": [
        "Start deep tissue immediately",
        "Begin without greeting",
        "Wash hands and greet the client",
        "Skip consultation"
      ],
      "topic": "techniques",
      "question_rank": 1,
      "short_explanation": "Hygiene and a friendly greeting establish professionalism and trust.",
      "keywords": [
        "greeting",
        "hand washing",
        "rapport",
        "hygiene"
      ],
      "difficulty": "easy"
    },
    {
      "id": "Massage 002",
      "question": "Why is it important to perform an intake interview before a massage?",
      "img": "intake_form_clipboard.png",
      "correct": 2,
      "tags": [
        "client_intake",
        "safety_screening",
        "customization"
      ],
      "long_explanation": "Gathering client history, goals, and preferences ensures safe, effective care. Skipping intake could lead to ineffective or harmful treatment.",
      "difficulty": "easy",
      "keywords": [
        "intake",
        "interview",
        "assessment",
        "client needs"
      ],
      "source_reference": "FSMTB, Trail Guide to the Body",
      "question_rank": 2,
      "category": "Techniques",
      "answers": [
        "To avoid small talk",
        "To fill time",
        "To understand client needs and conditions",
        "To sell more products"
      ],
      "clinical_application": "Essential for identifying medical conditions, pressure preferences, or areas to avoid.",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/techniques/beginning_a_massage_session_tips",
      "topic": "techniques",
      "short_explanation": "Intake helps customize treatment and avoid contraindications."
    },
    {
      "id": "Massage 003",
      "long_explanation": "Allergic reactions to oils or discomfort from pressure levels can ruin a session. Asking these up front prevents harm and builds trust.",
      "topic": "techniques",
      "tags": [
        "client_safety",
        "pressure_customization",
        "allergy_check"
      ],
      "img": "allergy_pressure_checklist.png",
      "answers": [
        "Favorite music",
        "Preferred hand",
        "Allergies and pressure preference",
        "Birthday"
      ],
      "question_rank": 3,
      "difficulty": "easy",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/techniques/beginning_a_massage_session_tips",
      "question": "What should be confirmed with the client before starting the session?",
      "short_explanation": "Safety and comfort depend on knowing allergies and preferred intensity.",
      "keywords": [
        "allergies",
        "pressure level",
        "client comfort"
      ],
      "source_reference": "Salvo, FSMTB",
      "category": "Techniques",
      "correct": 2,
      "clinical_application": "Vital for sensitive skin, previous injuries, or chronic conditions needing lighter work."
    },
    {
      "id": "Massage 004",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/techniques/beginning_a_massage_session_tips",
      "img": "calm_room_setup.png",
      "clinical_application": "Especially important for anxious clients, trauma-informed care, and stress recovery.",
      "question_rank": 4,
      "topic": "techniques",
      "question": "Which of the following helps create a relaxing environment at the start?",
      "category": "Techniques",
      "correct": 2,
      "short_explanation": "Calm sensory input helps trigger relaxation responses.",
      "answers": [
        "Bright overhead lights",
        "Loud music",
        "Soft lighting and calm music",
        "Strong perfume"
      ],
      "long_explanation": "Low lights and slow music help shift clients out of fight-or-flight mode and into rest-and-digest, optimizing therapeutic results.",
      "keywords": [
        "music",
        "lighting",
        "calm atmosphere"
      ],
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "tags": [
        "relaxation_environment",
        "sensory_preparation"
      ],
      "source_reference": "Massage Therapy Principles & Practice"
    },
    {
      "id": "Massage 005",
      "category": "Techniques",
      "keywords": [
        "modesty",
        "warmth",
        "safety",
        "ethics"
      ],
      "question": "What is the purpose of draping at the beginning of a session?",
      "clinical_application": "Crucial for trauma-informed care, ethics compliance, and client trust.",
      "short_explanation": "Draping preserves modesty and thermal comfort.",
      "source_reference": "FSMTB Ethics Section",
      "topic": "techniques",
      "correct": 2,
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/techniques/beginning_a_massage_session_tips",
      "answers": [
        "Decoration",
        "To cool the client",
        "To ensure client modesty and warmth",
        "To clean the table"
      ],
      "tags": [
        "draping_technique",
        "client_ethics",
        "comfort_modesty"
      ],
      "question_rank": 5,
      "img": "massage_drape_example.png",
      "long_explanation": "Draping establishes physical and psychological safety, allowing the client to relax fully without worry about exposure.",
      "difficulty": "easy"
    },
    {
      "id": "Massage 006",
      "long_explanation": "Knowing where to place the face, limbs, and head allows the client to get settled comfortably and safely, reducing anxiety.",
      "question": "Before leaving the room for the client to disrobe, what should the therapist explain?",
      "clinical_application": "Important for new clients, elderly clients, or those with mobility limitations.",
      "question_rank": 6,
      "category": "Techniques",
      "img": "client_table_position_diagram.png",
      "answers": [
        "Their resume",
        "Their favorite technique",
        "How to position themselves on the table",
        "The history of massage"
      ],
      "keywords": [
        "positioning",

    {
      "id": "Massage 007",
      "short_explanation": "Cold rooms make it harder for muscles to relax.",
      "source_reference": "Massage Therapy Fundamentals",
      "clinical_application": "Especially critical for elderly clients, those with circulation issues, or clients with anxiety.",
      "answers": [
        "To reduce equipment use",
        "To avoid using blankets",
        "To help the client stay relaxed",
        "To reduce costs"
      ],
      "long_explanation": "Shivering or tension caused by low room temperature interferes with relaxation and may increase discomfort during treatment.",
      "category": "Techniques",
      "topic": "techniques",
      "question": "Why should the therapist ensure the room is warm enough before the session?",
      "img": "warm_room_check.png",
      "tags": [

        "environment_control"

      "id": "Massage 008",
      "category": "Techniques",
      "short_explanation": "Reassurance builds safety and trust.",
      "img": "calm_talk_hand_gesture.png",
      "unit": "../../../src/data/questions/techniques/beginning_a_massage_session_tips",
      "topic": "techniques",
      "tags": [
        "client_reassurance",
        "trauma_informed_care"
      ],
      "clinical_application": "Essential for trauma survivors, first-time clients, or anyone exhibiting anxiety.",
      "question": "What should the therapist do if the client appears nervous?",
      "question_rank": 8,
      "keywords": [
        "nervous client",
        "anxiety",
        "communication"
      ],
      "correct": 2,
      "source_reference": "Trauma-Informed Massage Practice",
      "long_explanation": "Clients who are nervous may carry tension that interferes with therapeutic goals. Gentle, informative communication can lower cortisol and foster rapport.",
      "difficulty": "moderate",
      "answers": [
        "Ignore it",
        "Leave immediately",
        "Reassure them and explain the process",
        "Start without asking"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "Massage 009",
      "question": "How can a therapist build rapport quickly before beginning the massage?",
      "clinical_application": "Great for return clients and new clients alike—enhances long-term retention.",
      "long_explanation": "Human warmth at the start of the session builds trust and openness. Clients who feel emotionally safe relax more fully and benefit more from treatment.",

      "difficulty": "easy",
      "short_explanation": "A genuine, friendly greeting fosters connection.",
      "tags": [

      ]
    },

      "difficulty": "moderate",

      "correct": 2,
      "difficulty": "intermediate",
      "category": "End Feel Types",
      "source_reference": "Trail Guide, Orthopedic Assessment Textbook",
      "short_explanation": "End feel describes joint resistance at the end of a passive motion.",
      "img": "endfeel_passive_range.png",
      "answers": [
        "A measure of muscle strength",
        "The last movement in a stretch",
        "The quality of resistance felt at the end of passive range of motion",
        "The final phase of a gait cycle"
      ],
      "topic": "assessment",
      "long_explanation": "It refers to the tactile sensation or resistance a therapist perceives when passively moving a joint to its limit, helping differentiate between normal and pathological conditions.",
      "question": "What is an 'end feel' in manual therapy assessment?",
      "question_rank": 1,

      "question": "Which of the following is a normal soft end feel?",

      "clinical_application": "Considered normal during full-body assessments of flexibility.",
      "correct": 2,
      "short_explanation": "Soft end feel results from tissue approximation.",
      "long_explanation": "A soft end feel is typical when muscle or adipose tissue compresses against itself, like in hip flexion when the thigh meets the abdomen.",

      ],

    {
      "id": "End Feel 004",
      "answers": [
        "Ankle dorsiflexion",

      ],
      "keywords": [
        "elbow extension",
